" 

 " no , Denner ; I be go down immediately . " 

 as Mrs. Transome descend the stone staircase in her old black velvet and point , her appearance justify Denner 's personal compliment . she have that high - bear , imperious air which would have mark she as an 27   object of hatred and revile by a revolutionary mob . her person be too typical of social distinction to be pass by with indifference by any one : it would have fit an empress in her own right , who have have to rule in spite of faction , to dare the violation of treaty and dread retributive invasion , to grasp after new territory , to be defiant in desperate circumstance , and to feel a woman 's hunger of the heart forever unsatisfie . yet Mrs. Transome 's care and occupation have not be at all of an imperial sort . for thirty year she have lead the monotonous , narrow life which use to be the lot of our poor gentry ; who never go to town , and be probably not on speak term with two out of the five family whose park lie within the distance of a drive . when she be young she have be think wonderfully clever and accomplish , and have be rather ambitious of intellectual superiority have secretly pick out for private reading the high part of dangerous french author and in company have be able to talk of Mr. Burke 's style , or of Chateaubriand 's eloquence have laugh at the Lyrical Ballads , and admire Mr. Southey 's Thalaba . she always think that the dangerous french writer be wicked and that her reading of they be a sin ; but many sinful thing be highly agreeable to she , and many thing which she do not doubt to be good and true be dull and meaningless . she find ridicule of biblical character very amusing , and she be interested in story of illicit passion ; but she believe all the while that truth and safety lie in due attendance on prayer and sermon , in the admirable doctrine and ritual of the Church of England , equally remote from Puritanism and Popery ; in fact , in such a view of this world and the next as would preserve the exist arrangement of english society quite unshaken , keep down the obtrusiveness of the vulgar and the discontent of the poor . the history of the Jews , she know , ought to be prefer to any profane history ; the Pagans , of course , be vicious , and their religion quite nonsensical , consider as religion but classical learning come from the Pagans ; the Greeks be famous for sculpture ; the Italians for painting ; the middle age be dark and papistical ; but now Christianity go hand in hand with civilization , and the providential government of the world , though a little confused and entangle in foreign country , in our favored land be clearly see to be carry forward on Tory and Church of England principle , sustain by the succession of the House of Brunswick , and by sound English divine . for Miss Lingon have have a 28   superior governess , who hold that a woman should be able to write a good letter , and to express herself with propriety on general subject . and it be astonish how effective this education appear in a handsome girl , who sit supremely well on horseback , sing and play a little , paint small figure in water - color , have a naughty sparkle in her eye when she make a daring quotation , and an air of serious dignity when she recite something from her store of correct opinion . but however such a stock of idea may be make to tell in elegant society , and during a few season in town , no amount of bloom and beauty can make they a perennial source of interest in thing not personal ; and the notion that what be true and , in general , good for mankind , be stupid and drug - like , be not a safe theoretic basis in circumstance of temptation and difficulty . Mrs. Transome have be in her bloom before this century begin , and in the long painful year since then , what she have once regard as her knowledge and accomplishment have become as valueless as old - fashioned stucco ornament , of which the substance be never worth anything , while the form be no long to the taste of any live mortal . cross , mortification , money - care , conscious blame - worthiness , have change the aspect of the world for she ; there be anxiety in the morning sunlight ; there be unkind triumph or disapprove pity in the glance of greet neighbor ; there be advance age , and a contracting prospect in the change season as they come and go . and what could then sweeten the day to a hungry , much - exact self like Mrs. Transome 's ? under protract ill every live creature will find something that make a comparative ease , and even when life seem weave of pain , will convert the fainter pang into a desire . Mrs. Transome , whose imperious will have avail little to ward off the great evil of her life , find the opiate for her discontent in the exertion of she will about small thing . she be not cruel , and could not enjoy thoroughly what she call the old woman 's pleasure of tormenting ; but she like every little sign of power her lot have leave she . she like that a tenant should stand bareheade below she as she sit on horseback . she like to insist that work do without her order should be undo from begin to end . she like to be courtesie and bow to by all the congregation as she walk up the little barn of a church . she like to change a laborer 's medicine fetch from the doctor , and substitute a prescription of her own . 29   if she have only be more haggard and less majestic , those who have glimpse of her outward life might have say she be a tyrannical , grip harridan , with a tongue like a razor . no one say exactly that ; but they never say anything like the full truth about she , or divine what be hide under that outward life a woman 's keen sensibility and dread , which lie screen behind all her petty habit and narrow notion , as some quivering thing with eye and throb heart may lie crouch behind wither rubbish . the sensibility and dread have palpitate all the fast in the prospect of her son 's return ; and now that she have see he , she say to herself , in her bitter way , " it be a lucky eel that escape skin . the good happiness I shall ever know , will be to escape the bad misery . " 

 CHAPTER II . 

 a jolly parson of the good old stock , 
 by birth a gentleman , yet homely too , 
 suit his phrase to Hodge and Margery 
 whom he once christen , and have marry since , 
 a little lax in doctrine and in life . 
 not think God be captious in such thing 
 as what a man might drink on holiday , 
 but hold true religion be to do 
 as you would be do by which could never mean 
 that he should preach three sermon in a week . 
 Harold Transome do not choose to spend the whole evening with his mother . it be his habit to compress a great deal of effective conversation into a short space of time , ask rapidly all the question he want to get answer , and dilute no subject with irrelevancy , paraphrase , or repetition . he volunteer no information about himself and his past life at Smyrna , but answer pleasantly enough , though briefly , whenever his mother ask for any detail . he be evidently ill - satisfied as to his palate , try red pepper to everything , then ask if there be any relishing sauce in the house , and when Hickes bring various home - fill bottle , try several , find they failure , and finally fall back from his plate in despair . yet he remain good - humor , say something to his father now and then for the sake of be kind , and look on with a pity shrug as he see he watch Hickes cut his food . Mrs. Transome think with some bitterness that Harold show more feeling for her feeble husband who have never care in the least about he , than for she , 30   who have give he more than the usual share of mother 's love . an hour after dinner , Harold , who have already be turn over the leaf of his mother 's account - book , say 

 " I shall just cross the park to the parsonage to see my uncle Lingon . " 

 " very well . he can answer more question for you . " 

 " yes , " say Harold , quite deaf to the innuendo , and accept the word as a simple statement of the fact . " I want to hear all about the game and the North Loamshire hunt . I be fond of sport ; we have a great deal of it at Smyrna , and it keep down my fat . " 

 the Reverend John Lingon become very talkative over his second bottle of port , which be open on his nephew 's arrival . he be not curious about the manner of Smyrna , or about Harold 's experience , but he unbosom himself very freely as to what he himself like and dislike , which of the farmer he suspect of kill the fox , what game he have bag that very morning , what spot he would recommend as a new cover , and the comparative flatness of all exist sport compare with cock - fighting , under which Old England have be prosperous and glorious , while , so far as he could see , it have gain little by the abolition of a practice which sharpen the faculty of man , gratify the instinct of the fowl , and carry out the design of heaven in its admirable device of spur . from these main topic , which make his point of departure and return , he ramble easily enough at any new suggestion or query ; so that when Harold get home at a late hour , he be conscious of have gather from amidst the pompous full - toned triviality of his uncle 's chat some impression , which be of practical importance . among the rector 's dislikes , it appear , be Mr. Matthew Jermyn . 

 " a fat - handed , glib - tongue fellow , with a scented cambric handkerchief ; one of your educate low - breed fellow ; a foundling who get his Latin for nothing at Christ 's Hospital ; one of your middle - class upstart who want to rank with gentleman , and think they will do it with kid glove and new furniture . " 

 but since Harold mean to stand for the county , Mr. Lingon be equally emphatic as to the necessity of his not quarrel with Jermyn till the election be over . Jermyn must be his agent ; Harold must wink hard till he find himself safely return ; and even then it might be well to let Jermyn drop gently and raise no scandal . he himself have no quarrel with the fellow : a clergyman should have no quarrel , and he make it a 31   point to be able to take wine with any man he meet at table . and as to the estate , and his sister 's go too much by Jermyn 's advice , he never meddle with business : it be not his duty as a clergyman . that , he consider , be the meaning of Melchisedec and the tithe , a subject , into which he have go to some depth thirty year ago , when he preach the Visitation sermon . 

 the discovery that Harold mean to stand on the liberal side nay , that he boldly declare himself a Radical be rather startling ; but to his uncle 's good - humor , beatify by the sipping of port - wine , nothing could seem highly objectionable , provide it do not disturb that operation . in the course of half an hour he have bring himself to see that anything really worthy to be call British Toryism have be entirely extinct since the Duke of Wellington and Sir Robert Peel have pass the Catholic Emancipation Bill ; that Whiggery , with its right of man stop short at ten - pound householder , and its policy of pacify a wild beast with a bite , be a ridiculous monstrosity ; that therefore , since an honest man could not call himself a Tory , which it be , in fact , as impossible to be now as to fight for the old Pretender , and could still less become that execrable monstrosity a Whig , there remain but one course open to he . " why , lad , if the world be turn into a swamp , I suppose we should leave off shoe and stocking , and walk about like crane " whence it follow plainly enough that , in these hopeless time , nothing be leave to man of sense and good family but to retard the national ruin by declare themselves radical , and take the inevitable process of change everything out of the hand of beggarly demagogue and purse - proud tradesman . it be true the rector be help to this chain of reasoning by Harold 's remark ; but he soon become quite ardent in assert the conclusion . 

 " if the mob can not   be turn back , a man of family must try and head the mob , and save a few home and hearth , and keep the country up on its last leg as long as he can . and you be a man of family , my lad dash it ! you be a Lingon , whatever else you may be , and I will stand by you . I have   no great interest ; I be a poor parson . I have   be force to give up hunting ; my pointer and a glass of good wine be the only decency become my station that I can allow myself . but I will give you my countenance I will stick to you as my nephew . there be no need for I to change side exactly . I be bear a Tory , and I shall never be a 32   bishop . but if anybody say you be in the wrong , I shall say , ' my nephew be in the right ; he have turn Radical to save his country . if William Pitt have be live now he would have do the same ; for what do he say when he be die ? not ' oh , save my party ! ' but ' oh , save my country , heaven ! ' that be what they din in our ear about Peel and the Duke ; and now I will turn it round upon they . they shall be hoist with their own petard . yes , yes , I will stand by you . " 

 Harold do not feel sure that his uncle would thoroughly retain this satisfactory thread of argument in the uninspired hour of the morning ; but the old gentleman be sure to take the fact easily in the end , and there be no fear of family coolness or quarrel on this side . Harold be glad of it . he be not to be turn aside from any course he have choose ; but he dislike all quarrel as an unpleasant expenditure of energy that could have no good practical result . he be at once active and luxurious ; fond of mastery , and good - natured enough to wish that every one about he should like his mastery ; not care greatly to know other people 's thought , and ready to despise they as blockhead if their thought differ from his , and yet solicitous that they should have no colorable reason for slight thought about he . the blockhead must be force to respect he . hence , in proportion as he foresee his equal in the neighborhood would be indignant with he for his political choice , he care keenly about make a good figure before they in every other way . his conduct as a landholder be to be judicious , his establishment be to be keep up generously , his imbecile father treat with careful regard , his family relation entirely without scandal . he know that affair have be unpleasant in his youth that there have be ugly lawsuit and that his scapegrace brother Durfey have help to lower still far the depressed condition of the family . all this must be retrieve , now that event have make Harold the head of the Transome name . 

 Jermyn must be use for the election , and after that if he must be get rid of , it would be well to shake he loose quietly ; his uncle be probably right on both these point . but Harold 's expectation that he should want to get rid of Jermyn be found on other reason than his scented handkerchief and his charity - school Latin . 

 if the lawyer have be presume on Mrs. Transome 's ignorance as a woman , and on the stupid rakishness of the original heir , the new 33   heir would prove to he that he have calculate rashly . otherwise , Harold have no prejudice against he . in his boyhood and youth he have see Jermyn frequent Transome Court , but have regard he with that total indifference with which youngster be apt to view those who neither deny they pleasure nor give they any . Jermyn use to smile at he , and speak to he affably ; but Harold , half proud , half shy , get away from such patronage as soon as possible ; he know Jermyn be a man of business ; his father , his uncle , and Sir Maximus Debarry do not regard he as a gentleman and their equal . he have know no evil of the man ; but he see now that if he be really a covetous upstart , there have be a temptation for he in the management of the Transome affair ; and it be clear that the estate be in a bad condition . 

 when Mr. Jermyn be usher into the breakfast - room the next morning , Harold find he surprisingly little alter by the fifteen year . he be gray , but still remarkably handsome ; fat , but tall enough to bear that trial to man 's dignity . there be as strong a suggestion of toilette about he as if he have be five - and - twenty instead of nearly sixty . he choose always to dress in black , and be especially addict to black satin waistcoat , which carry out the general sleekness of his appearance ; and this , together with his white , fat , but beautifully - shape hand , which he be in the habit of rub gently on his entrance into a room , give he very much the air of a lady 's physician . Harold remember with some amusement his uncle 's dislike of those conspicuous hand ; but as his own be soft and dimpled , and as he too be give to the innocent practice of rub those member , his suspicion be not yet deepen . 

 " I congratulate you , Mrs. Transome , " say Jermyn , with a soft and deferential smile , " all the more , " he add , turn toward Harold , " now I have the pleasure of actually see your son . I be glad to perceive that an eastern climate have not be unfavorable to he . " 

 " no , " say Harold , shake Jermyn 's hand carelessly , and speak with more than his usual brusqueness , " the question be , whether the english climate will agree with I . it be deuce shift and damp ; and as for the food , it would be the fine thing in the world for this country if the southern cook would change their religion , get persecute , and fly to England , as the old silk - weaver do . " 

 " there be plenty of foreign cook for those who be rich enough to pay for they , I suppose , " say Mrs. Transome , " but they be unpleasant 34   people to have about one 's house . " 

 " Gad ! I do not   think so , " say Harold . 

 " the old servant be sure to quarrel with they . " 

 " that be no concern of mine . the old servant will have to put up with my man Dominic , who will show they how to cook and do everything else in a way that will rather astonish they . " 

 " old people be not so easily teach to change all their way , Harold . " 

 " well , they can give up and watch the young one , " say Harold , think only at that moment of old Mrs. Hickes and Dominic . but his mother be not think of they only . 

 " you have a valuable servant , it seem , " say Jermyn , who understand Mrs. Transome well than her son do , and wish to smoothen the current of their dialogue . 

 " oh , one of those wonderful southern fellow that make one 's life easy . he be of no country in particular . I do not   know whether he be most of a Jew , a Greek , an italian , or a spaniard . he speak five or six language , one as well as another . he be cook , valet , major - domo , and secretary all in one ; and what be more , he be an affectionate fellow I can trust to his attachment . that be a sort of human speciman that do not   grow here in England , I fancy . I should have be badly off if I could not have bring Dominic . " 

 they sit down to breakfast with such slight talk as this go on . each of the party be preoccupy and uneasy . Harold 's mind be busy construct probability about what he should discover of Jermyn 's mismanagement or dubious application of fund , and the sort of self - command he must in the bad case exercise in order to use the man as long as he want he . Jermyn be closely observe Harold with an unpleasant sense that there be an expression of acuteness and determination about he which would make he formidable . he would certainly have prefer at that moment that there have be no second heir of the Transome name to come back upon he from the East . Mrs. Transome be not observe the two man ; rather , her hand be cold , and her whole person shake by their presence ; she seem to hear and see what they say and do with preternatural acuteness , and yet she be also see and hear what have be say and do many year before , and feel a dim terror about the future . there be piteous sensibility in this faded woman , who thirty - four year ago , in the 35   splendor of her bloom , have be imperious to one of these man , and have rapturously press the other as an infant to her bosom , and now know that she be of little consequence to either of they . 

 " well , what be the prospect about election ? " say Harold , as the breakfast be advance . " there be two whig and one conservative likely to be in the field , I know . what be your opinion of the chance ? " 

 Mr. Jermyn have a copious supply of word which often lead he into periphrase , but he cultivate a hesitating stammer , which , with a handsome impassiveness of face , except when he be smile at a woman , or when the latent savageness of his nature be thoroughly rouse , he have find useful in many relation , especially in business . no one could have find out that he be not at his ease . " my opinion , " he reply , " be in a state of balance at present . this division of the county , you be aware , contain one manufacturing town of the first magnitude , and several small one . the manufacturing interest be widely disperse . so far a there be a presumption a in favor of the two liberal candidate . still , with a careful canvass of the agricultural district , such as those we have round we at Treby Magna , I think a the augury a would not be unfavorable to the return of a Conservative . a fourth candidate of good position , who should coalesce with Mr. Debarry a " 

 here Mr. Jermyn hesitate for the third time , and Harold break in . 

 " that will not be my line of action , so we need not discuss it . if I put up it will be as a Radical ; and I fancy , in any county that would return Whigs there would be plenty of voter to be comb off by a Radical who offer himself with good pretension . " 

 there be the slight possible quiver discernible across Jermyn 's face . otherwise he sit as he have do before , with his eye fix abstractedly on the frill of a ham before he , and his hand trifle with his fork . he do not answer immediately , but , when he do , he look round steadily at Harold . 

 " I be delighted to perceive that you have keep yourself so thoroughly acquaint with english politic . " 

 " oh , of course , " say Harold , impatiently . " I be aware how thing have be go on in England . I always mean to come back ultimately . I suppose I know the state of Europe as well as if I have be stationary at Little Treby for the last fifteen year . if a man go to the East , 36   people seem to think he get turn into something like the one - eyed calender in the ' Arabian Nights ! ' " 

 " yet I should think there be some thing which people who have be stationary at Little Treby could tell you , Harold , " say Mrs. Transome . " it do not signify about your hold radical opinion at Smyrna ; but you seem not to imagine how your put up as a Radical will affect your position here , and the position of your family . no one will visit you . and then the sort of people who will support you ! you really have no idea what an impression it convey when you say you be a Radical . there be none of our equal who will not feel that you have disgrace yourself . " 

 " Pooh ! " say Harold , rise and walk along the room . 

 but Mrs. Transome go on with grow anger in her voice " it seem to I that a man owe something to his birth and station , and have no right to take up this notion or other , just as it suit his fancy ; still less to work at the overthrow of his class . that be what every one say of Lord Grey , and my family at least be as good as Lord Grey 's . you have wealth now , and might distinguish yourself in the county ; and if you have be true to your color as a gentleman , you would have have all the great opportunity , because the time be so bad . the Debarrys and Lord Wyvern would have set all the more store by you . for my part , I can not   conceive what good you propose to yourself . I only entreat you to think again before you take any decide step . " 

 " Mother , " say Harold , not angrily or with any raising of his voice , but in a quick , impatient manner , as if the scene must be get through as quickly as possible ; " it be natural that you should think in this way . woman , very properly , do not   change their view , but keep to the notion in which they have be bring up . it do not   signify what they think they be not call upon to judge or to act . you must leave I to take my own course in these matter , which properly belong to man . beyond that , I will gratify any wish you may choose to mention . you shall have a new carriage and a pair of bay all to yourself ; you shall have the house do up in first - rate style , and I be not think of marry . but let we understand that there shall be no further collision between we on subject on which I must be master of my own action . " 

 " and you will put the crown to the mortification of my life , Harold . I do not   know who would be a mother if she could foresee what a slight 37   thing she will be to her son when she be old . " 

 Mrs. Transome here walk out of the room by the near way the glass door open toward the terrace . Mr. Jermyn have rise too , and his hand be on the back of his chair . he look quite impassive : it be not the first time he have see Mrs. Transome angry ; but now , for the first time , he think the outburst of her temper would be useful to he . she , poor woman , know quite well that she have be unwise , and that she have be make herself disagreeable to Harold to no purpose . but half the sorrow of woman would be avert if they could repress the speech they know to be useless nay , the speech they have resolve not to utter . Harold continue his walk a moment long , and then say to Jermyn 

 " you smoke ? " 

 " no , I always defer to the lady . Mrs. Jermyn be peculiarly sensitive in such matter , and do not   like tobacco . " 

 Harold , who , underneath all the tendency which have make he a Liberal , have intense personal pride , think , " confound the fellow with his Mrs. Jermyn ! do he think we be on a footing for I to know anything about his wife ? " 

 " well , I take my hookah before breakfast , " he say aloud , ' so , if you like , we will go into the library . my father never get up till midday , I find . " 

 " sit down , sit down , " say Harold , as they enter the handsome , spacious library . but he himself continue to stand before a map of the county which he have open from a series of roller occupy a compartment among the bookshelf . " the first question , Mr. Jermyn , now you know my intention , be , whether you will undertake to be my agent in this election , and help I through ? there be no time to be lose , and I do not   want to lose my chance , as I may not have another for seven year . I understand , " he go on , flash a look straight at Jermyn , " that you have not take any conspicuous course in politic , and I know that Labron be agent for the Debarrys . " 

 " oh a my dear sir a man necessarily have his political conviction , but of what use be it for a professional man a of some education , to talk of they in a little country town ? there really be no comprehension of public question in such place . party feeling , indeed , be quite asleep here before the agitation about the Catholic Relief Bill . it be true that I concur with our incumbent in get up a petition against the Reform Bill , 38   but I do not state my reason . the weak point in that Bill be a too palpable , and I fancy you and I should not differ much on that head . the fact be , when I know that you be to come back to we , I keep myself in reserve , though I be much press by the friend of Sir James Clement , the ministerial candidate , who be " 

 " however , you will act for I that be settle ? " say Harold . 

 " certainly , " say Jermyn , inwardly irritate by Harold 's rapid manner of cut he short . 

 " which of the liberal candidate , as they call themselves , have the well chance , eh ? " 

 " I be go to observe that Sir James Clement have not so good a chance as Mr. Garstin , suppose that a third liberal candidate present himself . there be two sens in which a politician can be liberal " here Mr. Jermyn smile " Sir James Clement be a poor baronet , hop for an appointment , and can not   be expect to be liberal in that wide sense which command majority . " 

 " I wish this man be not so much of a talker , " think Harold , " he will bore I . we shall see , " he say aloud , " what can be do in the way of combination . I will come down to your office after one o'clock if it will suit you ? " 

 " perfectly . " 

 " ah , and you will have all the list and paper and necessary information ready for I there . I must get up a dinner for the tenant , and we can invite whom we like besides the tenant . just now , I be go over one of the farm on hand with the bailiff . by the way , that be a desperately bad business , have three farm unlet how come that about , eh ? " 

 " that be precisely what I want to say a few word about to you . you have observe already how strongly Mrs. Transome take certain thing to heart . you can imagine that she have be severely try in many way . Mr. Transome 's want of health ; Mr. Durfey 's habit a " 

 " yes , yes . " 

 she be a woman for whom I naturally entertain the high respect , and she have have hardly any gratification for many year , except the sense of have affair to a certain extent in her own hand . she object to change ; she will not have a new style of tenant ; she like the old stock of farmer who milk their own cow , and send their young daughter out to service : all this make it difficult to do the good 39   with the estate . I be aware thing be not as they ought to be , for , in point of fact , improve agricultural management be a matter in which I take considerable interest , and the farm which I myself hold on the estate you will see , I think , to be in a superior condition . but Mrs. Transome be a woman of strong feeling , and I would urge you , my dear sir , to make the change which you have , but which I have not the right to insist on , as little painful to she as possible . " 

 " I shall know what to do , sir , never fear , " say Harold , much offended . 

 " you will pardon , I hope , a perhaps undue freedom of suggestion from a man of my age , who have be so long in a close connection with the family affair a I have never consider that connection simply in a light of business a " 

 " Damn he , I will soon let he know that I do , " think Harold . but in proportion as he find Jermyn 's manner annoying , he feel the necessity of control himself . he despise all person who defeat their own project by the indulgence of momentary impulse . 

 " I understand , I understand , " he say aloud . " you have   have more awkward business on your hand than usually fall to the share of a family lawyer . we shall set everything right by degree . but now as to the canvassing . I have   make arrangement with a first - rate man in London , who understand these matter thoroughly a solicitor , of course he have carry no end of man into Parliament . I will engage he to meet we at Duffield say when ? " 

 the conversation after this be drive carefully clear of all angle , and end with determined amicableness . when Harold , in his ride an hour or two afterward , encounter his uncle shoulder a gun , and follow by one black and one liver - spot pointer , his muscular person with its red eagle face set off by a velveteen jacket and leather legging , Mr. Lingon 's first question be 

 " well , lad , how have you get on with Jermyn ? " 

 " oh , I do not   think I shall like the fellow . he be a sort of amateur gentleman . but I must make use of he . I expect whatever I get out of he will only be something short of fair pay for what he have get out of we . but I shall see . " 

 " ay , ay , use his gun to bring down your game , and after that , beat the thief with the butt end . that be wisdom and justice and pleasure all in one talk between ourselves as uncle and nephew . but I say , Harold , I be go to tell you , now I come to think of it , this be 40   rather a nasty business , your call yourself a Radical . I have   be turn it over in after - dinner speech , but it look awkward it be not what people be use to it want a good deal of Latin to make it go down . I shall be worried about it at the session , and I can think of nothing neat enough to carry about in my pocket by way of answer . " 

 " nonsense , uncle ! I remember what a good speechifier you always be ; you will never be at a loss . you only want a few more evening to think of it . " 

 " but you will not be attack the Church and the institution of the country you will not be go those length ; you will keep up the bulwark , and so on , eh ? " 

 " no , I shall not   attack the Church , only the income of the bishop , perhaps , to make they eke out the income of the poor clergy . " 

 " well , well , I have no objection to that . nobody like our bishop : he be all greek and greediness ; too proud to dine with his own father . you may pepper the bishop a little . but you will respect the constitution hand down , etc . and you will rally round the throne and the king , God bless he , and the usual toast , eh ? " 

 " of course , of course . I be a Radical only in root out abuse . " 

 " that be the word I want , my lad ! " say the vicar , slap Harold 's knee . " that be a spool to wind a speech on . abuse be the very word ; and if anybody show himself offend , he will put the cap on for himself . " 

 " I remove the rotten timber , " say Harold , inwardly amuse , " and substitute fresh oak , that be all . " 

 " well do , my boy ! by George , you will be a speaker ! but I say , Harold , I hope you have   get a little Latin left . this young Debarry be a tremendous fellow at the classic , and walk on stilt to any length . he be one of the new conservative . Old Sir Maximus do not   understand he at all . " 

 " that will not   do at the husting , " say Harold . " he will get knock off his stilt pretty quickly there . " 

 " bless I ! it be astonishing how well you be up in the affair of the country , my boy . but rub up a few quotation ' Quod turpe boni decebat Crispinum ' and that sort of thing just to show Debarry what you could do if you like . but you want to ride on ? " 

 " yes ; I have an appointment at Treby . good - bye . " 

 " he be a cleverish chap , " mutter the vicar , as Harold ride away . " when he be have plenty of english exercise , and bring out his knuckle a 41   bit , he will be a Lingon again as he use to be . I must go and see how Arabella take his be a Radical . it be a little awkward ; but a clergyman must keep peace in a family . confound it ! I be not bind to love toryism well than my own flesh and blood , and the manor I shoot over . that be a heathenish , Brutus - like sort of thing , as if Providence could not   take care of the country without my quarrelling with my own sister 's son ! " 

 CHAPTER III . 

 ' Twas town , yet country too : you feel the warmth 
 of cluster house in the wintry time : 
 sup with a friend , and go by lantern home . 
 yet from your chamber window you could hear 
 the tiny bleat of new - yeane lamb , or see 
 the child bend beside the hedgerow bank 
 to pluck the primrose . 
 Treby Magna , on which the Reform Bill have thrust the new honor of be a polling - place , have be , at the beginning of the century , quite a typical old market - town , lie in pleasant sleepiness among green pasture , with a rush - fringe river meander through they . its principal street have various handsome and tall - windowed brick house with wall garden behind they ; and at the end , where it widen into the market - place , there be the cheerful rough - stuccoed front of that excellent inn , the Marquis of Granby , where the farmer put up their gig , not only on fair and market day , but on exceptional sunday when they come to church . and the church be one of those fine old english structure worth travel to see , stand in a broad churchyard with a line of solemn yew - tree beside it , and lift a majestic tower and spire far above the red - and - purple roof of the town . it be not large enough to hold all the parishioner of a parish which stretch over distant village and hamlet ; but then they be never so unreasonable as to wish to be all in at once , and have never complain that the space of a large side - chapel be take up by the tomb of the Debarrys , and shut in by a handsome iron screen . for when the black Benedictines cease to pray and chant in this church , when the Blessed Virgin and St. Gregory be expel , the Debarrys , as lord of the manor , naturally come next to Providence and take the place of the saint . long before that time , indeed , there have be a Sir Maximus Debarry who have be at the fortifying of the old castle , which now stand in ruin in the 42   midst of the green pasture , and with its shelter wall toward the north make an excellent strawyard for the pig of Wace    Co. , brewer of the celebrate Treby beer . Wace    Co. do not stand alone in the town as prosperous trader on a large scale , to say nothing of those who have retire from business ; and in no country town of the same small size as Treby be there a large proportion of family who have handsome set of china without handle , hereditary punch - bowl , and large silver ladle with a Queen Anne 's guinea in the centre . such people naturally take tea and sup together frequently ; and as there be no professional man or tradesman in Treby who be not connect by business , if not by blood , with the farmer of the district , the rich sort of these be much invite , and give invitation in their turn . they play at whist , eat and drink generously , praise Mr. Pitt and the war as keep up price and religion , and be very humorous about each other 's property , have much the same coy pleasure in allusion to their secret ability to purchase , as blushing lass sometimes have in joke about their secret preference . the rector be always of the Debarry family , associate only with county people , and be much respected for his affability ; a clergyman who would have take tea with the townspeople would have give a dangerous shock to the mind of a Treby churchman . 

 such be the old - fashion , grazing , brewing , wool - packing , cheese - loading life of Treby Magna , until there befell new condition , complicate its relation with the rest of the world , and gradually awakening in it that high consciousness which be know to bring high pain . first come the canal ; next , the working of the coal - mine at Sproxton , two mile off the town ; and thirdly , the discovery of a saline spring , which suggest to a too constructive brain the possibility of turn Treby Magna into a fashionable watering - place . so dare an idea be not originate by a native Trebian , but by a young lawyer who come from a distance , know the dictionary by heart , and be probably an illegitimate son of somebody or other . the idea , although it promise an increase of wealth to the town , be not well receive at first ; lady object to see " object " draw about in hand - carriage , the doctor foresee the advent of unsound practitioner , and most retail tradesman concur with he that new doing be usually for the advantage of new people . the more unanswerable reasoner urge that Treby have prosper without bath , and it be yet to be see how it would prosper with 43   they ; while a report that the propose name for they be Bethesda Spa , threaten to give the whole affair a blasphemous aspect . even Sir Maximus Debarry , who be to have an unprecedented return for the thousand he would lay out on a pump - room and hotel , regard the thing as a little too new , and hold back for some time . but the persuasive power of the young lawyer , Mr. Matthew Jermyn , together with the opportune opening of a stone - quarry , triumph at last ; the handsome building be erect , an excellent guide - book and descriptive card , surmount by vignette , be print , and Treby Magna become conscious of certain fact in its own history of which it have previously be in content ignorance . 

 but it be all in vain . the Spa , for some mysterious reason , do not succeed . some attribute the failure to the coal - mine and the canal ; other to the peace , which have have ruinous effect on the country ; and other , who dislike Jermyn , to the original folly of the plan . among these last be Sir Maximus himself , who never forgave the too persuasive attorney : it be Jermyn 's fault not only that a useless hotel have be build , but that he , Sir Maximus , be straiten for money , have at last let the building , with the adjacent land lie on the river , on a long lease , on the supposition that it be to be turn into a tape manufactory a bitter thing to any gentleman , and especially to the representative of one of the old family in England . 

 in this way it happen that Treby Magna gradually pass from be simply a respectable market town the heart of a great rural district , where the trade be only such as have close relation with the local land interest and take on the more complex life bring by mine and manufacture , which belong more directly to the great circulate system of the nation than to the local system to which they have be superadde ; and in this way it be that Trebian Dissent gradually alter its character . formerly it have be of a quiescent , well - to - do kind , represent architecturally by a small , venerable , dark - pewe chapel , build by Presbyterians , but long occupy by a sparse congregation of independent , who be as little move by doctrinal zeal as their church - go neighbor , and do not feel themselves deficient in religious liberty , inasmuch as they be not hinder from occasionally slumber in their pew , and be not oblige to go regularly to the weekly prayer - meeting . but when stone - pit and 44   coal - pit make new hamlet that threaten to spread up to the very town , when the tape - weaver come with their news - read inspector and book - keeper , the independent chapel begin to be fill with eager man and woman , to whom the exceptional possession of religious truth be the condition which reconcile they to a meagre existence , and make they feel in secure alliance with the unseen but supreme rule of a world in which their own visible part be small . there be dissenter in Treby now who could not be regard by the Church people in the light of old neighbor to whom the habit of go to chapel be an innocent , unenviable inheritance along with a particular house and garden , a tan - yard , or a grocery business Dissenters who , in their turn , without meaning to be in the least abusive , speak of the high - breed rector as a blind leader of the blind . and Dissent be not the only thing that the time have alter ; price have fall , poor - rate have rise , rent and tithe be not elastic enough , and the farmer 's fat sorrow have become lean ; he begin to speculate on cause , and to trace thing back to that causeless mystery , the cessation of one - pound note . thus , when political agitation sweep in a current through the country , Treby Magna be prepared to vibrate . the Catholic Emancipation Bill open the eye of neighbor and make they aware how very injurious they be to each other and to the welfare of mankind generally . Mr. Tiliot , the Church spirit - merchant , know now that Mr. Nuttwood , the oblige grocer , be one of those dissenter , deist , socinian , Papists , and Radicals , who be in league to destroy the Constitution . a retire old London tradesman , who be believe to understand politic , say that think people must wish George III be alive again in all his early vigor of mind : and even the farmer become less materialistic in their view of cause , and refer much to the agency of the devil and the Irish Romans . the rector , the Reverend Augustus Debarry , really a fine speciman of the old - fashioned aristocratic clergyman , preach short sermon , understand business , and act liberally about his tithe , have never before find himself in collision with dissenter ; but now he begin to feel that these people be a nuisance in the parish , that his brother Sir Maximus must take care lest they should get land to build more chapel , and that it might not have be a bad thing if the law have furnish he as a magistrate with a power of put a stop to the political sermon of the Independent preacher , which , in their way , 45   be as pernicious source of intoxication as the beerhouse . the Dissenters , on their side , be not dispose to sacrifice the cause of truth and freedom to a temporize mildness of language ; but they defend themselves from the charge of religious indifference , and solemnly disclaim any lax expectation that Catholics be likely to be save urge , on the contrary , that they be not too hopeful about Protestants who adhere to a bloated and worldly Prelacy . thus Treby Magna , which have live quietly through the great earthquake of the French Revolution and the napoleonic war , which have remain unmove by the " Rights of Man , " and see little in Mr. Cobbett 's " Weekly Register " except that he hold eccentric view about potato , begin at last to know the high pain of a dim political consciousness ; and the development have be greatly help by the recent agitation about the Reform Bill . Tory , Whig , and Radical do not perhaps become clear in their definition of each other ; but the name seem to acquire so strong a stamp of honor or infamy , that definition would only have weaken the impression . as to the short and easy method of judge opinion by the personal character of those who hold they , it be liable to be much frustrated in Treby . it so happen in that particular town that the Reformers be not all of they large - hearted patriot or ardent lover of justice ; indeed , one of they , in the very midst of the agitation , be detect in use unequal scale a fact to which many Tories point with disgust as show plainly enough , without further argument , that the cry for a change in the representative system be hollow trickery . again , the Tories be far from be all oppressor , dispose to grind down the working class into serfdom ; and it be undeniable that the inspector at the tape manufactory , who speak with much eloquence on the extension of the suffrage , be a more tyrannical personage than open - handed Mr. Wace , whose chief political tenet be that it be all nonsense to give man vote when they have no stake in the country . on the other hand there be some Tories who give themselves a great deal of leisure to abuse hypocrite , Radicals , dissenter , and atheism generally , but whose inflame face , theistic swearing , and frankness in express a wish to borrow , certainly do not mark they out strongly as hold opinion likely to save society . 

 the Reformers have triumph : it be clear that the wheel be go whither they be pull , and they be in fine spirit for 46   exertion . but if they be pull toward the country 's ruin , there be the more need for other to hang on behind and get the wheel to stick if possible . in Treby , as elsewhere , people be tell they must " rally " at the come election ; but there be now a large number of waverer man of flexible , practical mind , who be not such bigot as to cling to any view when a good tangible reason could be urge against they ; while some regard it as the most neighborly thing to hold a little with both side , and be not sure that they should rally or vote at all . it seem an invidious thing to vote for one gentleman rather than another . 

 these social change in Treby parish be comparatively public matter , and this history be chiefly concerned with the private lot of a few man and woman ; but there be no private life which have not be determine by a wide public life , from the time when the primeval milkmaid have to wander with the wandering of her clan , because the cow she milk be one of a herd which have make the pasture bare . even in that conservatory existence where the fair Camellia be sigh for by the noble young Pine - apple , neither of they need to care about the frost , or rain outside , there be a nether apparatus of hot water pipe liable to cool down on a strike of the gardener or a scarcity of coal . and the life we be about to look back upon do not belong to those conservatory specie ; they be root in the common earth , have to endure all the ordinary chance of past and present weather . as to the weather of 1832 , the Zadkiel of that time have predict that the electrical condition of the cloud in the political hemisphere would produce unusual perturbation in organic existence , and he would perhaps have see a fulfillment of his remarkable prophecy in that mutual influence of dissimilar destiny which we shall see gradually unfold itself . for if the mixed political condition of Treby Magna have not be act on by the passing of the Reform Bill , Mr. Harold Transome would not have present himself as a candidate for North Loamshire , Treby would not have be a polling - place , Mr. Matthew Jermyn would not have be on affable term with a dissent preacher and his flock , and the venerable town would not have be placard with handbill , more or less complimentary and retrospective condition in this case essential to the " where , " and the " what , " without which , as the learn know , there can be no event whatever . 

 for example , it be through these condition that a young man name Felix Holt make a considerable difference in the life of Harold 47   Transome , though nature and fortune seem to have do what they could to keep the lot of the two man quite aloof from each other . Felix be heir to nothing well than a quack medicine ; his mother live up a back street in Treby Magna , and her sitting - room be ornament with her good tea - tray and several frame testimonial to the virtue of Holt 's Cathartic Lozenges and Holt 's Restorative Elixir . there could hardly have be a lot less like Harold Transome 's than this of the quack doctor 's son , except in the superficial fact that he call himself a Radical , that he be the only son of his mother , and that he have lately return to his home with idea and resolve not a little disturbing to that mother 's mind . 

 but Mrs. Holt , unlike Mrs. Transome , be much disposed to reveal her trouble , and be not without a counsellor into whose ear she could pour they . on this second of September , when Mr. Harold Transome have have his first interview with Jermyn , and when the attorney go back to his office with new view of canvass in his mind , Mrs. Holt have put on her bonnet as early as nine o'clock in the morning , and have go to see the Reverend Rufus Lyon , minister of the Independent Chapel usually speak of as " Malthouse Yard .  CHAPTER IV . 

 " a pious and painful preacher . " Fuller . 

 Mr. Lyon live in a small house , not quite so good as the parish clerk 's , adjoin the entry which lead to the Chapel Yard . the new prosperity of Dissent at Treby have lead to an enlargement of the chapel , which absorb all extra fund and leave none for the enlargement of the minister 's income . he sit this morning , as usual , in a low up - stair room , call his study , which , by mean of a closet capable of hold his bed , serve also as a sleep - room . the bookshelf do not suffice for his store of old book , which lie about he in pile so arrange as to leave narrow lane between they ; for the minister be much give to walk about during his hour of meditation , and very narrow passage would serve for his small leg , unencumbered by any other drapery than his black silk stocking and the flexible , though prominent , bow of black ribbon that tie his knee - breech . he be walk about now , with his hand clasp behind he , an attitude in which his body seem to bear about the same proportion to his head as the low part of a stone Hermes bear to the carven image that crown it . his face look old 48   and wear , yet the curtain of hair that fall from his bald crown and hang about his neck retain much of its original auburn tint , and his large , brown , short - sighted eye be still clear and bright . at the first glance , every one think he a very odd - looking rusty old man ; the free - school boy often hoot after he , and call he " revelation " ; and to many respectable Church people , old Lyon 's little leg and large head seem to make Dissent additionally preposterous . but he be too short - sighted to notice those who titter at he too absent from the world of small fact and petty impulse in which titterer live . with Satan to argue against on matter of vital experience as well as of church government , with great text to meditate on , which seem to get deep as he try to fathom they , it have never occur to he to reflect what sort of image his small person make on the retina of a light - minded beholder . the good Rufus have his ire and his egoism ; but they exist only as the red heat which give force to his belief and his teaching . he be susceptible concern the true office of deacon in the primitive Church , and his small nervous body be jarred from head to foot by the concussion of an argument to which he see no answer . in fact , the only moment when he could be say to be really conscious of his body , be when he tremble under the pressure of some agitate thought . 

 he be meditate on the text for his Sunday morning sermon , " and all the people say , amen " a mere mustard - seed of a text , which have split at first only into two division , " what be say , " and " who say it " ; but these be grow into a many - branched discourse , and the preacher 's eye dilate , and a smile play about his mouth till , as his manner be , when he feel happily inspire , he have begin to utter his thought aloud in the varied measure and cadence habitual to he , change from a rapid but distinct undertone to a loud emphatic rallentando . 

 " my brother , do you think that great shout be raise in Israel by each man be wait to say ' amen ' till his neighbor have say aman ? do you think there will be a great shout for the right the shout of a nation as of one man , rounded and whole , like the voice of the archangel that bind together all the listener of earth and heaven if every Christian of you peep round to see what his neighbor in good coat be do , or else put his hat before his face that he may shout and never be hear ? but this be what you do : when the servant of God stand up 49   to deliver his message , do you lay your soul beneath the Word as you set out your plant beneath the fall rain ? no ; one of you send his eye to all corner , he smother his soul with small question , ' what do brother Y. think ? ' ' be this doctrine high enough for brother Z. ? ' ' will the church member be please ? ' and another " 

 here the door be open , and old Lyddy , the minister 's servant , put in her head to say , in a tone of despondency , finish with a groan , " here be Mrs. Holt want to speak to you ; she say she come out of season , but she be in trouble . " 

 " Lyddy , " say Mr. Lyon , fall at once into a quiet conversational tone , " if you be wrestle with the enemy , let I refer you to Ezekiel the thirteenth and twenty - second , and beg of you not to groan . it be a stumble - block and offence to my daughter ; she would take no broth yesterday , because she say you have cry into it . thus you cause the truth to be lightly speak of , and make the enemy rejoice . if your faceache give he an advantage , take a little warm ale with your meat I do not grudge the money . " 

 " if I think my drinking warm ale would hinder poor dear Miss Esther from speak light but she hat the smell of it . " 

 " answer not again , Lyddy , but send up Mistress Holt to I . " 

 Lyddy close the door immediately . 

 " I lack grace to deal with these weak sister , " say the minister , again think aloud , and walk . " their need lie too much out of the track of my meditation , and take I often unawares . mistress Holt be another who darken counsel by word without knowledge , and anger the reason of the natural man . Lord , give I patience . my sin be heavy to bear than this woman 's folly . come in , Mrs. Holt come in . " 

 he hasten to disencumber a chair of Matthew Henry 's Commentary , and beg his visitor to be seat . she be a tall elderly woman , dress in black , with a light - brown front and a black band over her forehead . she move the chair a little and seat herself in it with some emphasis , look fixedly at the opposite wall with a hurt and argumentative expression . Mr. Lyon have place himself in the chair against his desk , and wait with the resolute resignation of a patient who be about to undergo an operation . but his visitor do not speak . 

  50 

 " you have something on your mind , Mrs. Holt ? " he say , at last . 

 " indeed I have , sir , else I should not   be here . " 

 " speak freely . " 

 " it be well known to you , Mr. Lyon , that my husband , Mr. Holt , come from the north , and be a member in Malthouse Yard long before you begin to be pastor of it , which be seven year ago last Michaelmas . it be the truth , Mr. Lyon , and I be not that woman to sit here and say it if it be not   true . " 

 " certainly , it be true . " 

 " and if my husband have be alive when you would come to preach upon trial , he would have be as good a judge of your gift as Mr. Nuttwood or Mr. Muscat , though whether he would have agree with some that your doctrine be not   high enough , I can not   say . for myself , I have   my opinion about high doctrine . " 

 " be it my preach you come to speak about ? " say the minister , hurry in the question . 

 " no , Mr. Lyon , I be not that woman . but this I will say , for my husband die before your time , that he have a wonderful gift in prayer , as the old member well know , if anybody like to ask them , not believe my word , and he believe himself that the receipt for the Cancer Cure , which I have   send out in bottle till this very last April before September as now be , and have bottle stand by I he believe it be send to he in answer to prayer ; and nobody can deny it , for he pray most regular , and read out of the green baize Bible . " 

 Mrs. Holt pause , appear to think that Mr. Lyon have be successfully confute , and should show himself convince . 

 " have any one be asperse your husband 's character ? " say Mr. Lyon , with a slight initiative toward that relief of groan for which he have reprove Lyddy . 

 " Sir , they daredn't . for though he be a man of prayer , he do not   want skill and knowledge to find thing out for himself ; and that be what I use to say to my friend when they wonder at my marry a man from Lancashire , with no trade nor fortune , but what he would get in his head . but my husband 's tongue ' ud have be a fortune to anybody , and there be many a one say it be as good as a dose of physic to hear he talk ; not but what that get he into trouble in Lancashire , but he always say , if the bad come to the bad , he could go and preach to the black . but he do well than that , Mr. Lyon , for he marry I ; and this I will say , that for age , and conduct , and manage " 

  51 

 " Mistress Holt , " interrupt the minister , " these be not the thing whereby we may edify one another . let I beg of you to be as brief as you can . my time be not my own . " 

 " well , Mr. Lyon , I have   a right to my own character ; and I be one of your congregation , though I be not a church member , for I be bear in the General Baptist connection : and as for be save without work , there be a many , I dare say , can not   do without that doctrine ; but I thank the Lord I never need to put myself on a level with the thief on the cross . I have   do my duty , and more , if anybody come to that ; for I have   go without my bit of meat to make broth for a sick neighbor : and if there be any of the church member say they have   do the same , I would ask they if they have the sinking at the stomach as I have ; for I have   ever strove to do the right thing , and more , for good - natured I always be ; and I little think , after be respect by everybody , I should come to be reproach by my own son . and my husband say , when he be a - die ' Mary , ' he say , ' the Elixir , and the Pills , and the Cure will support you , for they have   a great name in all the country round , and you will pray for a blessing on they . ' and so I have   do , Mr. Lyon ; and to say they be not good medicine , when they have   be take for fifty mile round by high and low , rich and poor , and nobody speak against them but Dr. Lukin , it seem to I it be a flying in the face of Heaven ; for if it be wrong to take the medicine , could not   the blessed Lord have stop it ? " 

 Mrs. Holt be not give to tear ; she be much sustain by conscious unimpeachableness , and by an argumentative tendency which usually check the too great activity of the lachrymal gland ; nevertheless her eye have become moist , her finger play on her knee in an agitated manner , and she finally pluck a bit of her gown and hold it with great nicety between her thumb and finger . Mr. Lyon , however , by listen attentively , have begin partly to divine the source of her trouble . 

 " be I wrong in gather from what you say , Mistress Holt , that your son have object in some way to your sale of your late husband 's medicine ? " 

 " Mr. Lyon , he be masterful beyond everything , and he talk more than his father do . I have   get my reason , Mr. Lyon , and if anybody talk sense I can follow he ; but Felix talk so wild , and contradict his mother . and what do you think he say , after give up his ' prenticeship , and go off to study at Glasgow , and get through all the bit of money his 52   father save for his bringing - up what have all his learning come to ? he say I would well never open my Bible , for it be as bad poison to I as the pill be to half the people as swallow them . you will not speak of this again , Mr. Lyon I do not   think ill enough of you to believe that . for I suppose a Christian can understand the word o ' God without go to Glasgow , and there be text upon text about ointment and medicine , and there be one as might have be for a receipt of my husband 's it be just as if it be a riddle , and Holt 's Elixir be the answer . " 

 " your son use rash word , Mistress Holt , " say the minister , " but it be quite true that we may err in give a too private interpretation to the scripture . the word of God have to satisfy the large need of his people , like the rain and the sunshine which no man must think to be mean for his own patch of seed - ground solely . will it not be well that I should see your son , and talk with he on these matter ? he be at chapel , I observe , and I suppose I be to be his pastor . " 

 " that be what I want to ask you , Mr. Lyon . for perhaps he will listen to you , and not talk you down as he do his poor mother . for after we have be to chapel , he speak well of you than he do of most : he say you be a fine old fellow , and an old - fashioned Puritan he use dreadful language , Mr. Lyon ; but I see he do not   mean you ill , for all that . he call most folk 's religion rottenness ; and yet another time he will tell I I ought to feel myself a sinner , and do God 's will and not my own . but it be my belief he say first one thing and then another only to abuse his mother . or else he be go off his head , and must be send to a ' sylum . but if he write to the North Loamshire Herald first , to tell everybody the medicine be good for nothing , how can I ever keep he and myself ? " 

 " tell he I shall feel favor if he will come and see I this evening , " say Mr. Lyon , not without a little prejudice in favor of the young man , whose language about the preacher in Malthouse Yard do not seem to he to be altogether dreadful . " meanwhile , my friend , I counsel you to send up a supplication , which I shall not fail to offer also , that you may receive a spirit of humility and submission , so that you may not be hinder from see and follow the divine guidance in this matter by any false light of pride and obstinacy . of this more when I have speak with your son . " 

 " I be not proud or obstinate , Mr. Lyon . I never do say I be everything that be bad , and I never will . and why this trouble should be send on 53   I above everybody else for I have not   tell you all . he be make himself a journeyman to Mr. Prowd the watchmaker after all this learning and he say he will go with patch on his knee , and he shall like himself the well . and as for he have little boy to teach , they will come in all weather with dirty shoe . if it be madness , Mr. Lyon , it be no use your talk to he . " 

 " we shall see . perhaps it may even be the disguised working of grace within he . we must not judge rashly . many eminent servant of God have be lead by way as strange . " 

 " then I be sorry for their mother , that be all , Mr. Lyon ; and all the more if they have be well - speak - on woman . for not my big enemy , whether it be he or she , if they will speak the truth , can turn round and say I have   deserve this trouble . and when everybody get their due , and people 's doing be speak of on the house - top , as the Bible say they will be , it will be know what I have   go through with those medicine the pound and the pouring , and the let stand , and the weighing up early and down late there be nobody know yet but one that be worthy to know ; and the paste o ' the print label right side upwards . there be few woman would have go through with it ; and it be reasonable to think it will be make up to I ; for if there be promise and purchase blessing , I should think this trouble be purchase them . for if my son Felix do not   have a strait - waistcoat put on he , he will have his way . but I say no more . I wish you good - morning , Mr. Lyon , and thank you , though I well know it be your duty to act as you be do . and I never trouble you about my own soul , as some do who look down on I for not be a church member . " 

 " Farewell , Mistress Holt , farewell . I pray that a more powerful teacher than I be may instruct you . " 

 the door be close , and the much - try Rufus walk about again , say aloud , groaningly 

 " this woman have sit under the Gospel all her life , and she be as blind as a heathen , and as proud and stiff - neck as a Pharisee ; yet she be one of the soul I watch for . ' tis true that even Sara , the choose mother of God 's people , show a spirit of unbelief , and perhaps of selfish anger ; and it be a passage that bear the unmistakable signet , ' do honor to the wife or woman , as unto the weak vessel .
