 CHAPTER V. 

 1st Citizen . Sir , there be a hurry in the vein of youth 
 that make a vice of virtue by excess . 
 2d Citizen . what if the coolness of our tardier vein 
 be loss of virtue ? 
 1st Citizen . all thing cool with time 
 the sun itself , they say , till heat shall find 
 a general level , nowhere in excess . 
 2d Citizen . ' tis a poor climax , to my weak thought , 
 that future middlingness . 
 in the evening , when Mr. Lyon be expect the knock at the door that would announce Felix Holt , he occupy his cushionless arm - chair in the sitting - room , and be skim rapidly , in his short - sighted way , by the light of one candle , the page of a missionary report , emit occasionally a slight " Hm - m " that appear to be expressive of criticism rather than of approbation . the room be dismally furnish , the only object indicate an intention of ornament be a bookcase , a map of the Holy Land , an engraved portrait of Dr. Doddridge , and a black bust with a colored face , which for some reason or other be cover with green gauze . yet any one whose attention be quite awake must have be aware , even on enter , of certain thing that be incongruous with the general air of sombreness and privation . there be a delicate scent of dry rose - leaf ; the light by which the minister be read be a wax - candle in a white earthenware candle - stick , and the table on the opposite side of the fireplace hold a dainty work - basket frille with blue satin . 

 Felix Holt , when he enter , be not in an observant mood ; and when , after seat himself , at the minister 's invitation , near the little table which hold the work - basket , he star at the wax - candle opposite to he , he do so without any wonder or consciousness that the candle be not of tallow . but the minister 's sensitiveness give another interpretation to the gaze which he divine rather than see ; and in alarm lest this inconsistent extravagance should obstruct his usefulness , he hasten to say 

 " you be doubtless amazed to see I with a wax - light , my young friend ; but this undue luxury be pay for with the earning of my daughter , who be so delicately frame that the smell of tallow be loathsome to she . " 

 " I heed not the candle , sir . I thank Heaven I be not a mouse to have a nose that take note of wax or tallow . " 

 the loud abrupt tone make the old man vibrate a little . he have be stroke his chin gently before , with a sense that he must be very 55   quiet and deliberate in his treatment of the eccentric young man ; but now , quite unreflectingly , he draw forth a pair of spectacle , which he be in the habit of use when he want to observe his interlocutor more closely than usual . 

 " and I myself , in fact , be equally indifferent , " he say , as he open and adjust his glass , ' so that I have a sufficient light on my book . " here his large eye look discerningly through the spectacle . 

 " ' tis the quality of the page you care about , not of the candle , " say Felix , smile pleasantly enough at his inspector . " you be think that you have a roughly - write page before you now . " 

 that be true . the minister , accustom to the respectable air of provincial townsman , and especially to the sleek well - clip gravity of his own male congregation , feel a slight shock as his glass make perfectly clear to he the shaggy - head , large - eye , strong - limbed person of this questionable young man , without waistcoat or cravat . but the possibility , support by some of Mrs. Holt 's word , that a disguised work of grace might be go on in the son of whom she complain so bitterly , check any hasty interpretation . 

 " I abstain from judge by the outward appearance only , " he answer , with his usual simplicity . " I myself have experience that when the spirit be much exercise it be difficult to remember neck - band and string and such small accident of our vesture , which be nevertheless decent and needful so long as we sojourn in the flesh . and you , too , my young friend , as I gather from your mother 's troubled and confused report , be undergo some travail of mind . you will not , I trust , object to open yourself fully to I , as to an aged pastor who have himself have much inward wrestling , and have especially know much temptation from doubt . " 

 " as to doubt , " say Felix , loudly and brusquely as before , " if it be those absurd medicine and gull advertisement that my mother have be talk of to you and I suppose it be I have   no more doubt about they than I have about pocket - picking . I know there be a stage of speculation in which a man may doubt whether a pickpocket be blameworthy but I be not one of your subtle fellow who keep look at the world through their own leg . if I allow the sale of those medicine to go on , and my mother to live out of the proceed when I can keep she by the honest labor of my hand , I have   not the least doubt that I should be a rascal . " 

  56 

 " I would fain enquire more particularly into your objection to these medicine , " say Mr. Lyon , gravely . notwithstanding his conscientiousness and a certain originality in his own mental disposition , he be too little use to high principle quite dissociate from sectarian phraseology to be as immediately in sympathy with it as he would otherwise have be . " I know they have be well report of , and many wise person have try remedy providentially discover by those who be not regular physician , and have find a blessing in the use of they . I may mention the eminent Mr. Wesley , who , though I hold not altogether with his arminian doctrine , nor with the usage of his institution , be nevertheless a man of God ; and the journal of various Christians whose name have leave a sweet savor , might be cite in the same sense . moreover , your father , who originally concoct these medicine and leave they as a provision for your mother , be , as I understand , a man whose walk be not unfaithful . " 

 " my father be ignorant , " say Felix , bluntly . " he know neither the complication of the human system , nor the way in which drug counteract each other . ignorance be not so damnable as humbug , but when it prescribe pill it may happen to do more harm . I know something about these thing . I be ' prentice for five miserable year to a stupid brute of a country apothecary my poor father leave money for that he think nothing could be fine for I . no matter : I know that the Cathartic Pills be a drastic compound which may be as bad as poison to half the people who swallow they ; that the Elixir be an absurd farrago of a dozen incompatible thing ; and that the Cancer Cure might as well be bottled ditch - water . " 

 Mr. Lyon rise and walk up and down the room . his simplicity be strongly mix with sagacity as well as sectarian prejudice , and he do not rely at once on a loud - speak integrity Satan might have flavor it with ostentation . presently he ask , in a rapid , low tone , " how long have you know this , young man ? " 

 " well put , sir , " say Felix . " I have   know it a good deal long than I have act upon it , like plenty of other thing . but you believe in conversion ? " 

 " yea , verily . " 

 " so do I. I be convert by six week ' debauchery . " 

 the minister start . " young man , " he say , solemnly , go up close to Felix and lay a hand on his shoulder , ' speak not lightly of the 57   divine operation , and restrain unseemly word . " 

 " I be not speak lightly , " say Felix . " if I have not see that I be make a hog of myself very fast , and that pig - wash , even if I could have get plenty of it , be a poor sort of thing , I should never have look life fairly in the face to see what be to be do with it . I laugh out loud at last to think that a poor devil like I , in a Scotch garret , with my stocking out at heel and a shilling or two to be dissipate upon , with a smell of raw haggis mount from below , and old woman breathe gin as they pass I on the stair want to turn my life into easy pleasure . then I begin to see what else it could be turn into . not much , perhaps . this world be not a very fine place for a good many of the people in it . but I have   make up my mind it shall not   be the bad for I , if I can help it . they may tell I I can not   alter the world that there must be a certain number of sneak and robber in it , and if I do not   lie and filch somebody else will . well then , somebody else shall , for I will not . that be the upshot of my conversion , Mr. Lyon , if you want to know it . " 

 Mr. Lyon remove his hand from Felix 's shoulder and walk about again . " do you sit under any preacher at Glasgow , young man ? " 

 " no : I hear most of the preacher once , but I never want to hear they twice . " 

 the good Rufus be not without a slight rise of resentment at this young man 's want of reverence . it be not yet plain whether he want to hear twice the preacher in Malthouse Yard . but the resentful feeling be carefully repress : a soul in so peculiar a condition must be deal with delicately . 

 " and now , may I ask , " he say , " what course you mean to take , after hinder your mother from make and sell these drug ? I speak no more in their favor after what you have say . God forbid that I should strive to hinder you from seek whatsoever thing be honest and honorable . but your mother be advanced in year ; she need comfortable sustenance ; you have doubtless consider how you may make her amend ? ' he that provideth not for his own ' I trust you respect the authority that so speak . and I will not suppose that , after be tender of conscience toward stranger , you will be careless toward your mother . there be indeed some who , take a mighty charge on their 58   shoulder , must perforce leave their household to Providence , and to the care of humbler brother , but in such a case the call must be clear . " 

 " I shall keep my mother as well nay , well than she have keep herself . she have always be frugal . with my watch and clock cleaning , and teach one or two little chap that I have   get to come to I , I can earn enough . as for I , I can live on bran porridge . I have the stomach of a rhinoceros . " 

 " but for a young man so well furnish as you , who can questionless write a good hand and keep book , be it not well to seek some high situation as clerk or assistant ? I could speak to Brother Muscat , who be well acquainted with all such opening . any place in Pendrell 's Bank , I fear , be now close against such as be not Churchmen . it use not to be so , but a year ago he discharge Brother Bodkin , although he be a valuable servant . still , something might be find . there be rank and degree and those who can serve in the high must not unadvisedly change what seem to be a providential appointment . your poor mother be not altogether " 

 " excuse I , Mr. Lyon ; I have   have all that out with my mother , and I may as well save you any trouble by tell you that my mind have be make up about that a long while ago . I will take no employment that oblige I to prop up my chin with a high cravat , and wear strap , and pass the livelong day with a set of fellow who spend their spare money on shirt pin . that sort of work be really low than many handicraft ; it only happen to be pay out of proportion . that be why I set myself to learn the watchmaking trade . my father be a weaver first of all . it would have be well for he if he have remain a weaver . I come home through Lancashire and see an uncle of mine who be a weaver still . I mean to stick to the class I belong to people who do not   follow the fashion . " 

 Mr. Lyon be silent a few moment . this dialogue be far from plain sailing ; he be not certain of his latitude and longitude . if the despiser of Glasgow preacher have be argue in favor of gin and Sabbath - breaking , Mr. Lyon 's course would have be clear . " well , well , " he say , deliberately , " it be true that St. Paul exercise the trade of tent - making , though he be learn in all the wisdom of the Rabbis . " 

 " St. Paul be a wise man , " say Felix . " why should I want to get into the middle class because I have some learning ? the most of the middle class be as ignorant as the work people about everything that 59   do not   belong to their own Brummagem life . that be how the workingman be leave to foolish device and keep worsen themselves : the good head among they forsake their boon comrade , and go in for a house with a high door - step and a brass knocker . " 

 Mr. Lyon stroke his mouth and chin , perhaps because he feel some disposition to smile ; and it would not be well to smile too readily at what seem but a weedy resemblance of christian unworldliness . on the contrary , there might be a dangerous snare in an unsanctified outstepping of average christian practice . 

 " nevertheless , " he observe , gravely , " it be by such self - advancement that many have be enable to do good service to the cause of liberty and to the public well - being . the ring and the robe of Joseph be no object for a good man 's ambition , but they be the sign of that credit which he win by his divinely - inspire skill , and which enable he to act as a saviour to his brother . " 

 " oh , yes , your ringed and scented man of the people ! I will not   be one of they . let a man once throttle himself with a satin stock , and he will get new want and new motif . metamorphosis will have begin at his neck - joint , and it will go on till it have change his liking first and then his reasoning , which will follow his liking as the foot of a hungry dog follow his nose . I will have none of your clerkly gentility . I might end by collect greasy pence from poor man to buy myself a fine coat and a glutton 's dinner , on pretence of serve the poor man . I would soon be Paley 's fat pigeon than a demagogue all tongue and stomach , though " here Felix change his voice a little " I should like well enough to be another sort of demagogue , if I could . " 

 " then you have a strong interest in the great political movement of these time ? " say Mr. Lyon , with a perceptible flashing of the eye . 

 " I should think so . I despise every man who have not or , have it , do not   try to rouse it in other man . " 

 " right , my young friend , right , " say the minister , in a deep cordial tone . inevitably his mind be draw aside from the immediate consideration of Felix Holt 's spiritual interest by the prospect of political sympathy . in those day so many instrument of God 's cause in the fight for religious and political liberty hold creed that be painfully wrong , and , indeed , irreconcilable with salvation ! " that be my own view , which I maintain in the face of some opposition from 60   brother who contend that a share in public movement be a hindrance to the close walk , and that the pulpit be no place for teach man their duty as member of the commonwealth . I have have much puerile blame cast upon I because I have utter such name as Brougham and Wellington in the pulpit . why not Wellington as well as Rabshakeh ? and why not Brougham as well as Balaam ? do God know less of man than he do in the day of Hezekiah and Moses ? be his arm shorten , and be the world become too wide for his providence ? but , they say , there be no politic in the New Testament " 

 " well , they be right enough there , " say Felix , with his usual unceremoniousness . 

 " what ! you be of those who hold that a christian minister should not meddle with public matter in the pulpit ? " say Mr. Lyon , color . " I be ready to join issue on that point . " 

 " not I , sir , " say Felix ; " I should say , teach any truth you can , whether it be in the Testament or out of it . it be little enough anybody can get hold of , and still less what he can drive into the skull of a pence - counting , parcel - tie generation , such as mostly fill your chapel . " 

 " young man , " say Mr. Lyon , pause in front of Felix . he speak rapidly , as he always do , except when his word be specially weight with emotion : he overflow with matter , and in his mind matter be always completely organize into word . " I speak not on my own behalf , for not only have I no desire that any man should think of I above that which he seeth I to be , but I be aware of much that should make I patient under a disesteem rest even on too hasty a construction . I speak not as claim reverence for my own age and office not to shame you , but to warn you . it be good that you should use plainness of speech , and I be not of those who would enforce a submissive silence on the young , that they themselves , be elder , may be hear at large ; but Elihu be the young of Job 's friend , yet be there a wise rebuke in his word ; and the aged Eli be teach by a revelation to the boy Samuel . I have to keep a special watch over myself in this matter , inasmuch as I have need of utterance which make the thought within I seem as a pen - up fire , until I have shoot it forth , as it be , in arrowy word , each one hit its mark . therefore I pray for a listen spirit , which be a great mark of grace . nevertheless , my young friend , I be bind , as I 61   say , to warn you . the temptation that most beset those who have great natural gift , and be wise after the flesh , be pride and scorn , more particularly toward those weak thing of the world which have be choose to confound the thing which be mighty . the scornful nostril and the high head gather not the odor that lie on the track of truth . the mind that be too ready at contempt and reprobation be " 

 here the door open , and Mr. Lyon pause to look around , but see only Lyddy with the tea - tray , he go on 

 " be , I may say , as a clenched fist that can give blow , but be shut up from receive and hold aught that be precious though it be heaven - send manna . "

 Esther bow slightly as she walk across the room to fetch the candle and place it near her tray . Felix rise and bow , also with an air of indifference , which be perhaps exaggerate by the fact that he be inwardly surprised . the minister 's daughter be not the sort of person he expect . she be quite incongruous with his notion of minister ' daughter in general ; and though he have expect something nowise delightful , the incongruity repel he . a very delicate scent , the faint suggestion of a garden , be waft as she go . he would not observe she , but he have a sense of an elastic walk , the tread of small foot , a long neck and a high crown of shin brown plait and curl that float backward thing , in short , that suggest a fine lady to he , and determine he to notice she as little as possible . a fine lady be always a sort of spin - glass affair not natural , and with no beauty for he as art ; but a fine lady as the daughter of this rusty old Puritan be especially offensive . 

 " nevertheless , " continue Mr. Lyon , who rarely let drop any thread of discourse , " that phrenological science be not irreconcilable with the revealed dispensation . and it be undeniable that we have our vary native disposition which even grace will not obliterate . I myself , from my youth up , have be give to question too curiously concern the truth to examine and sift the medicine of the soul rather than to apply it . "

" but truth - vendor and medicine - vendor usually recommend swallowing . when a man see his livelihood in a pill or a proposition , he like to have order for the dose , and not curious enquiry . " 

 this speech verge on rudeness , but it be deliver with a brusque openness that imply the absence of any personal intention . the minister 's daughter be now for the first time startle into look at Felix . but her survey of this unusual speaker be soon make , and she relieve her father from the need to reply by say 

 " the tea be pour out , father . "

 " will you draw up to the table , Mr. Holt ? " say the minister . 

 in the act of rise , Felix push back his chair too suddenly against the rickety table close by he , and down go the blue - frille work - basket , fly open , and disperse on the floor reel , thimble , muslin - work , a small seal bottle of attar of rose , and something heavy than these a duodecimo volume which fall near he between the table and the fender .

 she redden , draw up her long neck , and say , as she retreat to her chair again

 Mr. Lyon have pause in the act of draw his chair to the tea table , and be look on at this scene , wrinkle the corner of his eye with a perplexed smile . Esther would not have wish he to know anything about the volume of Byron , but she be too proud to show any concern .

 " Ho , ho ! " think Felix , " her father be frightened at she . how come he to have such a nice - step , long - neck peacock for his daughter ? but she shall see that I be not frightened . " then he say aloud , " I should like to know how you will justify your admiration for such a writer , Miss Lyon . " 

 " I should not attempt it with you , Mr. Holt , " say Esther . " you have such strong word at command that they make the small argument seem formidable . if I have ever meet the giant Cormoran , I should have make a point of agree with he in his literary opinion . " 

 Esther have that excellent thing in woman , a soft voice with clear fluent utterance . her sauciness be always charming because it be without emphasis , and be accompany with graceful little turn of the head . 

 Felix laugh at her thrust with young heartiness . 

 " my daughter be a critic of word , Mr. Holt , " say the minister , smile complacently , " and often correct mine on the ground of nicety , which I profess be as dark to I as if they be the report of a sixth sense which I possess not . I be an eager seeker for precision , and would fain find language subtle enough to follow the utmost intricacy of the soul 's pathway , but I see not why a round word that mean some object , make and bless by the Creator , should be brand and banish as a malefactor . " 

 " oh , your nicety I know what they be , " say Felix , in his usual fortissimo . " they will go on your system of make - believe . ' Rottenness ' may suggest what be unpleasant , so you would well say ' sugar - plum , ' or something else such a long way off the fact that nobody be oblige to think of it . those be your roundabout euphuism that dress up swindling till it look as well as honesty , and shoot with boil pea instead of bullet . I hate your gentlemanly speaker . "

 " then you would not like Mr. Jermyn , I think , " say Esther . " that remind I , father , that to - day , when I be give Miss Louisa Jermyn her lesson , Mr. Jermyn come in and speak to I with grand politeness , and ask I at what time you be likely to be disengage , because he wish to make your well acquaintance , and consult you on matter of importance . he never take the least notice of I before . can you guess the reason of his sudden ceremoniousness ? " 

 " Nay , child , " say the minister , ponderingly . 

 " Politics , of course , " say Felix . " he be on some committee . an election be come . universal peace be declare , and the fox have a sincere interest in prolong the life of the poultry . eh , Mr. Lyon ? be not   that it ? "

" 

 " he be come , " say Esther . " I hear Miss Jermyn tell her sister that she have see he go out of her father 's room . " 

 " ' tis strange , " say Mr. Lyon .

I say , so they be , usually , and Church people also in small town . she consider herself a judge of what be ladylike , and she be vulgarity personify with large foot , and the most odious scent on her handkerchief , and a bonnet that look like ' the Fashion ' print in capital letter .  " no , indeed . pardon I , " say Esther . " a real fine - lady do not wear clothe that flare in people 's eye , or use importunate scent , or make a noise as she move : she be something refined and graceful , and charming , and never obtrusive . " 

 " oh , yes , " say Felix , contemptuously . " and she read Byron also , and admire Childe Harold gentleman of unspeakable woe , who employ a hairdresser , and look seriously at themselves in the glass . "

 Esther redden , and give a little toss . Felix go on triumphantly . " a fine - lady be a squirrel - head thing , with small air , and small notion , about as applicable to the business of life as a pair of tweezer to the clearing of a forest . ask your father what those old persecute emigrant Puritans would have do with fine - lady wife and daughter . " 

 " oh , there be no danger of such mťsalliance , " say Esther . " man who be unpleasant companion and make fright of themselves , be sure to get wife tasteless enough to suit they . " 

 " Esther , my dear , " say Mr. Lyon , " let not your playfulness betray you into disrespect toward those venerable pilgrim . they struggle and endure in order to cherish and plant anew the seed of a scriptural doctrine and of a pure discipline . " 

 " yes , I know , " say Esther , hastily , dread a discourse on the pilgrim father .

" Miss Medora would not   have mind if they have all be put into the pillory and lose their ear . she would have say , ' their ear do stick out so . ' I should not   wonder if that be a bust of one of they . " here Felix , with sudden keenness of observation , nod at the black bust with the gauze over its colored face .

" 

 " she have put a veil over it . suppose you have squint yourself ? " say Felix , look at Esther . 

 " then , doubtless , you could have be more polite to I , Mr. Holt , " say Esther , rise and place herself at her work - table . " you seem to prefer what be unusual and ugly . " 

 " a peacock ! " think Felix . " I should like to come and scold she every day , and make her cry and cut her fine hair off . " 

 Felix rise to go , and say , " I will not take up any more of your valuable time , Mr. Lyon . I know that you have not many spare evening . " 

  67 

 " that be true , my young friend ; for I now go to Sproxton one evening in the week . I do not despair that we may some day need a chapel there , though the hearer do not multiply save among the woman , and there be no work as yet begin among the miner themselves . I shall be glad of your company in my walk thither to - morrow at five o'clock , if you would like to see how that population have grow of late year . " 

 " oh , I have   be to Sproxton already several time . I have a congregation of my own there last Sunday evening . " 

 " what ! do you preach ? " say Mr. Lyon , with brightened glance . 

 " not exactly . I go to the ale - house . " 

 Mr. Lyon start . " I trust you be put a riddle to I , young man , even as Samson do to his companion . from what you say but lately , it can not be that you be give to tipple and to tavern . " 

 " oh , I do not   drink much . I order a pint of beer , and I get into talk with the fellow over their pot and pipe . somebody must take a little knowledge and common - sense to they in this way , else how be they to get it ? I go for educate the non - elector , so I put myself in the way of my pupil my academy be the beer - house . I will walk with you to - morrow with pleasure . " 

 " do so , do so , " say Mr. Lyon , shake hand with his odd acquaintance . " we shall understand each other well by - and - by , I doubt not . " 

 " I wish you good - evening , Miss Lyon . " 

 Esther bow very slightly , without speak . 

 " that be a singular young man , Esther , " say the minister , walk about after Felix be go . " I discern in he a love for whatsoever thing be honest and true , which I would fain believe to be an earnest of further endowment with the wisdom that be from on high . it be true that , as the traveller in the desert be often lure , by a false vision of water and freshness , to turn aside from the track which lead to the try and establish fountain , so the Evil One will take advantage of a natural yearning toward the well , to delude the soul with a self - flattering belief in a visionary virtue , high than the ordinary fruit of the Spirit . but I trust it be not so here . I feel a great enlargement in this young man 's presence , notwithstanding a certain license in his language , which I shall use my effort to correct . " 

 " I think he be very coarse and rude , " say Esther , with a touch of temper in her voice . " but he speak well English than most of 68   our visitor . what be his occupation ? " 

 " watch and clock making , by which , together with a little teaching , as I understand , he hop to maintain his mother , not think it right that he should live by the sale of medicine whose virtue he distrust . it be no common scruple . " 

 " dear I , " say Esther , " I think he be something high than that . " she be disappoint . 

 Felix , on his side , as he stroll out in the evening air , say to himself : " now by what fine mesh of circumstance do that queer devout old man , with his awful creed , which make this world a vestibule with double door to hell , and a narrow stair on one side whereby the thin sort may mount to heaven by what subtle play of flesh and spirit do he come to have a daughter so little in his own likeness ? marry foolishly , I suppose . I will never marry , though I should have to live on raw turnip to subdue my flesh . I will never look back and say , ' I have a fine purpose once I mean to keep my hand clean and my soul upright , and to look truth in the face ; but pray excuse I , I have a wife and child I must lie and simper a little , else they will starve ' ; or ' my wife be nice , she must have her bread well butter , and her feeling will be hurt if she be not think genteel . ' that be the lot Miss Esther be prepare for some man or other . I could grind my tooth at such self - satisfy minx , who think they can tell everybody what be the correct thing , and the utmost stretch of their idea will not place they on a level with the intelligent flea . I should like to see if she could be make ashamed of herself . " 

 CHAPTER VI . 

 though she be dead , yet let I think she live , 
 and feed my mind , that die for want of she . 
  Marlowe : tamburlaine the great . 
 hardly any one in Treby who think at all of Mr. Lyon and his daughter have not feel the same sort of wonder about Esther as Felix feel . she be not much like by her father 's church and congregation . the less serious observe that she have too many air and grace and hold her head much too high ; the strict sort fear greatly that Mr. Lyon have not be sufficiently careful in place his daughter among God - fear people , and that , be lead astray by the melancholy vanity of give she exceptional accomplishment , he have send she to a french school , and allow she to take situation where she have contract notion not 69   only above her own rank , but of too worldly a kind to be safe in any rank . but no one know what sort of woman her mother have be , for Mr. Lyon never speak of his past domesticity . when he be choose as pastor at Treby in 1825 , it be understand that he have be a widower many year , and he have no companion but the tearful and much - exercise Lyddy , his daughter be still at school . it be only two year ago that Esther have come home to live permanently with her father , and take pupil in the town . within that time she have excite a passion in two young dissenting breast that be clothe in the good style of Treby waistcoat a garment which at that period display much design both in the stuff and the wearer ; and she have secure an astonished admiration of her cleverness from the girl of various age who be her pupil ; indeed , her knowledge of French be generally hold to give a distinction to Treby itself as compare with other market - town . but she have win little regard of any other kind . wise dissent matron be divide between fear lest their son should want to marry she and resentment that she should treat those " undeniable " young man with a distant scorn which be hardly to be tolerate in a minister 's daughter ; not only because that parentage appear to entail an obligation to show an exceptional degree of christian humility , but because , look at from a secular point of view , a poor minister must be below the substantial householder who keep he . for at that time the preacher who be pay under the Voluntary system be regard by his flock with feeling not less mixed than the spiritual person who still take his tithe - pig or his modus . his gift be admire , and tear be shed under good bonnet at his sermon ; but the weak tea be think good enough for he ; and even when he go to preach a charity sermon in a strange town , he be treat with home - make wine and the small bedroom . as the good Churchman 's reverence be often mix with growling , and be apt to be give chiefly to an abstract parson who be what a parson ought to be , so the good Dissenter sometimes mix his approval of ministerial gift with considerable criticism and cheapening of the human vessel which contain those treasure . Mrs. Muscat and Mrs. Nuttwood apply the principle of christian equality by remark that Mr. Lyon have his oddity , and that he ought not to allow his daughter to indulge in such unbecoming expenditure on her glove , shoe , and hosiery , even if she do pay for they out of her earning . as for the Church people who 70   engage Miss Lyon to give lesson in their family , their imagination be altogether prostrate by the incongruity between accomplishment and Dissent , between weekly prayer - meeting and a conversance with so lively and altogether worldly a language as the French . Esther 's own mind be not free from a sense of irreconcilableness between the object of her taste and the condition of her lot . she know that Dissenters be look down upon by those whom she regard as the most refined class ; her favorite companion , both in France and at an english school where she have be a junior teacher , have think it quite ridiculous to have a father who be a dissenting preacher ; and when an ardently admire school - fellow induce her parent to take Esther as a governess to the young child , all her native tendency toward luxury , fastidiousness , and scorn of mock gentility , be strengthen by witness the habit of a well - bear and wealthy family . yet the position of servitude be irksome to she , and she be glad at last to live at home with her father , for though , throughout her girlhood , she have wish to avoid this lot , a little experience have teach she to prefer its comparative independence . but she be not content with her life ; she seem to herself to be surround with ignoble , uninteresting condition , from which there be no issue ; for even if she have be unamiable enough to give her father pain deliberately , it would have be no satisfaction to she to go to Treby church , and visibly turn she back on Dissent . it be not religious difference , but social difference , that Esther be concern about , and her ambitious taste would have be no more gratified in the society of the Waces than in that of the Muscats . the Waces speak imperfect English and play whist ; the Muscats speak the same dialect and take in the " Evangelical Magazine . " Esther like neither of these amusement . she have one of those exceptional organization which be quick and sensitive without be in the least morbid ; she be alive to the fine shade of manner , to the nice distinction of tone and accent ; she have a little code of her own about scent and color , texture and behavior , by which she secretly condemn or sanction all thing and person . and she be well satisfied with herself for her fastidious taste , never doubt that her be the high standard . she be proud that the well - bear 71   and handsome girl at school have always say that she might be take for a bear lady . her own pretty instep , clothe in a silk stocking , her little heel , just rise from a kid slipper , her irreproachable nail and delicate wrist , be the object of delighted consciousness to she ; and she feel that it be her superiority which make she unable to use without disgust any but the fine cambric handkerchief and freshest glove . her money all go in the gratification of these nice taste , and she save nothing from her earning . I can not say that she have pang of conscience on this score ; for she feel sure that she be generous : she hat all meanness , would empty her purse impulsively on some sudden appeal to her pity , and if she find out that her father have a want , she would supply it with some pretty device of a surprise . but then the good man so seldom have a want except the perpetual desire , which she could never gratify , of see she under conviction , and fit to become a member of the church . 

 as for little Mr. Lyon , he love and admire this unregenerate child more , he fear , than be consistent with the due preponderance of impersonal and ministerial regard : he pray and plead for she with tear , humble himself for her spiritual deficiency in the privacy of his study ; and then come down stair to find himself in timorous subjection to her wish , lest , as he inwardly say , he should give his teaching an ill savor , by mingle it with outward crossing . there will be queen in spite of salic or other law of later date than Adam and Eve ; and here in this small dingy house of the minister in Malthouse Yard , there be a light - footed , sweet - voice Queen Esther . 

 the strong will always rule , say some , with an air of confidence which be like a lawyer 's flourish , forbid exception or addition . but what be strength ? be it blind wilfulness that see no terror , no many - link consequence , no bruise and wound of those whose cord it tighten ? be it the narrowness of a brain that conceive no need differ from its own , and look to no result beyond the bargain of to - day ; that tug with emphasis for every small purpose , and think it weakness to exercise the sublime power of resolve renunciation ? there be a sort of subjection which be the peculiar heritage of largeness and of love ; and strength be often only another name for willing bondage to irremediable weakness . 

 Esther have affection for her father : she recognize the purity of his character , and a quickness of intellect in he which respond to her 72   own liveliness , in spite of what seem a dreary piety , which select everything that be least interesting and romantic in life and history . but his old clothe have a smoky odor , and she do not like to walk with he , because , when people speak to he in the street , it be his will not , instead of remark on the weather and pass on , to pour forth in an absent manner some reflection that be occupy his mind about the trace of the divine government , or about a peculiar incident narrate in the life of the eminent Mr. Richard Baxter . Esther have a horror of appear ridiculous even in the eye of vulgar Trebians . she fancy that she should have love her mother well than she be able to love her father ; and she wish she could have remember that mother more thoroughly . 

 but she have no more than a broken vision of the time before she be five year old the time when the word oftenest on her lip be " Mamma " ; when a low voice speak caress french word to she , and she in her turn repeat the word to her rag doll ; when a very small white hand , different from any that come after , use to pat she , and stroke she , and tie on her frock and pinafore , and when at last there be nothing but sit with a doll on a bed where mamma be lie , till her father once carry she away . where distinct memory begin , there be no long the low caress voice and the small white hand . she know that her mother be a Frenchwoman , that she have be in want and distress , and that her maiden name be Annette Ledru . her father have tell she no more than this ; and once , he have say , " my Esther , until you be a woman , we will only think of your mother : when you be about to be marry and leave I , we will speak of she , and I will deliver to you her ring and all that be her ; but , without a great command lay upon I , I can not pierce my heart by speak of that which be and be not . " Esther have never forget these word , and the old she become , the more impossible she feel it that she should urge her father with question about the past . 

 his inability to speak of that past to she depend on manifold cause . partly it come from an initial concealment . he have not the courage to tell Esther that he be not really her father : he have not the courage to renounce that hold on her tenderness which the belief in his natural fatherhood must help to give he , or to incur any resentment that her quick spirit might feel at have be bring up under a false 73   supposition . but there be other thing yet more difficult for he to be quite open about deep sorrow of his life as a christian minister that be hardly to be tell to a girl . 

 twenty - two year ago , when Rufus Lyon be no more than thirty - six year old , he be the admired pastor of a large independent congregation in one of our southern seaport town . he be unmarried , and have meet all exhortation of friend who represent to he that a bishop , i.e. , the overseer of an independent church and congregation should be the husband of one wife , by say that St. Paul mean this particular as a limitation , and not as an injunction ; that a minister be permit to have one wife , but that he , Rufus Lyon , do not wish to avail himself of that permission , find his study and other labor of his vocation all - absorbing , and see that mother in Israel be sufficiently provide by those who have not be set apart for a more special work . his church and congregation be proud of he : he be put forward on platform , be make a " deputation , " and be request to preach anniversary sermon in far - off town . wherever noteworthy preacher be discuss , Rufus Lyon be almost sure to be mention as one who do honor to the Independent body ; his sermon be say to be full of fire ; and while he have more of human knowledge than many of his brother , he show in an eminent degree the mark of a true ministerial vocation . but on a sudden this burn and shin light seem to be quench : Mr. Lyon voluntarily resign his charge and withdraw from the town . 

 a terrible crisis have come upon he ; a moment in which religious doubt and newly - awaken passion have rush together in a common flood , and have paralyze his ministerial gift . his thirty - six year have be a story of purely religious and studious fervor ; his passion have be for doctrine , for argumentative conquest on the side of right ; the sin he have chiefly to pray against have be those of personal ambition   under such form as ambition take in the mind of a man who have choose the career of an independent preacher , and those of a too restless intellect , ceaselessly urge question concern the mystery of that which be assuredly reveal , and thus hinder the due nourishment of the soul on the substance of the truth deliver . even at that time of comparative youth , his unworldliness and simplicity in small matter   for he be keenly awake to the large affair of this world   give a certain oddity to his manner and appearance ; and though his sensitive face have much beauty , his person altogether seem so irrelevant to a fashionable view of thing , that well - dress lady and gentleman 74   usually laugh at he , as they probably do at Mr. John Milton after the Restoration and ribbon have come in , and still more at that apostle , of weak bodily presence , who preach in the back street of Ephesus and elsewhere , a new view of a religion that hardly anybody believe in . Rufus Lyon be the singular - look apostle of the Meeting in Skipper 's Lane . be it likely that any romance should befall such a man ? perhaps not ; but romance do befall he . 

 one winter 's evening in 1812 , Mr. Lyon be return from a village preaching . he walk at his usual rapid rate , with busy thought undisturbe by any sight more distinct than the bush and the hedgerow tree , black beneath a faint moonlight , until something suggest to he that he have perhaps omit to bring away with he a thin account - book in which he record certain subscription . he pause , unfasten his outer coat , and feel in all his pocket , then he take off his hat and look inside it . the book be not to be find , and he be about to walk on , when he be startle by hear a low , sweet voice , say , with a strong foreign accent 

 " have pity on I , sir . " 

 search with his short - sighted eye , he perceive some one on a side - bank ; and approach , he find a young woman with a baby on her lap . she speak again more faintly than before . 

 " Sir , I die with hunger ; in the name of God take the little one . " 

 there be no distrusting the pale face and the sweet low voice . without pause , Mr. Lyon take the baby in his arm and say , " can you walk by my side , young woman ? " 

 she rise , but seem tottering . " lean on I , " say Mr. Lyon , and so they walk slowly on , the minister for the first time in his life carry a baby . 

 nothing well occur to he than to take his charge to his own house ; it be the simple way of relieve the woman 's want , and find out how she could be help far ; and he think of no other possibility . she be too feeble for more word to be speak between they till she be seat by his fireside . his elderly servant be not easily amazed at anything her master do in the way of charity , and at once take the baby , while Mr. Lyon unfasten the mother 's damp bonnet and shawl , and give she something warm to drink . then , wait by she till it be time to offer she more , he have nothing to do but to notice 75   the loveliness of her face , which seem to he as that of an angel , with a benignity in its repose that carry a more assured sweetness than any smile . gradually she revive , lift up her delicate hand between her face and the firelight , and look at the baby which lie opposite to she on the old servant 's lap , take in spoonful with much content , and stretch out naked foot toward the warmth . then , as her consciousness of relief grow into contrast memory , she lift up her eye to Mr. Lyon , who stand close by she , and say , in her pretty broken way : 

 " I know you have a good heart when you take your hat off . you seem to I as the image of the bien - amiť Saint Jean . " 

 the grateful glance of those blue - gray eye , with their long shadow - make eyelash , be a new kind of good to Rufus Lyon ; it seem to he as if a woman have never really look at he before . yet this poor thing be apparently a blind french Catholic of delicate nurture , surely , judge from her hand . he be in a tremor ; he feel that it would be rude to question she , and he only urge she now to take a little food . she accept it with evident enjoyment , look at the child continually , and then , with a fresh burst of gratitude , lean forward to press the servant 's hand and say , " oh , you be good ! " then she look up at Mr. Lyon again and say , " be there in the world a pretty marmot ? " 

 the evening pass ; a bed be make up for the strange woman , and Mr. Lyon have not ask she so much as her name . he never go to bed himself that night . he spend it in misery , endure a horrible assault of Satan . he think a frenzy have seize he . wild vision of an impossible future thrust themselves upon he . he dread lest the woman have a husband ; he wish that he might call she his own , that he might worship her beauty , that she might love and caress he . and what to the mass of man would have be only one of many allowable folly a transient fascination , to be dispel by daylight and contact with those common fact of which common - sense be the reflex be to he a spiritual convulsion . he be as one who rave , and know that he rave . these mad wish be irreconcilable with what he be , and must be , as a christian minister , nay , penetrate his soul as tropic heat penetrate the frame , and change for it all aspect and all flavor , they be irreconcilable with that conception of the world which make his faith . all the busy 76   doubt which have before be mere impish shadow flit around a belief that be strong with the strength of an unswerve moral bias , have now gather blood and substance . the questioning spirit have become suddenly bold and blasphemous ; it no long insinuate scepticism it prompt defiance ; it no long express cool , inquisitive thought , but be the voice of a passionate mood . yet he never cease to regard it as the voice of the tempter : the conviction which have be the law of his well life remain within he as a conscience . 

 the struggle of that night be an abridgment of all the struggle that come after . quick soul have their intense life in the first anticipatory sketch of what may or will be , and the pursuit of their wish be the pursuit of that paradisiacal vision which only impel they , and be leave far and far behind , vanish forever even out of hope in the moment which be call success . 

 the next morning Mr. Lyon hear his guest 's history . she be the daughter of a french officer of considerable rank , who have fall in the russian campaign . she have escape from France to England with much difficulty in order to rejoin her husband , a young Englishman , to whom she have become attach during his detention as a prisoner of war on parole at Vesoul , where she be live under the charge of some relative , and to whom she have be marry without the consent of her family . her husband have serve in the hanoverian army , have obtain his discharge in order to visit England on some business , with the nature of which she be not acquaint , and have be take prisoner as a suspect spy . a short time after their marriage he and his fellow - prisoner have be move to a town nearer the coast , and she have remain in wretched uncertainty about he , until at last a letter have come from he tell she that an exchange of prisoner have occur , that he be in England , that she must use her utmost effort to follow he , and that on arrive on english ground she must send he word under a cover which he enclose , bear an address in London . fear the opposition of her friend , she start unknown to they , with a very small supply of money ; and after endure much discomfort and many fear in wait for a passage which she at last get in a small trading smack , she arrive at Southampton ill . before she be able to write , her baby be bear ; and before her husband 's answer come , she have be oblige to pawn some clothe and trinket . he desire she to travel to London where he would meet she at the Belle Sauvage , add that he be himself in distress , 77   and unable to come to she : when once she be in London they would take ship and quit the country . arrive at the Belle Sauvage , the poor thing wait three day in vain for her husband : on the fourth a letter come in a strange hand , say that in his last moment he have desire this letter to be write to inform she of his death , and recommend she to return to her friend . she could choose no other course , but she have soon be reduce to walk , that she might save her pence to buy bread with : and on the evening when she make her appeal to Mr. Lyon , she have pawn the last thing , over and above needful clothing , that she could persuade herself to part with . the thing she have not bear to part with be her marriage - ring , and a locket contain her husband 's hair , and bear his baptismal name . this locket , she say , exactly resemble one wear by her husband on his watch - chain , only that his bore the name Annette , and contain a lock of her hair . the precious trifle now hang round her neck by a cord , for she have sell the small gold chain which formerly hold it . 

 the only guarantee of this story , besides the exquisite candor of her face , be a small packet of paper which she carry in her pocket , consist of her husband 's few letter , the letter which announce his death , and her marriage certificate . it be not so probable a story as that of many an inventive vagrant ; but Mr. Lyon do not doubt it for a moment . it be impossible to he to suspect this angelic - faced woman , but he have strong suspicion concern her husband . he could not help be glad that she have not retain the address he have desire she to send to in London , as that remove any obvious mean of learn particular about he . but enquiry might have be make at Vesoul by letter , and her friend there might have be appeal to . a consciousness , not to be quite silence , tell Mr. Lyon that this be the course he ought to take , but it would have require an energetic self - conquest , and he be excuse from it by Annette 's own disinclination to return to her relative , if any other acceptable possibility could be find . 

 he dread , with a violence of feeling which surmount all struggle , lest anything should take she away , and place such barrier between they as would make it unlikely or impossible that she should ever love he well enough to become his wife . yet he see with perfect clearness that unless he tear up his mad passion by the root , his ministerial 78   usefulness would be frustrate , and the repose of his soul would be destroy . this woman be an unregenerate Catholic ; ten minute ' listen to her artless talk make that plain to he : even if her position have be less equivocal , to unite himself to such a woman be nothing less than a spiritual fall . it be already a fall that he have wish there be no high purpose to which he owe an allegiance that he have long to fly to some backwood where there be no church to reproach he , and where he might have this sweet woman to wife , and to know the joy of tenderness . those sensibility which in most life be diffuse equally through the youthful year , be arouse suddenly in Mr. Lyon , as some man have their special genius reveal to they by a tardy concurrence of condition . his love be the first love of a fresh young heart full of wonder and worship . but what to one man be the virtue which he have sink below the possibility of aspire to , be to another the backsliding by which he forfeit his spiritual crown . 

 the end be , that Annette remain in his house . he have strive against himself so far as to represent her position to some chief matron in his congregation , pray and yet dread that they would so take she by the hand as to impose on he that denial of his own longing not to let she go out of his sight , which he find it too hard to impose on himself . but they regard the case coldly ; the woman be , after all , a vagrant . Mr. Lyon be observe to be surprisingly weak on the subject his eagerness seem disproportionate and unbecoming ; and this young Frenchwoman , unable to express herself very clearly , be no more interesting to those matron and their husband than other pretty young woman suspiciously circumstance . they be willing to subscribe something to carry she on her way , or if she take some lodging they would give she a little sewing , and endeavor to convert she from Papistry . if , however , she be a respectable person , as she say , the only proper thing for she be to go back to her own country and friend . in spite of himself , Mr. Lyon exult . there seem a reason now that he should keep Annette under his own eye . he tell himself that no real object would be serve by his provide food and lodge for she elsewhere an expense which he could ill afford . and she be apparently so helpless , except as to the one task of attend to her baby , that it would have be folly to think of she exert herself for her own support .

 but this course of his be severely disapprove by his church . there be various sign that the minister be under some evil influence : his preaching want its old fervor , he seem to shun the intercourse of his brother , and very mournful suspicion be entertain . a formal remonstrance be present to he , but he meet it as if he have already determine to act in anticipation of it . he admit that external circumstance , conjoin with a peculiar state of mind , be likely to hinder the fruitful exercise of his ministry , and he resign it . there be much sorrow , much expostulation , but he declare that for the present he be unable to unfold himself more fully ; he only wish to state solemnly that Annette Ledru , though blind in spiritual thing , be in a worldly sense a pure and virtuous woman . no more be to be say , and he depart to a distant town . here he maintain himself , Annette and the child , with the remainder of his stipend , and with the wage he earn as a printer 's reader . Annette be one of those angelic - faced helpless woman who take all thing as manna from heaven : the good image of the well - beloved Saint John wish she to stay with he , and there be nothing else that she wish for except the unattainable . yet for a whole year Mr. Lyon never dare to tell Annette that he love she : he tremble before this woman ; he see that the idea of his be her lover be too remote from her mind for she to have any idea that she ought not to live with he . she have never know , never ask the reason why he give up his ministry . she seem to entertain as little concern about the strange world in which she live as a bird in its nest : an avalanche have fall over the past , but she sit warm and uncrushe there be food for many morrow , and her baby flourish . she do not seem even to care about a priest , or about have her child baptize ; and on the subject of religion Mr. Lyon be as timid , and shrink as much from speak to she , as on the subject of his love . he dread anything that might cause she to feel a sudden repulsion toward he . he dread disturb her simple gratitude and content . in these day his religious faith be not slumber ; it be awake and achingly conscious of have fall in a struggle . he have have a great treasure commit to he , and have fling it away : he hold himself a backslider . his unbelieve thought never gain the full ear and consent of his soul . his prayer have be stifle by the sense that there be something he prefer 80   to complete obedience ; they have cease to be anything but intermittent cry and confession , and a submissive presentiment , rise at time even to an entreaty , that some great discipline might come , that the dull spiritual sense might be rouse to full vision and hearing as of old , and the supreme fact become again supreme in his soul . Mr. Lyon will perhaps seem a very simple personage , with pitiably narrow theory ; but none of our theory be quite large enough for all the disclosure of time , and to the end of man 's struggle a penalty will remain for those who sink from the rank of the hero into the crowd for whom the hero fight and die . 

 one day , however , Annette learn Mr. Lyon 's secret . the baby have a tooth come , and be large and strong now , be noisily fretful .  " dear I , what a splendid little boy , Mrs. Transome ! why it can not be can it be that you have the happiness to be a grandmamma ? "

 " no , " say Mrs. Transome , coldly ; ' she be dead . "

 the black eye continue fix as if by a sort of fascination on Lady Debarry 's face , and her affable invitation be unheeded . at last , put his head forward and pout his lip , the cherub give forth with mark intention the sound , " Nau - o - oom , " many time repeat : apparently they sum up his opinion of Lady Debarry , and may perhaps have mean " naughty old woman , " but his speech be a broken lisping polyglot of hazardous interpretation . then he turn to pull at the Blenheim spaniel , which , be old and peevish , give a little snap . 

 " go , go , Harry ; let poor Puff alone he will bite you , " say Mrs. Transome , stoop to release her aged pet . 

 her word be too suggestive , for Harry immediately lay hold of her arm with his tooth , and bit with all his might . happily the stuff upon it be some protection , but the pain force Mrs. Transome to give a low cry ; and Sir Maximus , who have now turn to reseat himself , shake the little rascal off , whereupon he burst away and trot into the library again .

Treby Manor , measure from the front saloon to the remotest shed , be as large as a moderate - sized village , and there be certainly more light burn in it every evening , more wine , spirit , and ale drunk , more waste and more folly , than could be find in some large village . there be fast revelry in the steward 's room , and slow revelry in the Scotch bailiff 's room ; short whist , costume , and flirtation in the housekeeper 's room , and the same at a low price in the servant ' hall ; a select olympian feast in the private apartment of the cook , who be a much grander person than her ladyship , and wear gold and jewelry to a vast amount of suet ; a gambling group in the stable , and the coachman , perhaps the most innocent member of the establishment , tipple in majestic solitude by a fire in the harness - room . for Sir Maximus , as every one say , be a gentleman of the right sort , condescend to no mean enquiry , greet his head - servant with a " good - evening , gentleman , " when he meet they in the park , and only snarl in a subdue way when he look over the account , willing to endure some personal inconvenience in order to keep up the institution of the country , to maintain his hereditary establishment , and do his duty in that station of life the station of the long - tail saurian to which it have please Providence to call he . 

 the focus of brilliancy at Treby Manor that evening be in no way the dining - room , where Sir Maximus sip his port under some mental depression , as he discuss with his brother , the Reverend Augustus , the sad fact that one of the old name in the county be to be on the wrong side not in the drawing - room , where Miss Debarry and Miss Selina , quietly elegant in their dress and manner , be feel rather dull than otherwise , have finish Mr. Bulwer 's " Eugene Aram , " and be throw back on the last great prose work of Mr. Southey , while their mamma slumber a little on the sofa . no ; the centre of eager talk and enjoyment be the steward 's room , where Mr. Scales , house - steward and head - butler , a man most solicitous about his boot , wristband , the roll of his whisker , and other attribute of a gentleman , distribute cigar , cognac , and whiskey , to various colleague and guest who be discuss , with that freedom of conjecture which be one of our inalienable privilege as Britons , the probable amount of Harold 91   Transome 's fortune , concern which fame have already be busy long enough to have acquire vast magnifying power .

 Sircome be an eminent miller who have considerable business transaction at the Manor , and appreciate Mr. Scales 's merit at a handsome percentage on the yearly account . he be a highly honorable tradesman , but in this and in other matter submit to the institution of his country ; for great house , as he observe , must have great butler . he reply to his friend Crowder sententiously .

" reform have set in by the will of the majority that be the rabble , you know ; and the respectability and good sense of the country , which be in the minority , be afraid of Reform run on too fast . so the stream must be run toward Reform and Radicalism ; and if you swim with it , Mr. Sircome , you be a Reformer and a Radical , and your flour be objectionable , and not full weight and be try by Scales , will be find want . " 

 there be a roar of laughter . this pun upon Scales be highly appreciated by every one except the miller and butler . the latter pull down his waistcoat , and puff and star in rather an excited manner . Mr. Christian 's wit , in general , seem to he a poor kind of quibble . 

 " what a fellow you be for fence , Christian , " say the gardener . " hang I , if I do not   think you be up to everything . "

 " a joke 's a joke among gentleman , " say the butler , get exasperate ; " I think there have be quite liberty enough take with my name . but if you must talk about name , I have   hear of a party before now call himself a Christian , and be anything but it . "
