I should not   have mind so much if he have be a tory ! " 

 Felix go along in the twilight struggle in this way with the intricacy of life , which would certainly be greatly simplify if corrupt practice be the invariable mark of wrong opinion . when he have cross the common and have enter the park , the overshadow tree deepen the gray gloom of the evening ; it be useless to try and keep the blind path , and he could only be careful that his step should be bent in the direction of the park gate . he be stride along rapidly now , whistle " bannockburn " in a subdue way as an accompaniment to 135   his inward discussion , when something smooth and soft on which his foot alight arrest he with an unpleasant startling sensation , and make he stoop to examine the object he be treading on . he find it to be a large leather pocket - book swell by its content , and fasten with a seal ribbon as well as a clasp . in stoop he see about a yard off something whitish and square lie on the dark grass . this be an ornamental note - book of pale leather stamp with gold . apparently it have burst open in fall , and out of the pocket form by the cover , there protrude a small gold chain about four inch long , with various seal and other trifle attach to it by a ring at the end . Felix thrust the chain back , and find that the clasp of the note - book be break , he close it and thrust it into his side - pocket , walk along under some annoyance that fortune have make he the finder of article belong most probably to one of the family at Treby Manor . he be much too proud a man to like any contact with the aristocracy , and he could still less endure come within speech of their servant . some plan must be devise by which he could avoid carry these thing up to the Manor himself : he think at first of leave they at the lodge , but he have a scruple against place property , of which the ownership be after all uncertain , in the hand of person unknown to he . it be possible that the large pocket - book contain paper of high importance , and that it do not belong to any of the Debarry family . he resolve at last to carry his finding to Mr. Lyon , who would perhaps be good - natured enough to save he from the necessary transaction with the people at the Manor by undertake those transaction himself . with this determination he walk straight to Malthouse Yard , and wait outside the chapel until the congregation be disperse , when he pass along the aisle to the vestry in order to speak to the minister in private . 

 but Mr. Lyon be not alone when Felix enter . Mr. Nuttwood , the grocer , who be one of the deacon , be complain to he about the obstinate demeanor of the singer , who have decline to change the tune in accordance with a change in the selection of hymn , and have stretch short metre into long out of pure wilfulness and defiance , irreverently adapt the most sacred monosyllable to a multitude of quaver , arrange , it be to be fear , by some musician who be inspire by conceit rather than by the true spirit of psalmody . 

  136 

 " come in , my friend , " say Mr. Lyon , smile at Felix , and then continue in a faint voice , while he wipe the perspiration from his brow and bald crown , " Brother Nuttwood , we must be content to carry a thorn in our side while the necessity of our imperfect state demand that there should be a body set apart and call a choir , whose special office it be to lead the singing , not because they be more disposed to the devout uplifting of praise , but because they be endow with well vocal organ , and have attain more of the musician 's art . for all office , unless it be accompany by peculiar grace , become , as it be , a diseased organ , seek to make itself too much of a centre . singer , specially so call , be , it must be confess , an anomaly among we who seek to reduce the Church to its primitive simplicity , and to cast away all that may obstruct the direct communion of spirit with spirit . " 

 " they be so headstrong , " say Mr. Nuttwood , in a tone of sad perplexity , " that if we deal not warily with they they might end in divide the church , even now that we have have the chapel enlarge . Brother Kemp would side with they , and draw the half part of the member after he . I can not but think it a snare when a professing Christian have a bass voice like Brother Kemp 's . it make he desire to be hear of man ; but the weak song of the humble may have more power in the ear of God . " 

 " do you think it any well vanity to flatter yourself that God like to hear you , though man do not ? " say Felix , with unwarrantable bluntness . 

 the civil grocer be prepared to be scandalize by anything that come from Felix . in common with many hearer in Malthouse Yard , he already feel an objection to a young man who be notorious for have interfere in a question of wholesale and retail , which should have be leave to Providence . old Mr. Holt , be a church member , have probably have " leading " which be more to be rely on than his son 's boasted knowledge . in any case , a little visceral disturbance and inward chastisement to the consumer of questionable medicine would tend less to obscure the divine glory than a show of punctilious morality in one who be not a " professor . " besides , how be it to be know that the medicine would not be bless , if take with due trust in a high influence ? a Christian must consider not the medicine alone in their relation to our frail body   which be dust , but the medicine with Omnipotence behind they . hence a pious vendor will look for 137   " leading , " and he be likely to find they in the cessation of demand and the disproportion of expense and return . the grocer be thus on his guard against the presumptuous disputant . 

 " Mr. Lyon may understand you , sir , " he reply . " he seem to be fond of your conversation . but you have too much of the pride of human learning for I . I follow no new light . " 

 " then follow an old one , " say Felix , mischievously dispose toward a sleek tradesman . " follow the light of the old - fashioned Presbyterians that I have   hear se at Glasgow . the preacher give out the psalm , and then everybody sing a different tune , as it happen to turn up in their throat . it be a domineering thing to set a tune and expect everybody else to follow it . it be a denial of private judgment . " 

 " hush , hush , my young friend , " say Mr. Lyon , hurt by this levity , which glance at himself as well as at the deacon . " play not with paradox . that caustic which you handle in order to scorch other , may happen to sear your own finger and make they dead to the quality of thing . ' tis difficult enough to see our way and keep our torch steady in this dim labyrinth : to whirl the torch and dazzle the eye of our fellow - seeker be a poor daring , and may end in total darkness . you yourself be a lover of freedom , and a bold rebel against usurping authority . but the right to rebellion be the right to seek a high rule , and not to wander in mere lawlessness . wherefore , I beseech you , seem not to say that liberty be license . and I apprehend though I be not endow with an ear to seize those earthly harmony , which to some devout soul have seem , as it be , the broken echo of the heavenly choir I apprehend that there be a law in music , disobedience whereunto would bring we in our singing to the level of shriek maniacs or howl beast : so that herein we be well instruct how true liberty can be nought but the transfer of obedience from the will of one or of a few man to that will which be the norm or rule for all man . and though the transfer may sometimes be but an erroneous direction of search , yet be the search good and necessary to the ultimate finding . and even as in music , where all obey and concur to one end , so that each have the joy of contribute to a whole whereby he be ravish and lift up into the court of heaven , so will it be in that crown time of the millennial reign , when our daily prayer will be fulfil , and one law shall be write on all heart , and be the very structure of all thought , and be the principle of all action . "

 Tired , even exhaust , as the minister have be when Felix Holt enter , the gathering excitement of speech give more and more energy to his voice and manner ; he walk away from the vestry table , he pause , and come back to it ; he walk away again , then come back , and end with his deep toned largo , keep his hand clasp behind he , while his brown eye be bright with the last youthfulness of enthusiastic thought and love . but to any one who have no share in the energy that be thrill his little body , he would have look queer enough . no soon have he finish his eager speech , than he hold out his hand to the deacon , and say , in his former faint tone of fatigue 

 " God be with you , brother . we shall meet to - morrow , and we will see what can be do to subdue these refractory spirit . " 

 when the deacon be go , Felix say , " forgive I , Mr. Lyon ; I be wrong , and you be right . " 

 " yes , yes , my friend , you have that mark of grace within you , that you be ready to acknowledge the justice of a rebuke . sit down ; you have something to say some packet there . " 

 they sit down at a corner of the small table , and Felix draw the note - book from his pocket to lay it down with the pocket - book , say

 as he lift the note - book close to his eye , the chain again slip out . he arrest it and hold it in his hand , while he examine some writing , which appear to be a name on the inner leather . he look long , as if he be try to decipher something that be partly rub out ; and his hand begin to tremble noticeably . he make a movement in an agitated manner , as if he be go to examine the chain and seal , which he hold in his hand . but he check himself , close his hand 139   again , and rest it on the table , while with the other hand he press the side of the note - book together . 

 Felix observe his agitation , and be much surprised ; but with a delicacy of which he be capable under all his abruptness , he say , " you be overcome with fatigue , sir . I be thoughtless to tease you with these matter at the end of Sunday , when you have be preach three sermon . "

 CHAPTER XV . 

 and doubt shall be as lead upon the foot 
 of thy most anxious will . 
 Mr. Lyon be careful to look in at Felix as soon as possible after Christian 's departure , to tell he that his trust be discharge . during the rest of the day he be somewhat relieved from agitate reflection by the necessity of attend to his ministerial duty , the rebuke of rebellious singer be one of they ; and on his return from the Monday evening prayer - meeting he be so overcome with weariness that he go to bed without take note of any object in his study . but when he rise the next morning , his mind , once more eagerly active , be arrest by Philip Debarry 's letter , which still lie open on his desk , and be arrest by precisely that portion which have be unheede the day before : " I shall consider myself doubly fortunate if at any time you can point out to I some method by which I may procure you as lively a satisfaction as I be now feel , in that full and speedy relief from anxiety which I owe to your considerate conduct . " 

 to understand how these word could carry the suggestion they actually have for the minister in a crisis of peculiar personal anxiety and struggle , we must bear in mind that for many year he have walk through life with the sense of have for a space be unfaithful to what he esteem the high trust ever commit to man the ministerial vocation . in a mind of any nobleness , a lapse into transgression against an object still regard as supreme , issue in a new and purer devotedness , chastise by humility and watch over by a passionate regret . so it be with that ardent spirit which animate the little body of Rufus Lyon . once in his life he have be blind , defeat , hurry along by rebellious impulse ; he have go astray after his own desire , and have let the fire die out on the altar ; and as the true penitent , 150   hat his self - besotte error , ask from all come life duty instead of joy , and service instead of ease , so Rufus be perpetually on the watch lest he should ever again postpone to some private affection a great public opportunity which to he be equivalent to a command . 

 now here be an opportunity bring by a combination of that unexpected incalculable kind which might be regard as the divine emphasis invoke especial attention to trivial event an opportunity of secure what Rufus Lyon have often wish for as a means of honor truth , and exhibit error in the character of a stammering , halting , short - breathe usurper of office and dignity . what be more exasperating to a zealous preacher , with whom copious speech be not a difficulty but a relief who never lack argument , but only combatant and listener than to reflect that there be thousand on thousand of pulpit in this kingdom , supply with handsome sounding - board , and occupy an advantageous position in building far large than the chapel in Malthouse Yard building sure to be place of resort , even as the market be , if only from habit and interest ; and that these pulpit be fill , or rather make vacuous , by man whose privileged education in the ancient centre of instruction issue in twenty minute ' formal reading of tepid exhortation or probably infirm deduction from premise base on rotten scaffolding ? and it be in the nature of exasperation gradually to concentrate itself . the sincere antipathy of a dog toward cat in general , necessarily take the form of indignant barking at the neighbor 's black cat which make daily trespass ; the bark at imagined cat , though a frequent exercise of the canine mind , be yet comparatively feeble . Mr. Lyon 's sarcasm be not without an edge when he dilate in general on an elaborate education for teacher which issue in the minimum of teaching , but it find a whetstone in the particular example of that bad system know as the rector of Treby Magna . there be nothing positive to be say against the Rev. Augustus Debarry ; his life could not be pronounce blameworthy except for its negative . and the good Rufus be too pure - minded not to be glad of that . he have no delight in vice as discredit wicked opponent ; he shrink from dwell on the image of cruelty or grossness , and his indignation be habitually inspire only by those moral and intellectual mistake which darken the soul but do not injure or degrade the temple of the body . if the rector have be a less respectable man , Rufus would have more reluctantly make he an object of antagonism ; but as an incarnation of soul - destroy error , 151   dissociate from those base sin which have no good repute even with the worldly , it would be an argumentative luxury to get into close quarter with he , and fight with a dialectic short - sword in the eye of the Treby world   send also a write account thereof to the chief organ of dissenting opinion . Vice be essentially stupid a deaf and eyeless monster , insusceptible to demonstration : the Spirit might work on it by unseen way , and the unstudied sally of sermon be often as the arrow which pierce and awaken the brutified conscience ; but illuminate thought , finely divide speech , be the choicer weapon of the Divine armory , which whoso could wield must be careful not to leave idle . 

 here , then , be the long - for opportunity . here be an engagement an expression of a strong wish on the part of Philip Debarry , if it be in his power , to procure a satisfaction to Rufus Lyon . how have that man of God and exemplary Independent minister , Mr. Ainsworth , of persecute sanctity , conduct himself when a similar occasion have befall he at Amsterdam ? he have think of nothing but the glory of the high cause , and have convert the offer of recompense into a public debate with a Jew on the chief mystery of the faith . here be a model : the case be nothing short of a heavenly indication , and he , Rufus Lyon , would seize the occasion to demand a public debate with the rector on the constitution of the true Church . 

 what if he be inwardly tear by doubt and anxiety concern his own private relation and the fact of his past life ? that danger of absorption within the narrow bound of self only urge he the more toward action which have a wide bearing , and might tell on the welfare of England at large . it be decide . before the minister go down to breakfast that morning he have write the following letter to Mr. Philip Debarry : 

 Sir , refer to your letter of yesterday , I find the following word : " I shall consider myself doubly fortunate if at any time you can point out to I some method by which I may procure you as lively a satisfaction as I be now feel , in that full and speedy relief from anxiety which I owe to your considerate conduct . " 

 I be not unaware , sir , that , in the usage of the world , there be word of courtesy   so call   which be understand , by those amongst whom they be current , to have no precise meaning , and to constitute no bond of obligation . I will not now insist that this be an abuse of language , wherein our fallible nature require the 152   strictest safeguard against laxity and misapplication , for I do not apprehend that in write the word I have above quote , you be open to the reproach of use phrase which , while seem to carry a specific meaning , be really no more than what be call a polite form . I believe , sir , that you use these word advisedly , sincerely , and with an honorable intention of act on they as a pledge , should such action be demand . no other supposition on my part would correspond to the character you bear as a young man who aspire   albeit mistakenly   to engraft the fine fruit of public virtue on a creed and institution , whereof the sap be compose rather of human self - seeking than of everlasting truth . 

 wherefore I act on this my belief in the integrity of your write word ; I beg you to procure for I   as it be doubtless in your power   that I may be allow a public discussion with your near relative , the rector of this parish , the Reverend Augustus Debarry , to be hold in the large room of the Free School , or in the Assembly Room of the Marquis of Granby , these be the large cover space at our command . for I presume he would neither allow I to speak within his church , nor would consent himself to speak within my chapel ; and the probable inclemency of the approach season forbid an assured expectation that we could discourse in the open air . the subject I desire to discuss be first , the Constitution of the true Church ; and secondly , the bearing thereupon of the English Reformation . confidently expect that you will comply with this request , which be the sequence of your express desire , I remain , sir , your , with the respect offer to a sincere withstander , 

 Rufus Lyon , 

 Malthouse Yard . 

 after write this letter , the good Rufus feel that serenity and elevation of mind which be infallibly bring by a preoccupation with the wide relation of thing . already he be begin to sketch the course his argument might most judiciously take in the come debate ; his thought be run into sentence , and mark off careful exception in parenthesis ; and he have come down and seat himself at the breakfast - table quite automatically , without expectation of toast or coffee , when Esther 's voice and touch recall to he an inward debate of another kind , in which he feel himself much weak . again there arise before he the image of that cool , hard - eye , worldly man , who might be this dear child 's father , and one against whose right he have himself grievously offend . always as the image recur to he Mr. Lyon 's heart send forth a prayer for guidance , but no definite guidance have yet make itself visible for he . it could not be guidance it be a temptation that say , " let the matter rest : seek to know no more ; know only what be thrust upon you . " the remembrance that in his time of wander he have wilfully remain in ignorance of fact which he might have enquire after , deepen the impression that it be now an imperative duty to seek the full attainable knowledge . and the 153   enquiry might possibly issue in a blessed repose , by put a negative on all his suspicion . but the more vividly all the circumstance become present to he , the more unfit he feel himself to set about any investigation concern this man who call himself Maurice Christian . he could seek no confidant or helper among " the brother " ; he be oblige to admit to himself that the member of his church , with whom he hop to go to heaven , be not easy to converse with on earth touch the deep secret of his experience , and be still less able to advise he as to the wise procedure in a case of high delicacy , with a worldling who have a carefully - trim whisker and a fashionable costume . for the first time in his life it occur to the minister that he should be glad of an adviser who have more worldly than spiritual experience , and that it might not be inconsistent with his principle to seek some light from one who have study human law . but it be a thought to be pause upon , and not follow out rashly ; some other guidance might intervene . 

 Esther notice that her father be in a fit of abstraction , that he seem to swallow his coffee and toast quite unconsciously , and that he vent from time to time a low guttural interjection , which be habitual with he when he be absorb by an inward discussion . she do not disturb he by remark , and only wonder whether anything unusual have occur on Sunday evening . but at last she think it needful to say , " you recollect what I tell you yesterday , father ? "

I say not that compromise be unnecessary , but it be an evil attendant on our imperfection ; and I would pray every one to mark that , where compromise broaden , intellect and conscience be thrust into narrow room . wherefore it have be my object to show our people that there be many who have help to draw the car of Reform , whose end be but partial , and who forsake not the ungodly principle of selfish alliance , but would only substitute Syria for Egypt think chiefly of their own share in peacock , gold and ivory .  at that time , when faith in the efficacy of political change be at fever - heat in ardent Reformers , many measure which man be still discuss with little confidence on either side , be then talk about and dispose of like property in near reversion . crying abuse " bloated pauper , " " bloated pluralist , " and other corruption hinder man from be wise and happy have to be fight against and slain . such a time be a time of hope . afterward when the corps of those monster have be hold up to the public wonder and abhorrence , and yet wisdom and happiness do not follow , but rather a more abundant breeding of the foolish and unhappy , come a time of doubt and despondency . but in the great reform - year Hope be mighty : the prospect of Reform have even serve the voter instead of drink ; and in one place , at least , there have be " a dry election .  " you grieve I , sir ; you grieve I much . and I pray you to reconsider this question , for it will take you to the root , as I think , of political morality . I engage to show to any impartial mind , duly furnish with the principle of public and private rectitude , that the ballot would be pernicious , and that if it be not pernicious , it would still be futile . I will show , first , that it would be futile as a preservative from bribery and illegitimate influence ; and , secondly , that it would be in the bad kind pernicious , as shut the door against those influence whereby the soul of a man and the character of a citizen be duly educate for their great function . be not alarmed if I detain you , sir . it be well worth the while . "

 if a cynical sprite be present , rid on one of the mote in that dusty room , he may have make himself merry at the illusion of the little minister who bring so much conscience to - bear on the production of so slight an effect . I confess to smile myself , be sceptical as to the effect of ardent appeal and nice distinction on gentleman who be get up , both inside and out , as candidate in the style of the period ; but I never smile at Mr. Lyon 's trustful energy without fall to penitence and veneration immediately after . for what we call illusion be often , in truth , a wide vision of past and recent 165   reality a willing movement of a man 's soul with the large sweep of the world 's force a movement toward a more assured end than the chance of a single life . we see human heroism break into unit and say , this unit do little might as well not have be . but in this way we might break up a great army into unit ; in this way we might break the sunlight into fragment , and think that this and the other might be cheaply part with . let we rather raise a monument to the soldier whose brave heart only keep the rank unbroken and meet death a monument to the faithful who be not famous , and who be precious as the continuity of the sunbeam be precious , though some of they fall unseen and on barrenness . 

 at present , look back on that day at Treby , it seem to I that the sad illusion lie with Harold Transome , who be trust in his own skill to shape the success of his own morrow , ignorant of what many yesterday have determine for he beforehand .

 it be a good and soothfast saw ;

 no crock new - shapen by the wheel ;

 CHAPTER XVIII . 

 the little , nameless , unremembered act 
 of kindness and of love .

 Jermyn do not forget to pay his visit to the minister in Malthouse Yard that evening . the mingled irritation , dread and defiance which he be feel toward Harold Transome in the middle of the day depend on too many and far - stretch cause to be dissipate by eight o'clock ; but when he leave Mr. Lyon 's house he be in a state of comparative triumph in the belief that he , and he alone , be now in possession of fact which , once group together , make a secret that give he new power over Harold .

 " Nay , child , I trust that while I would willingly depart from my evil habit of a somewhat slovenly forgetfulness in my attire , I shall never arrive at the opposite extreme . for though there be that in apparel which please the eye , and I deny not that your neat gown and the color thereof which be that of certain little flower that spread themselves in the hedgerow , and make a blueness there as of the sky when it be deepen in the water I deny not , I say , that these minor striving after a perfection which be , as it be , an irrecoverable yet haunt memory , be a good in their proportion . nevertheless , the brevity of our life , and the hurry and crush of the great battle with error and sin , often oblige we to an advise neglect of what be less momentous . this , I conceive , be the principle on which my friend Felix Holt act ; and I can not but think the light come from the true fount , though it shin through obstruction .  CHAPTER XIX . 

 Consistency ? I never change my mind , 
 which be , and always be , to live at ease . 
 it be only in the time of summer fair that the market - place have ever look more animated than it do under that autumn midday sun . there be plenty of blue cockade and streamer , face at all the window , and a crush buzzing crowd , urge each other backward and forward round the small husting in front of the Ram Inn , which show its more plebeian sign at right angle with the venerable Marquis of Granby . sometimes there be scornful shout , sometimes a rolling cascade of 177   cheer , sometimes the shriek of a penny whistle ; but above all these fitful and feeble sound , the fine old church - tower , which look down from above the tree on the other side of the narrow stream , send vibrating , at every quarter , the sonorous tone of its great bell , the Good Queen Bess . 

 two carriage , with blue ribbon on the harness , be conspicuous near the husting . one be Jermyn 's , fill with the brilliantly - attire daughter , accompany by Esther , whose quieter dress help to mark she out for attention as the most striking of the group . the other be Harold Transome 's ; but in this there be no lady only the olive - skin Dominic , whose acute yet mild face be brighten by the occupation of amusing little Harry and rescue from his tyranny a King Charles puppy , with big eye , much after the pattern of the boy 's . 

 this Trebian crowd do not count for much in the political force of the nation , but it be not the less determined as to lend or not lend its ear . no man be permit to speak from the platform except Harold and his uncle Lingon , though , in the interval of expectation , several Liberals have come forward . among these ill - advise person the one whose attempt meet the most emphatic resistance be Rufus Lyon . this might have be take for resentment at the unreasonableness of the cloth , that , not content with pulpit , from whence to tyrannize over the ear of man , wish to have the large share of the platform ; but it be not so , for Mr. Lingon be hear with much cheering , and would have be welcome again . 

 the rector of Little Treby have be a favorite in the neighborhood since the beginning of the century . a clergyman thoroughly unclerical in his habit have a piquancy about he which make he a sort of practical joke . he have always be call Jack Lingon , or Parson Jack sometimes , in old and less serious day , even " cock - fighting Jack . " he swear a little when the point of a joke seem to demand it , and be fond of wear a colored bandana tie loosely over his cravat , together with large brown leather legging ; he speak in a pithy familiar way that people could understand , and have none of that frigid mincingness call dignity , which some have think a peculiar clerical disease . in fact , he be " a charicter " something cheerful to think of , not entirely out of connection with Sunday and sermon . and it seem in keep that he should have turn sharp round in politic , his opinion be only 178   part of the excellent joke call Parson Jack . when his red eagle face and white hair be see on the platform , the dissenter hardly cheer this questionable Radical ; but to make amend , all the Tory farmer give he a friendly " hurray . " " let us hear what old Jack will say for himself , " be the predominant feeling among they ; " he will have something funny to say , I will bet a penny . " 

 it be only Lawyer Labron 's young clerk and their hanger - on who be sufficiently dead to trebian tradition to assail the parson with various sharp - edge interjection , such as broken shell , and cry of " Cock - a - doodle - doo . " 

 " come now , my lad , " he begin , in his full , pompous , yet jovial tone , thrust his hand into the stuff - out pocket of his greatcoat , " I will tell you what ; I be a parson you know ; I ought to return good for evil . so here be some good nut for you to crack in return for your shell . " 

 there be a roar of laughter and cheer as he throw handfuls of nut and filbert among the crowd . 

 " come now , you will say I use to be a tory ; and some of you , whose face I know as well as I know the head of my own crab - stick , will say that be why I be a good fellow . but now I will tell you something else . it be for that very reason that I use to be a tory , and be a good fellow that I go along with my nephew here , who be a thorough - go liberal . for will anybody here come forward and say , ' a good fellow have no need to tack about and change his road ? ' no , there be not one of you such a Tom - noddy . what be good for one time be bad for another . if anybody contradict that , ask he to eat pickle pork when he be thirsty , and to bathe in the Lapp there when the spike of ice be shoot . and that be the reason why the man who be the good liberal now be the very man who use to be the good Tories . there be not   a nasty horse than your horse that will jib and back and turn round when there be but one road for he to go , and that be the road before he . 

 " and my nephew here he come of a Tory breed , you know I will answer for the lingon . in the old Tory time there be never a pup belong to a Lingon but would howl if a Whig come near he . the Lingon blood be good , rich old Tory blood like good rich milk and that be why , when the right time come , it throw up a liberal cream . the good sort of Tory turn to the good sort of Radical . there be plenty of radical scum I say , beware of the scum , and look but for the cream . and here be my nephew some 179   of the cream , if there be any : none of your whig , none of your paint water that look as if it run , and it be stand still all the while ; none of your spinning - jenny fellow . a gentleman ; but up to all sort of business . I be no fool myself ; I be force to wink a good deal , for fear of see too much , for a neighborly man must let himself be cheat a little . but though I have   never be out of my own country , I know less about it than my nephew do . you may tell what he be , and only look at he . there be one sort of fellow see nothing but the end of his own nose , and another sort that see nothing but the hinder side of the moon ; but my nephew Harold be of another sort ; he see everything that be at hit distance , and he be not one to miss his mark . a good - look man in his prime ! not a greenhorn ; not a shrivelled old fellow , who will come to speak to you and find he be leave his tooth at home by mistake . Harold Transome will do you credit ; if anybody say the Radicals be a set of sneak , Brummagem half - penny , scamp who want to play pitch - and - toss with the property of the country , you can say , ' look at the member for North Loamshire ! ' and mind what you will hear he say ; he will go in for make everything right poor - law and Charities and Church he want to reform them all . perhaps you will say , ' there be that Parson Lingon talk about Church Reform why , he belong to the Church himself he want reform too . ' well , well , wait a bit , and you will hear by - and - by that old Parson Lingon be reform shoot no more , crack his joke no more , have drink his last bottle : the dog , the old pointer , will be sorry ; but you will hear that the Parson at Little Treby be a new man . that be what Church Reform be sure to come to before long . so now here be some more nuts for you , lad , and I leave you to listen to your candidate . here he be give he a good hurray ; wave your hat , and I will begin . hurray ! " 

 Harold have not be quite confident beforehand as to the good effect of his uncle 's introduction ; but he be soon reassure . there be no acrid partisanship among the old - fashioned Tories who muster strong about the Marquis of Granby , and Parson Jack have put they in a good humor . Harold 's only interruption come from his own party . the oratorical clerk at the Factory , act as the tribune of the dissenting interest , and feel bound to put question , might have be troublesome ; but his voice be unpleasantly sharp , while Harold 's be full and penetrate , the questioning be cry down . Harold 's speech " do " : it be not of the glib - nonsensical sort , not ponderous , 180   not hesitate which be as much as to say , that it be remarkable among british speech . read in print the next day , perhaps it would be neither pregnant nor conclusive , which be say no more than that its excellence be not of an abnormal kind , but such as be usually find in the good effort of eloquent candidate . accordingly , the applause drown the opposition , and content predominate . 

 but , perhaps , the moment of most diffusive pleasure from public speaking be that in which the speech cease and the audience can turn to comment on it . the one speech , sometimes utter under great responsibility as to missile and other consequence , have give a text to twenty speaker who be under no responsibility . even in the day of duel a man be not challenge for be a bore , nor do this quality apparently hinder he from be much invite to dinner , which be the great index of social responsibility in a less barbarous age . 

 certainly the crowd in the market - place seem to experience this culminate enjoyment when the speaking on the platform in front of the Ram have cease , and there be no less than three orator hold forth from the elevation of chance vehicle , not at all to the prejudice of the talking among those who be on a level with their neighbor . there be little ill - humor among the listener , for Queen Bess be strike the last quarter before two , and a savory smell from the inn kitchen inspire they with an agreeable consciousness that the speaker be help to trifle away the brief time before dinner .

 CHAPTER XX . 

 " good earthenware pitcher , sir ! of an excellent quaint pattern and sober color . " 

 the market dinner at " the Marquis " be in high repute in Treby and its neighborhood . the frequenter of this three - and - sixpenny ordinary like to allude to it , as man allude to anything which imply that they move in good society , and habitually converse with those who be in the secret of the high affair . the guest be not only such rural resident as have drive to market , but some of the most substantial townsman , who have always assure their wife that business require this weekly sacrifice of domestic pleasure . the poor farmer , who put up at the Ram or the Seven Stars , where there be no fish , feel their disadvantage , bear it modestly or bitterly , as the case might be ; 182   and although the Marquis be a Tory house , devote to Debarry , it be too much to expect that such tenant of the Transomes as have always be use to dine there , should consent to eat a bad dinner , and sit with bad company , because they suddenly find themselves under a radical landlord , oppose to the political party know as Sir Maxim 's . hence the recent political division have not reduce the handsome length of the table at the Marquis ; and the many gradation of dignity from Mr. Wace , the brewer , to the rich butcher from Leek Malton , who always modestly take the low seat , though without the reward of be ask to come up higher have not be abbreviate by any secession . 

 to - day there be an extra table spread for expect supernumerary , and it be at this that Christian take his place with some of the young farmer , who have almost a sense of dissipation in talk to a man of his questionable station and unknown experience . the provision be especially liberal , and on the whole the presence of a minority destine to vote for Transome be a ground for joking , which add to the good humor of the chief talker . a respectable old acquaintance turn Radical rather against his will , be rally with even great gusto than if his wife have have twin twice over . the good Trebian Tories be far too sweet - blooded to turn against such old friend , and to make no distinction between they and the Radical , Dissenting , Papistical , Deistical set with whom they never din , and probably never see except in their imagination . but the talk be necessarily in abeyance until the more serious business of dinner be end , and the wine , spirit , and tobacco raise mere satisfaction into beatitude . 

 among the frequent though not regular guest , whom every one be glad to see , be Mr. Nolan , the retire London hosier , a wiry old gentleman past seventy , whose square , tight forehead , with its rigid hedge of gray hair , whose bushy eyebrow , sharp dark eye , and remarkable hooked nose , give a handsome distinction to his face in the midst of rural physiognomy . he have marry a Miss Pendrell early in life , when he be a poor young Londoner , and the match have be think as bad as ruin by her family ; but fifteen year ago he have have the satisfaction of bring his wife to settle amongst her own friend , and of be receive with pride as a brother - in - law , retire from business , possess of unknown thousand , and of a most agreeable talent for anecdote and conversation generally . no question have ever be raise 183   as to Mr. Nolan 's extraction on the strength of his hooked nose , or of his name be baruch . hebrew name " run " in the good Saxon family ; the Bible account for they ; and no one among the upland and hedgerow of that district be suspect of have an oriental origin unless he carry a peddler 's jewel - box . certainly , whatever genealogical research might have discover , the worthy Baruch Nolan be so free from any distinctive mark of religious persuasion he go to church with so ordinary an irregularity , and so often grumble at the sermon that there be no ground for class he otherwise than with good Trebian Churchmen . he be generally regard as a good - look old gentleman , and a certain thin eagerness in his aspect be attribute to the life of the metropolis , where narrow space have the same sort of effect on man as on thickly - plant tree . Mr. Nolan always order his pint of port , which , after he have sip it a little , be will not to animate his recollection of the Royal Family , and the various ministry which have be contemporary with the successive stage of his prosperity . he be always listen to with interest : a man who have be bear in the year when good old King George come to the throne who have be acquaint with the nude leg of the Prince Regent , and hint at private reason for believe that the Princess Charlotte ought not to have die have conversational matter as special to his auditor as Marco Polo could have have on his return from his asiatic travel . 

 " my good sir , " he say to Mr. Wace , as he cross his knee and spread his silk handkerchief over they , " Transome may be return , or he may not be return that be a question for North Loamshire ; but it make little difference to the kingdom . I do not   want to say thing which may put young man out of spirit , but I believe this country have see it be good day I do , indeed . " 

 " I be sorry to hear it from one of your experience , Mr. Nolan , " say the brewer , a large , happy - look man . " I would make a good fight myself before I would leave a bad world for my boy than I have   find for myself . there be not   a great pleasure than do a bit of planting and improve one 's building , and invest one 's money in some pretty acre of land , and when it turn up here and there land you have   know from a boy . it be a nasty thought that these Radicals be to turn thing round so as one can calculate on nothing . one do not   like it for one 's self , and one do not   like it for one 's neighbor . but somehow , I believe it will not   184   do : if we can not   trust the Government just now , there be Providence and the good sense of the country ; and there be a right in thing that be what I have   always say there be a right in thing . the heavy end will get downmost . and if Church and King , and every man be sure of his own , be thing good for this country , there be a God above will take care of them . " 

 " it will not   do , my dear sir , " say Mr. Nolan " it will not   do . when Peel and the Duke turn round about the Catholics in ' 29 , I see it be all over with we . we could never trust minister any more . it be to keep off a rebellion , they say ; but I say it be to keep their place . they be monstrously fond of place , both of they that I know .  CHAPTER XXI . 

 ' tis grievous that with all amplification of travel both by sea and land , a man can never separate himself from his past history . 

 Mr. Jermyn 's handsome house stand a little way out of the town , surround by garden and lawn and plantation of hopeful tree . as Christian approach it he be in a perfectly easy state of mind : the business he be go on be none of his , otherwise than as he be well satisfied with any opportunity of make himself valuable to Mr. Philip Debarry . as he look at Jermyn 's length of wall and iron railing , he say to himself , " these lawyer be the fellow for get on in the world with the least expense of civility . with this cursed conjuring secret of theirs call Law , they think everybody be frightened at they . my Lord Jermyn seem to have his insolence as ready as his soft sawder . he be as sleek as a rat , and have as vicious a tooth . I know the sort of vermin well enough . I have   help to fatten one or two . " 

 in this mood of conscious , contemptuous penetration , Christian be show by the footman into Jermyn 's private room , where the attorney sit surround with massive oaken bookcase , and other furniture to correspond , from the thick - legged library - table to the calendar frame and card - rack . it be the sort of a room a man prepare for himself when he feel sure of a long and respectable future . he be lean back in his leather chair , against the broad window opening on the lawn , and have just take off his spectacle and let the newspaper fall on his knee , in despair of reading by the fade light . 191 

 when the footman open the door and say , " Mr. Christian , " Jermyn say , " good evening , Mr. Christian . be seat , " point to a chair opposite himself and the window . " light the candle on the shelf , John , but leave the blind alone . " 

 he do not speak again till the man be go out , but appear to be refer to a document which lie on the bureau before he . when the door be close he draw himself up again , begin to rub his hand , and turn toward his visitor , who seem perfectly indifferent to the fact that the attorney be in shadow , and that the light fall on himself . 

 " a your name a be Henry Scaddon . " 

 there be a start through Christian 's frame which he be quick enough , almost simultaneously , to try and disguise as a change of position . he uncross his leg and unbutton his coat . but before he have time to say anything , Jermyn go on with slow emphasis . 

 " you be bear on the sixteenth of December , 1782 , at Blackheath . your father be a cloth - merchant in London : he die when you be barely of age , leave an extensive business : before you be five - and - twenty you have run through the great part of the property , and have compromise your safety by an attempt to defraud your creditor . subsequently you forge a check on your father 's eld brother , who have intend to make you his heir . " 

 here Jermyn pause a moment and refer to the document . Christian be silent . 

 " in 1808 you find it expedient to leave this country in a military disguise , and be take prisoner by the French . on the occasion of an exchange of prisoner you have the opportunity of return to your own country , and to the bosom of your own family . you be generous enough to sacrifice that prospect in favor of a fellow - prisoner , of about your own age and figure , who have more pressing reason than yourself for wish to be on this side of the water . you exchange dress , luggage , and name with he , and he pass to England instead of you as Henry Scaddon . almost immediately afterward you escape from your imprisonment , after feign an illness which prevent your exchange of name from be discover ; and it be report that you that be , you under the name of your fellow - prisoner be drown in an open boat , try to reach a Neapolitan vessel bind for Malta . nevertheless I have to congratulate you on the falsehood of that report , and on the certainty that you be now , after the lapse of more than twenty year , seat here in perfect safety . " 

 Jermyn pause so long that he be evidently await some answer . at last Christian reply in a dogged tone 

 " well , sir , I have   hear much long story than that tell quite as solemnly , when there be not a word of truth in they . suppose I deny the very peg you hang your statement on . suppose I say I be not Henry Scaddon . " 

 " a in that case a , " say Jermyn , with wooden indifference , " you would lose the advantage which a may attach to your possession of Henry Scaddon 's knowledge . and at the same time , if it be in the least a inconvenient to you that you should be recognize as Henry Scaddon , your denial would not prevent I from hold the knowledge and evidence which I possess on that point ; it would only prevent we from pursue the present conversation . " 

 " well , sir , suppose we admit , for the sake of the conversation , that your account of the matter be the true one : what advantage have you to offer the man name Henry Scaddon ? " 

 " the advantage a be problematical ; but it may be considerable . it might , in fact , release you from the necessity of act as courier , or a valet , or whatever other office you may occupy which prevent you from be your own master . on the other hand , my acquaintance with your secret be not necessarily a disadvantage to you . to put the matter in a nutshell , I be not incline a gratuitously to do you any harm , and I may be able to do you a considerable service . " 

 " which you want I to earn somehow ? " say Christian . " you offer I a turn in a lottery ? " 

 " precisely . the matter in question be of no earthly interest to you , except a as it may yield you a prize . we lawyer have to do with complicated question , and a legal subtlety , which be never a fully know even to the party immediately interested , still less to the witness . shall we agree , then , that you continue to retain two - third of the name which you gain by exchange , and that you oblige I by answer certain question as to the experience of Henry Scaddon ? " 

 " very good . go on . " 

 " what article of property once belong to your fellow - prisoner , Maurice Christian Bycliffe , do you still retain ? " 

  193 

 " this ring , " say Christian , twirl round the fine seal - ring on his finger , " his watch , and the little matter that hang with it , and a case of paper . I get rid of a gold snuff - box once when I be hard up . the clothe be all go , of course . we exchange everything ; it be all do in a hurry . Bycliffe think we should meet again in England before long , and he be mad to get there . but that be impossible I mean that we should meet soon after . I do not   know what be become of he , else I would give he up his paper and the watch , and so on though , you know , it be I who do he the service , and he feel that . " 

 " you be at Vesoul together before be move to Verdun ? " 

 " yes . " 

 " what else do you know about Bycliffe ? " 

 " oh , nothing very particular , " say Christian pausing , and rap his boot with his cane . " he have be in the hanoverian army a high - spirited fellow , take nothing easily ; not over - strong in health . he make a fool of himself with marry at Vesoul ; and there be the devil to pay with the girl 's relation ; and then , when the prisoner be order off , they have to part . whether they ever get together again I do not   know . " 

 " be the marriage all right then ? " 

 " oh , all on the square civil marriage , church everything . Bycliffe be a fool a good - natured , proud , headstrong fellow . " 

 " how long do the marriage take place before you leave Vesoul ? " 

 " about three month . I be witness to the marriage . " 

 " and you know no more about the wife ? "

 CHAPTER XXIII . 

 " I will not excuse you ; you shall not be excuse ; excuse shall not be admit ; there be no excuse shall serve ; you shall not be excuse . " Henry IV . 

 when Philip Debarry have come home that morning and read the letter which have not be forward to he , he laugh so heartily at Mr. Lyon 's that he congratulate himself on be in his private room . otherwise his laughter would have awaken the curiosity of Sir Maximus , and Philip do not wish to tell any one the content of the letter until he have show they to his uncle . he determine to ride over to the rectory to lunch ; for as Lady Mary be away , he and his uncle might be tete - š - tete . 

 the rectory be on the other side of the river , close to the church of which it be the fitting companion : a fine old brick - and - stone house , with a great bow - window opening from the library on to the deep - turfed lawn , one fat dog sleep on the door - stone , another fat dog waddle on the gravel , the autumn leave duly sweep away , the linger chrysanthemum cherish , tall tree stoop or soar in the most picturesque variety , and a virginian creeper turn a little rustic hut into a scarlet pavilion . it be one of those rectory which be among the bulwark of our venerable institution which arrest disintegrating doubt , serve as a double embankment against Popery and Dissent , and rally feminine instinct and affection to reinforce the decision of masculine thought . 

 " what make you look so merry , Phil ? " say the rector , as his nephew enter the pleasant library . 

 " something that concern you , " say Philip , take out the letter . " a clerical challenge . here be an opportunity for you to emulate the divine of the sixteenth century and have a theological duel . read this letter . " 

 " what answer have you send the crazy little fellow ? " say the rector , keep the letter in his hand and run over it again and again , with brow knit , but eye gleam without any malignity . 

 " oh , I send no answer . I await your . " 

 " mine ! " say the rector , throw down the letter on the table . " you do not   suppose I be go to hold a public debate with a schismatic of that sort ? I should have an infidel shoemaker next expect I to answer blasphemy deliver in bad grammar . " 

 " but you see how he put it , " say Philip . with all his gravity of nature he could not resist a slightly mischievous prompting , though he 206   have a serious feeling that he should not like to be regard as fail to fulfill his pledge . " I think if you refuse , I shall be oblige to offer myself . " 

 " nonsense ! tell he he be himself act a dishonorable part in interpret your word as a pledge to do any preposterous thing that suit his fancy . suppose he have ask you to give he land to build a chapel on ; doubtless that would have give he a ' lively satisfaction . ' a man who put a non - natural , strained sense on a promise be no well than a robber . " 

 " but he have not ask for land . I dare say he think you will not   object to his proposal . I confess there be a simplicity and quaintness about the letter that rather please I . " 

 " let I tell you , Phil , he be a crazy little firefly , that do a great deal of harm in my parish . he inflame the dissenter ' mind on politic . there be no end to the mischief do by these busy , prate man . they make the ignorant multitude the judge of the large question , both political and religious , till we shall soon have no institution leave that be not on a level with the comprehension of a huckster or a drayman . there can be nothing more retrograde lose all the result of civilization , all the lesson of Providence let the windlass run down after man have be turn at it painfully for generation . if the instruct be not to judge for the uninstructed , why , let we set Dick Stubbs to make our almanac , and have a President of the Royal Society elect by universal suffrage . " 

 the rector have rise , place himself with his back to the fire , and thrust his hand in his pocket , ready to insist far on this wide argument . Philip sit nursing one leg , listen respectfully , as he always do , though often listen to the sonorous echo of his own statement , which suit his uncle 's need so exactly that he do not distinguish they from his own impression . 

 " true , " say Philip ; " but in special case we have to do with special condition . you know I defend the casuist . and it may happen that , for the honor of the church in Treby , and a little also for my honor , circumstance may demand a concession even to some notion of a dissent preacher . " 

 " not at all . I should be make a figure which my brother clergy might well take as an affront to themselves . the character of the Establishment have suffer enough already through the Evangelicals , with their extempore incoherence and their pipe - smoking piety . look at Wimple , the man who be vicar of Shuttleton without his gown and 207   band anybody would take he for a grocer in mourning . " 

 " well , I shall cut a still bad figure , and so will you , in the dissent magazine and newspaper . it will go the round of the kingdom . there will be a paragraph head , ' Tory Falsehood and Clerical Cowardice , ' or else , ' the Meanness of the Aristocracy and the Incompetence of the Beneficed Clergy . ' " 

 " there would be a bad paragraph if I be to consent to the debate . of course it would be say that I be beat hollow , and , that now the question have be clear up at Treba Magna , the Church have not a sound leg to stand on . besides , " the rector go on , frown and smile , " it be all very well for you to talk , Phil ; but this debating be not so easy when a man 's close upon sixty . what one write or say must be something good and scholarly ; and , after all have be do , this little Lyon would buzz about one like a wasp , and cross - question and rejoin . let I tell you , a plain truth may be so worried and maul by fallacy as to get the bad of it . there be no such thing as tire a talk - machine like Lyon . " 

 " then you absolutely refuse ? " 

 " yes , I do . " 

 " you remember that when I write my letter of thank to Lyon you approve my offer to serve he if possible . " 

 " certainly I remember it . but suppose he have ask you to vote for civil marriage , or to go and hear he preach every Sunday ? " 

 " but he have not ask that . " 

 " something as unreasonable , though . "

 Philip have not time to object before Mr. Sherlock appear a young divine of good birth and figure , of sallow complexion and bashful address .

 Mr. Sherlock smile with rather a trembling lip , willing to distinguish himself , but hop that the rector only allude to a dialogue on Baptism by Aspersion , or some other pamphlet suit to the purpose of the Christian Knowledge Society . but as the rector proceed to unfold the circumstance under which his eminent service be to be render , he grow more and more nervous .

 " that be your modesty , Sherlock . do not   let I hear any more of that . " 

  209 

 Philip sit down to write , and the rector , with his firm ring voice , go on at his ease , give " indication " to his agitated curate . 

 " but you can begin at once prepare a good , cogent , clear statement , and consider the probable point of assault . you can look into Jewel , Hall , Hooker , Whitgift , and the rest : you will find they all here . my library want nothing in english divinity . sketch the low ground take by Usher and those man , but bring all your force to bear on mark out the true high - church doctrine . expose the wretched cavil of the Noncomformists , and the noisy futility that belong to schismatic generally . I will give you a telling passage from Burke on the Dissenters , and some good quotation which I bring together in two sermon of my own on the Position of the English Church in Christendom . how long do you think it will take you to bring your thought together ? you can throw they afterward into the form of an essay ; we will have the thing print ; it will do you good with the Bishop . " 

 with all Mr. Sherlock 's timidity , there be fascination for he in this distinction . he reflect that he could take coffee and sit up late , and perhaps produce something rather fine . it might be a first step toward that eminence which it be no more than his duty to aspire to . even a polemical fame like that of a Philpotts must have have a beginning . Mr. Sherlock be not insensible to the pleasure of turn sentence successfully , and it be a pleasure not always unconnected with preferment . a diffident man like the idea of do something remarkable , which will create belief in he without any immediate display of brilliancy . celebrity may blush and be silent , and win a grace the more . thus Mr. Sherlock be constrain , tremble all the while , and much wish that his essay be already in print . 

 " I think I could hardly be ready under a fortnight . " 

 " very good . just write that , Phil , and tell he to fix the precise day and place . and then we will go to lunch . " 

 the rector be quite satisfied . he have talk himself into think that he should like to give Sherlock a few useful hint , look up his own early sermon , and benefit the curate by his criticism , when the argument have be get into shape . he be a healthy - natured man , but that be not at all a reason why he should not have those sensibility to the odor of authorship which belong to almost everybody who be not expect to be a writer and especially to that form of authorship which be call suggestion , and consist in tell another man that 210   he might do a great deal with a give subject , by bring a sufficient amount of knowledge , reasoning , and wit to bear upon it .

 if he come not , the play be mar . Midsummer Night 's Dream . 

 Rufus Lyon be very happy on that mild November morning appoint for the great conference in the large room at the Free School , between himself and the Reverend Theodore Sherlock , B.A. the disappointment of not contend with the rector in person , which have at first be bitter , have be gradually lose sight of in the positive enjoyment of an opportunity for debate on any term .  " and suppose I have reason to suspect that there would be some great advantage for she if the law know who be her father ? " 222

 Felix go in with Esther to take tea , but the conversation be entirely between he and Mr. Lyon about the trick of canvassing , the foolish personality of the placard , and the probability of Transome 's return , as to which Felix declare himself to have become indifferent . this scepticism make the minister uneasy : he have great belief in the old political watchword , have preach that universal suffrage and no ballot be agreeable to the will of God , and like to believe that a visible " instrument " be forthcoming in the radical candidate who have pronounce emphatically against Whig finality . Felix , be in a perverse mood , contend that universal suffrage would be equally agreeable to the devil ; that he would change his politic a little , have a large traffic , and see himself more fully represent in Parliament . 

 " Nay , my friend , " say the minister , " you be again sport with paradox ; for you will not deny that you glory in the name of Radical , or Root - and - branch man , as they say in the great time when Nonconformity be in its giant youth . " 

 " a radical yes ; but I want to go to some root a good deal lower down than the franchise . " 

 " truly there be a work within which can not be dispense with ; but it be our preliminary work to free man from the stifle life of political nullity , and bring they into what Milton call ' the liberal air , ' wherein alone can be work the final triumph of the Spirit . " 

 " with all my heart . but while Caliban be caliban , though you multiply he by a million , he will worship every Trinculo that carry a bottle . I forget , though you do not   read Shakespeare , Mr. Lyon . " 

 " I be bind to confess that I have so far look into a volume of Esther 's as to conceive your meaning ; but the fantasy therein be 236   so little to be reconcile with a steady contemplation of that divine economy which be hide from sense and reveal to faith , that I forbear the reading , as likely to perturb my ministration . " 

 Esther sit by in unusual silence . the conviction that Felix will her exclusion from his life be make it plain that something more than friendship between they be not so thoroughly out of the question as she have always inwardly assert . in her pain that his choice lie aloof from she , she be compel frankly to admit to herself the longing that it have be otherwise , and that he have entreat she to share his difficult life . he be like no one else to she : he have seem to bring at once a law , and the love that give strength to obey the law . yet the next moment , sting by his independence of she , she deny that she love he ; she have only long for a moral support under the negation of her life . if she be not to have that support , all effort seem useless . 

 Esther have be so long use to hear the formula of her father 's belief without feel or understand they , that they have lose all power to touch she . the first religious experience of her life the first self - questioning , the first voluntary subjection , the first longing to acquire the strength of great motif and obey the more strenuous rule have come to she through Felix Holt . no wonder that she feel as if the loss of he be inevitable backslide . 

 but be it certain that she should lose he ? she do not believe that he be really indifferent to she . 

 CHAPTER XXVIII . 

 Titus . but what say Jupiter , I ask thee ? 
 Clown . alas , sir , I know not Jupiter : 
  I never drink with he in all my life .

 the multiplication of uncomplimentary placard notice by Mr. Lyon and Felix Holt be one of several sign that the day of nomination and election be approach . the presence of the Revising Barrister in Treby be not only an opportunity for all person not otherwise busy to show their zeal for the purification of the voting - list , but also to reconcile private ease and public duty by stand about the street and lounge at door . 

 it be no light business for Trebians to form an opinion ; the mere fact of a public functionary with an unfamiliar title be enough to give 237   they pause , as a premise that be not to be quickly start from . to Mr. Pink , the saddler , for example , until some distinct injury or benefit have accrue to he , the existence of the Revising Barrister be like the existence of the young giraffe which Wombwell have lately bring into those part it be to be contemplate , and not criticise . Mr. Pink profess a deep - dye Toryism ; but he regard all fault - finding as radical and somewhat impious , as disturbing to trade , and likely to offend the gentry , or the servant through whom their harness be order : there be a Nemesis in thing which make objection unsafe , and even the Reform Bill be a sort of electric eel which a thrive tradesman have well leave alone . it be only the " Papists " who live far enough off to be speak of uncivilly . 

 but Mr. Pink be fond of news , which he collect and retail with perfect impartiality , note fact and reject comment . hence he be well pleased to have his shop so constant a place of resort for lounger , that to many Trebians there be a strong association between the pleasure of gossip and the smell of leather . he have the satisfaction of chalking and cutting , and of keep his journeyman close at work , at the very time that he learn from his visitor who be those whose vote have be call in question before his Honor , how Lawyer Jermyn have be too much for Lawyer Labron about Todd 's cottage , and how , in the opinion of some townsman , this look into the value of people 's property , and swear it down below a certain sum , be a nasty inquisitorial kind of thing ; while other observe that be nice to a few pound be all nonsense they should put the figure high enough , and then never mind if a voter 's qualification be thereabout . but , say Mr. Sims , the auctioneer , everything be do for the sake of the lawyer .  the Cross - Keys be a very old - fashioned " public " ; its bar be a big ramble kitchen , with an undulating brick floor ; the small - pan window throw an interesting obscurity over the far - off dresser , garnish with pewter and tin , and with large dish that seem to speak of well time ; the two settle be half push under the wide - mouthed chimney ; and the grate with its brick hob , massive iron crane , and various pothook , suggest a generous plenty possibly existent in all mood and tense except the indicative present . one way of get an idea of our fellow - countryman 's misery be to go and look at their pleasure . the Cross - Keys have a fungous - feature landlord and a yellow sickly landlady , with a large white kerchief bind round her cap , as if her head have recently require surgery ; it have doctor ale , an odor of bad tobacco , and remarkably strong cheese . it be not what Astraa , when come back , might be expect to approve as the scene of ecstatic enjoyment for the being whose special prerogative it be to lift their sublime face toward heaven . still , there be ample 240   space on the hearth accommodation for narrative bagman or boxmen room for a man to stretch his leg ; his brain be not press upon by a white wall within a yard of he , and the light do not stare in mercilessly on bare ugliness , turn the fire to ash . compare with some beerhouse of this more advanced period , the Cross - key of that day present a high standard of pleasure .
