 CHAPTER XXIX . 

 I doe believe that , as the gall have severall receptacle in several creature , soe there be scarce any creature but hath that emunctorye somewhere . Sir Thomas Browne . 

 Fancy what a game at chess would be if all the chessman have passion and intellect , more or less small and cunning : if you be not only uncertain about your adversary 's man , but a little uncertain about your own ; if your knight could shuffle himself on to a new square by the sly ; if your bishop , in disgust at your castling , could wheedle your pawn out of their place ; and if your pawn , hat you because they be pawn , could make away from their appoint post that you might get checkmate on a sudden . you might be the long - headed of deductive reasoner , and yet you might be beat by your own pawn . you would be especially likely to be beat , if you depend arrogantly on your mathematical imagination , and regard your passionate piece with contempt . 

 yet this imaginary chess be easy compare with the game a man have to play against his fellow - man with other fellow - man for his instrument . he think himself sagacious , perhaps , because he trust no bond except that of self - interest : but the only self - interest he can safely rely on be what seem to be such to the mind he would use or govern . can he ever be sure of know this ? 

 Matthew Jermyn be under no misgiving as to the fealty of Johnson . he have " be the making of Johnson " ; and this seem to many man as a reason for expect devotion , in spite of the fact that they themselves , though very fond of their own person and life , be not at all devoted to the Maker they believe in . Johnson be a most serviceable 248   subordinate . be a man who aim at respectability , a family man , who have a good church - pew , subscribe for engraving of banquet picture where there be portrait of political celebrity , and wish his child to be more unquestionably genteel than their father , he present all the more numerous handle of worldly motive by which a judicious superior might get a hold on he . but this useful regard to respectability have its inconvenience in relation to such a superior : it be a mark of some vanity and some pride , which , if they be not touch just in the right handling - place , be liable to become raw and sensitive . Jermyn be aware of Johnson 's weakness , and think he have flatter they sufficiently . but on the point of know when we be disagreeable , our human nature be fallible . our lavender - water , our smile , our compliment , and other polite falsity , be constantly offensive , when in the very nature of they they can only be mean to attract admiration and regard . Jermyn have often be unconsciously disagreeable to Johnson , over and above the constant offence of be an ostentatious patron . he would never let Johnson dine with his wife and daughter ; he would not himself dine at Johnson 's house when he be in town . he often do what be equivalent to poohpoohing his conversation by not even appear to listen , and by suddenly cut it short with a query on a new subject . Jermyn be able and politic enough to have command a great deal of success in his life , but he could not help be handsome , arrogant , fond of be hear , indispose to any kind of comradeship , amorous and bland toward woman , cold and self - contain toward man . you will hear very strong denial that an attorney 's be handsome could enter into the dislike he excite ; but conversation consist a good deal in the denial of what be true . from the british point of view masculine beauty be regard very much as it be in the drapery business : as good solely for the fancy department for young nobleman , artist , poet , and the clergy . some one who , like Mr. Lingon , be disposed to revile Jermyn   perhaps it be Sir Maximus , have call he " a cursed , sleek , handsome , long - wind , overbear sycophant " ; epithet which express , rather confusedly , the mingled character of the dislike he excite . and serviceable John Johnson , himself sleek , and mindful about his broadcloth and his cambric front , have what he consider ' spirit " 249   enough within he to feel that dislike of Jermyn gradually gather force through year of obligation and subjection , till it have become an actuating motive dispose to use an opportunity ; if it do not watch for one . 

 it be not this motive , however , but rather the ordinary course of business , which account for Johnson 's play a double part as an electioneer agent . what man do in election be not to be class either among sin or mark of grace ; it would be profane to include business in religion , and conscience refer to failure , not to success . still , the sense of be gall by Jermyn 's harness be an additional reason for cultivate all relation that be independent of he ; and pique at Harold Transome 's behavior to he in Jermyn 's office perhaps give all the more zest to Johnson 's use of his pen and ink when he write a handbill in the service of Garstin , and Garstin 's incomparable agent , Putty , full of innuendo against Harold Transome , as a descendant of the Durfey - Transomes . it be a natural subject of self - congratulation to a man , when special knowledge , gain long ago without any forecast , turn out to afford a special inspiration in the present ; and Johnson feel a new pleasure in the consciousness that he of all people in the world next to Jermyn have the most intimate knowledge of the Transome affair . still well some of these affair be secret of Jermyn 's . if in an uncomplimentary spirit he might have be call Jermyn 's " man of straw , " it be a satisfaction to know that the unreality of the man John Johnson be confine to his appearance in annuity deed , and that elsewhere he be solid , locomotive , and capable of remember anything for his own pleasure and benefit . to act with doubleness towards a man whose conduct be double , be so near an approach to virtue that it deserve to be call by no mean name than diplomacy . 

 by such cause it come to pass that Christian hold in his hand a bill in which Jermyn be playfully allude to as Mr. German Cozen , who win game by clever shuffling and odd trick without any honor , and back Durfey 's crib against Bycliffe in which it be adroitly imply that the so - call head of the Transomes be only the tail of the Durfeys and that some say the Durfeys would have die out and leave their nest empty if it have not be for their German Cozen . 

 Johnson have not dare to use any recollection except such as might credibly exist in other mind besides his own . in the truth of the case , no one but himself have the prompting to recall these out - worn 250   scandal ; but it be likely enough that such foul - wing thing should be revive by election heat for Johnson to escape all suspicion . 

 Christian could gather only dim and uncertain inference from this flat irony and heavy joking ; but one chief thing be clear to he . he have be right in his conjecture that Jermyn 's interest about Bycliffe have its source in some claim of Bycliffe 's on the Transome property . and then , there be that story of the old bill - sticker 's , which , closely consider , indicate that the right of the present Transomes depend , or , at least , have depend on the continuance of some other life . christian in his time have gather enough legal notion to be aware that possession by one man sometimes depend on the life of another ; that a man might sell his own interest in property , and the interest of his descendant , while a claim on that property would still remain to some one else than the purchaser , suppose the descendant become extinct , and the interest they have sell be at an end . but under what condition the claim might be valid or void in any particular case , be all darkness to he . suppose Bycliffe have any such claim on the Transome estate : how be christian to know whether at the present moment it be worth anything more than a bit of rotten parchment ? Old Tommy Trounsem have say that Johnson know all about it . but even if Johnson be still above - ground and all johnson be mortal he might still be an understrapper of Jermyn 's , in which case his knowledge would be on the wrong side of the hedge for the purpose of Henry Scaddon . his immediate care must be to find out all he could about Johnson . he blame himself for not have question Tommy far while he have he at command ; but on this head the bill - sticker could hardly know more than the less dilapidated denizen of Treby . 

 now it have happen that during the week in which Christian have be at work try to solve the enigma of Jermyn 's interest about Bycliffe , Johnson 's mind also have be somewhat occupy with suspicion and conjecture as to new information on the subject of the old Bycliffe claim which Jermyn intend to conceal from he . the letter which , after his interview with Christian , Jermyn have write with a sense of perfect safety to his faithful ally Johnson , be , as we know , write to a Johnson who have find his self - love incompatible with that faithfulness of which it be suppose to be the foundation . anything that the patron feel it inconvenient for his oblige friend and 251   servant to know , become by that very fact an object of peculiar curiosity . the obliged friend and servant secretly dot on his patron 's inconvenience , provide that he himself do not share it ; and conjecture naturally become active . 

 Johnson 's legal imagination , be very differently furnish from Christian 's , be at no loss to conceive condition under which there might arise a new claim on the Transome estate . he have before he the whole history of the settlement of those estate make a hundred year ago by John Justus Transome , entail they , whilst in his possession , on his son Thomas and his heir - male , with remainder to the Bycliffes in fee . he know that Thomas , son of John Justus , prove a prodigal , have , without the knowledge of his father , the tenant in possession , sell his own and his descendant ' right to a lawyer - cousin name Durfey ; that , therefore , the title of the Durfey - Transomes , in spite of that old Durfey 's trick to show the contrary , depend solely on the purchase of the " base fee " thus create by Thomas Transome ; and that the Bycliffes be the " remainder man " who might fairly oust the Durfey - Transomes if ever the issue of the prodigal Thomas go clean out of existence , and cease to represent a right which he have bargain away from they . 

 Johnson , as Jermyn 's subordinate , have be closely cognizant of the detail concern the suit institute by successive Bycliffes , of whom Maurice Christian Bycliffe be the last , on the plea that the extinction of Thomas Transome 's line have actually come to pass a weary suit , which have eat into the fortune of two family , and have only make the cankerworm fat . the suit have close with the death of Maurice Christian Bycliffe in prison ; but before his death , Jermyn 's exertion to get evidence that there be still issue of Thomas Transome 's line surviving , as a security of the Durfey title , have issue in the discovery of a Thomas Transome at Littleshaw , in Stonyshire , who be the representative of the pawn inheritance . the death of Maurice have make this discovery useless have make it seem the wise part to say nothing about it ; and the fact have remain a secret know only to Jermyn and Johnson . no other Bycliffe be know or believe to exist , and the Durfey - Transomes might be consider safe , unless yes , there be an " unless " which Johnson could conceive : an heir or heiress of the Bycliffes if such a personage turn out to be in existence might sometime raise a new and valid claim when once inform that wretche old Tommy 252   Trounsem the bill - sticker , totter drunkenly on the edge of the grave , be the last issue remain above - ground from that dissolute Thomas who play his Esau part a century before . while the poor old bill - sticker breathe , the Durfey - Transomes could legally keep their possession in spite of a possible Bycliffe prove real ; but not when the parish have bury the bill - sticker . 

 still , it be one thing to conceive condition , and another to see any chance of prove their existence . Johnson at present have no glimpse of such a chance ; and even if he ever gain the glimpse , he be not sure that he should ever make any use of it . his enquiry of Medwin , in obedience to Jermyn 's letter , have extract only a negative as to any information possess by the lawyer of Bycliffe concern a marriage , or expectation of offspring on his part . but Johnson feel not the less sting by curiosity to know what Jermyn have find out : that he have find something in relation to a possible Bycliffe , Johnson feel pretty sure . and he think with satisfaction that Jermyn could not hinder he from know what he already know about Thomas Transome 's issue . many thing might occur to alter his policy and give a new value to fact . be it certain that Jermyn would always be fortunate ? 

 when greed and unscrupulousness exhibit themselves on a grand historical scale , and there be a question of peace or war or amicable partition , it often occur that gentleman of high diplomatic talent have their mind bent on the same object from different point of view . each , perhaps , be think of a certain duchy or province , with a view to arrange the ownership in such a way as shall well serve the purpose of the gentleman with high diplomatic talent in whom each be more especially interested . but these select mind in high office can never miss their aim from ignorance of each other 's existence or whereabouts . their high title may be learn even by common people from every pocket almanac . 

 but with mean diplomat , who might be mutually useful , such ignorance be often obstructive . Mr. John Johnson and Mr. Christian , otherwise Mr. Scaddon , might have have a concentration of purpose and an ingenuity of device fit they to make a figure in the parcelling of Europe , and yet they might never have meet , simply because Johnson know nothing of Christian , and because Christian do not know where to find Johnson .

 CHAPTER XXX . 

 his nature be too noble for the world : 
 he would not flatter Neptune for his trident , 
 or Jove for his power to thunder . his heart be his mouth ; 
 what his breast forge , that his tongue must vent ;

 he hear the name of death . Coriolanus . 
 Christian and Johnson do meet , however , by mean that be quite incalculable . the incident which bring they into communication be due to Felix Holt , who of all man in the world have the least affinity either for the industrious or the idle parasite . 

 Mr. Lyon have urge Felix to go to Duffield on the fifteenth of December to witness the nomination of the candidate for North Loamshire . the minister wish to hear what take place ; and the pleasure of gratify he help to outweigh some oppose reason . 

 " I shall get into a rage at something or other , " Felix have say . " I have   tell you one of my weak point . where I have any particular business , I must incur the risk my nature bring . but I have   no particular business at Duffield . however , I will make a holiday and go . by dint of see folly , I shall get lesson in patience . " 

 the weak point to which Felix refer be his liability to be carry completely out of his own mastery by indignant anger . his strong health , his renunciation of selfish claim , his habitual preoccupation with large thought and with purpose independent of everyday casualty , secure he a fine and even temper , free from moodiness or irritability . he be full of long - suffering toward his unwise mother , who " press he daily with her word and urge he , so that his soul be vex ; " he have choose to fill his day in a way that require the utmost exertion of patience , that require those little rill - like outflowing of goodness which in mind of great energy must be feed from deep source of thought and passionate devotedness . in this way his energy serve to make he gentle ; and now , in this twenty - sixth year of his life , they have cease to make he angry , except in the presence of something that rouse his deep indignation . when once exasperate , the passionateness of his nature throw off the yoke of a long - train consciousness in which thought and emotion have be more and more completely mingle , and concentrate itself in a rage as ungovernable as that of boyhood . he be thoroughly aware of the liability , and know that in such circumstance he could not answer for himself . sensitive people with feeble frame 254   have often the same sort of fury within they ; but they be themselves shatter , and shatter nothing . Felix have a terrible arm : he know that he be dangerous ; and he avoid the condition that might cause he exasperation as he would have avoid intoxicate drink if he have be in danger of intemperance . 

 the nomination - day be a great epoch of successful trickery , or , to speak in a more parliamentary manner , of war - stratagem , on the part of skilful agent . and Mr. Johnson have his share of inward chuckle and self - approval , as one who might justly expect increase renown , and be some day in as general request as the great Putty himself . to have the pleasure and the praise of electioneer ingenuity , and also to get pay for it , without too much anxiety whether the ingenuity will achieve its ultimate end , perhaps give to some select person a sort of satisfaction in their superiority to their more agitated fellow - man that be worthy to be class with those generous enjoyment of have the truth chiefly to yourself , and of see other in danger of drown while you be high and dry , which seem to have be regard as unmixe privilege by Lucretius and Lord Bacon . 

 one of Mr. Johnson 's great success be this . Spratt , the hat manager of the Sproxton Colliery , in careless confidence that the collier and other laborer under he would follow his order , have provide cart to carry some load of voteless enthusiasm to Duffield on behalf of Garstin ; enthusiasm which , be already pay for by the recognize benefit of Garstin 's existence as a capitalist with a share in the Sproxton mine , be not to cost much in the form of treat . a capitalist be hold worthy of pious honor as the cause why workingmen exist . but Mr. Spratt do not sufficiently consider that a cause which be to be prove by argument or testimony be not an object of passionate devotion to collier : a visible cause of beer act on they much more strongly . and even if there have be any love of the far - off Garstin , hatred of the too immediate Spratt would have be the strong motive . hence Johnson 's calculation , make long ago with Chubb , the remarkable publican , have be well found , and there have be diligent care to supply treat at Duffield in the name of Transome . after the election be over it be not improbable that there would be much friendly joke between Putty and Johnson as to the success of this trick against Putty 's employer , and Johnson would be conscious of rise in the opinion of his celebrated senior .

 for the show of hand and the cheering , the hustling and the pelting , the roaring and the hissing , the hard hit with small missile and the soft hit with small joke , be strong enough on the side of Transome to balance the similar " demonstration " for Garstin , even with the Debarry interest in his favor . and the inconvenient presence of Spratt be easily get rid of by a dexterously - manage accident , which send he bruise and limp from the scene of action . Mr. Chubb have never before feel so thoroughly that the occasion be up to a level with his talent , while the clear daylight in which his virtue would appear when at the election he vote , as his duty to himself bind he , for Garstin only , give he thorough repose of conscience . 

 Felix Holt be the only person look on at the senseless exhibition of this nomination - day , who know from the beginning the history of the trick with the Sproxton man . he have be aware all along that the treating at Chubb 's have be continue , and that so far Harold Transome 's promise have produce no good fruit ; and what he be observe to - day , as he watch the uproarious crowd , convince he that the whole scheme would be carry out just as if he have never speak about it . he could be fair enough to Transome to allow that he might have wish , and yet have be unable , with his notion of success , to keep his promise ; and his bitterness toward the candidate only take the form of contemptuous pity ; for Felix be not spar in his contempt for man who put their inward honor in pawn by seek the prize of the world . his scorn fall too readily on the fortunate . but when he see Johnson pass to and fro , and speak to Jermyn on the husting , he feel himself get angry , and jump off the wheel of the stationary cart on which he be mount , that he might no long be in sight of this man , whose vitiating can not have make his blood hot and his finger tingle on the first day of encounter he at Sproxton . it be a little too exasperating to look at this pink - faced rotund speciman of prosperity , to witness the power for evil that lie in his vulgar can not , back by another man 's money , and to know that such stupid iniquity flourish the flag of Reform , and Liberalism , and justice to the needy . while the roaring and the scuffling be still go on , Felix , with his thick stick in his hand , make his way through the crowd , and walk on through the Duffield street , till he come out on a grassy suburb , where the house surround a small common . here he walk 256   about in the breezy air , and eat his bread and apple , tell himself that this angry haste of his about evil that could only be remedie slowly , could be nothing else than obstructive , and might some day he see it so clearly that the thought seem like a presentiment be obstructive of his own work . 

 " not to waste energy , to apply force where it would tell , to do small work close at hand , not wait for speculative chance of heroism , but prepare for they " these be the rule he have be constantly urge on himself . but what could be a great waste than to beat a scoundrel who have law and opodeldoc at command ? after this meditation , Felix feel cool and wise enough to return into the town , not , however , intend to deny himself the satisfaction of a few pungent word wherever there be place for they . blow be sarcasm turn stupid : wit be a form of force that leave the limb at rest . 

 anything that could be call a crowd be no long to be see . the show of hand have be pronounce to be in favor of Debarry and Transome , and a poll have be demand for Garstin , the business of the day might be consider at an end . but in the street where the husting be erect , and where the great hotel stand , there be many group , as well as stroller and steady walker to and fro . man in superior greatcoat and well - brush hat be await with more or less impatience an important dinner , either at the Crown , which be Debarry 's house , or at the Three Cranes , which be Garstin 's , or at the Fox and Hounds , which be Transome 's . knot of sober retailer , who have already din , be to be see at some shop - door ; man in very shabby coat and miscellaneous head - covering , inhabitant of Duffield and not county voter , be lounge about in dull silence , or listen , some to a grimy man in a flannel shirt , hatless and with turbid red hair , who be insist on political point with much more ease than have seem to belong to the gentleman speaker on the husting , and other to a Scotch vendor of article useful to sell , whose unfamiliar accent seem to have a guarantee of truth in it want as an association with everyday English . some rough - look pipe - smoker , or distinguish cigar - smoker , choose to walk up and down in isolation and silence . but the majority of those who have show a burn interest in the nomination have disappear , and cockade no long stud a close - press crowd , like , and also very unlike , meadow - flower among the grass . the street 257   pavement be strangely paint with fragment of perishable missile grind flat under heavy foot : but the worker be rest from their toil , and the buzz and tread and the fitfully discernible voice seem like stillness to Felix after the roar with which the wide space have be fill when he leave it . 

 the group round the speaker in the flannel shirt stand at the corner of a side - street , and the speaker himself be elevate by the head and shoulder above his hearer , not because he be tall , but because he stand on a project stone . at the opposite corner of the turning be the great inn of the Fox and Hounds , and this be the ultra - liberal quarter of the high street . Felix be at once attract by this group ; he like the look of the speaker , whose bare arm be powerfully muscular , though he have the pallid complexion of a man who live chiefly amidst the heat of furnace . he be lean against the dark stone building behind he with fold arm , the grimy paleness of his shirt and skin stand out in high relief against the dark stone building behind he . he lift up one forefinger , and mark his emphasis with it as he speak . his voice be high and not strong , but Felix recognize the fluency and the method of an habitual preacher or lecturer . 

 " it be the fallacy of all monopolist , " he be say . " we know what monopolist be : man who want to keep a trade all to themselves , under the pretence that they will furnish the public with a well article . we know what that come to : in some country a poor man can not   afford to buy a spoonful of salt , and yet there be salt enough in the world to pickle every live thing in it . that be the sort of benefit monopolist do to mankind . and these be the man who tell we we be to let politic alone ; they will govern we well without our know anything about it . we must mind our business ; we be ignorant ; we have   no time to study great question . but I tell they this : the great question in the world be , how to give every man a man 's share in what go on in life " 

 " hear , hear ! " say Felix in his sonorous voice , which seem to give a new impressiveness to what the speaker have say . every one look at he : the well - wash face and its educate expression along with a dress more careless than that of most well - to - do workman on a holiday , make his appearance strangely arrest . 

 " not a pig 's share , " the speaker go on , " not a horse 's , not the share of a machine feed with oil only to make it work and nothing else . it 258   be not   a man 's share just to mind your pin - making , or your glass - blowing , and higgle about your own wage , and bring up your family to be ignorant son of ignorant father , and no well prospect ; that be a slave 's share ; we want a freeman 's share , and that be to think and speak and act about what concern we all , and see whether these fine gentleman who undertake to govern we be do the good they can for we . they have   get the knowledge , say they . very well , we have   get the want . there be many a one would be idle if hunger do not   pinch he ; but the stomach set we to work . there be a fable tell where the noble be the belly and the people the member . but I make another sort of fable . I say , we be the belly that feel the pinch , and we will set these aristocrat , these great people who call themselves our brain , to work at some way of satisfy we a bit well . the aristocrat be pretty sure to try and govern for their own benefit ; but how be we to be sure they will try and govern for our ? they must be look after , I think , like other workman . we must have what we call inspector , to see whether the work 's well do for we . we want to send our inspector to Parliament . well , they say you have   get the Reform Bill ; what more can you want ? send your inspector . but I say , the Reform Bill be a trick it be nothing but swear - in special constable to keep the aristocrat safe in their monopoly ; it be bribe some of the people with vote to make they hold their tongue about give vote to the rest . I say , if a man do not   beg or steal , but work for his bread , the poor and the more miserable he be , the more he would need have a vote to send an inspector to Parliament else the man who be bad off be likely to be forget ; and I say , he be the man who ought to be first remember . else what do their religion mean ? why do they build church and endow they that their son may get pay well for preach a Saviour , and make themselves as little like he as can be ? if I want to believe in Jesus Christ , I must shut my eye for fear I should see a parson . and what be a bishop ? a bishop 's a person dress up , who sit in the House of Lords to help and throw out Reform Bills . and because it be hard to get anything in the shape of a man to dress himself up like that , and do such work , they have to give he a palace for it , and plenty of thousand a - year . and then they cry out ' the Church be in danger , ' ' the poor man 's Church . ' and why be it the poor man 's Church ? because he can have a seat for nothing . I think it be for nothing ; for it would be hard to tell what he get by it . if the 259   poor man have a vote in the matter , I think he would choose a different sort of church to what that be . but do you think the aristocrat will ever alter it , if the belly do not   pinch they ? not they . it be part of their monopoly . they will supply we with our religion like everything else , and get a profit on it . they will give we plenty of heaven . we may have land there . that be the sort of religion they like a religion that give we workingmen heaven , and nothing else . but we will offer to change with they . we will give they back some of their heaven , and take it out in something for we and our child in this world . they do not   seem to care so much about heaven themselves till they feel the gout very bad ; but you will not   get they to give up anything else , if you do not   pinch them for it . and to pinch they enough , we must get the suffrage , we must get vote , that we may send the man to Parliament who will do our work for we ; and we must have Parliament dissolve every year , that we may change our man if he do not   do what we want he to do ; and we must have the country divide so that the little king of the county can not   do as they like , but must be shake up in one bag with we . I say , if we workingman be ever to get a man 's share , we must have universal suffrage , and annual Parliaments , and the vote by ballot , and electoral district . " 

 " no ! something else before all that , " say Felix , again startle the audience into look at he . but the speaker glance coldly at he and go on . 

 " that be what Sir Francis Burdett go in for fifteen year ago ; and it be the right thing for we , if it be Tomfool who go in for it . you must lay hold of such handle as you can . I do not   believe much in liberal aristocrat ; but if there be any fine carve gold - head stick of an aristocrat will make a broomstick of himself , I will lose no time but I will sweep with he . and that be what I think about Transome . and if any of you have acquaintance among county voter , give them a hint that you wish them to vote for Transome . " 

 at the last word , the speaker step down from his slight eminence , and walk away rapidly , like a man whose leisure be exhaust , and who must go about his business . but he have leave an appetite in his audience for further oratory , and one of they seem to express a general sentiment as he hurry immediately to Felix , and say , " come , sir , what do you say ?  Felix do at once what he would very likely have do without be ask he step on the stone , and take off his cap by an instinctive prompting that always lead he to speak uncover . the effect of his figure in relief against the stone background be unlike that of the previous speaker . he be considerably tall , his head and neck be more massive , and the expression of his mouth and eye be something very different from the mere acuteness and rather hard - lipped antagonism of the trade - union man . Felix Holt 's face have the look of habitual meditative abstraction from object of mere personal vanity or desire , which be the peculiar stamp of culture , and make a very roughly - cut face worthy to be call " the human face divine . " even lion and dog know a distinction between man 's glance ; and doubtless those Duffield man , in the expectation with which they look up at Felix , be unconsciously influence by the grandeur of his full yet firm mouth , and the calm clearness of his gray eye , which be somehow unlike what they be accustom to see along with an old brown velveteen coat , and an absence of chin - propping . when he begin to speak , the contrast of voice be still strong than that of appearance . the man in the flannel shirt have not be hear have probably not care to be hear beyond the immediate group of listener . but Felix at once draw the attention of person comparatively at a distance . 

 " in my opinion , " he say , almost the moment after he be address , " that be a true word speak by your friend when he say the great question be how to give every man a man 's share in life . but I think he expect voting to do more toward it than I do . I want the workingmen to have power . I be a workingman myself , and I do not   want to be anything else . but there be two sort of power . there be a power to do mischief to undo what have be do with great expense and labor , to waste and destroy , to be cruel to the weak , to lie and quarrel , and to talk poisonous nonsense . that be the sort of power that ignorant number have . it never make a joint stool or plant a potato . do you think it be likely to do much toward govern a great country , and make wise law , and give shelter , food , and clothe to million of man ? ignorant power come in the end to the same thing as wicked power ; it make misery . it be another sort of power that I want we workingman to have , and I can see plainly enough that our all have vote will do little toward it at present . I hope we , or the child that come after we , will get plenty of political power some time . I tell everybody plainly , I hope there will be great change , and that some time , whether we 261   live to see it or not , man will have come to be ashamed of thing they be proud of now . but I should like to convince you that vote would never give you political power worth have while thing be as they be now , and that if you go the right way to work you may get power soon without vote . perhaps all you who hear I be sober man , who try to learn as much of the nature of thing as you can , and to be as little like fool as possible . a fool or idiot be one who expect thing to happen that never can happen ; he pour milk into a can without a bottom , and expect the milk to stay there . the more of such vain expectation a man have , the more he be a fool or idiot . and if any work man expect a vote to do for he what it never can do , he be foolish to that amount , if no more . I think that be clear enough , eh ? " 

 " hear , hear , " say several voice , but they be not those of the original group ; they belong to some stroller who have be attract by Felix Holt 's vibrate voice , and be Tories from the Crown . among they be Christian , who be smoke a cigar with a pleasure he always feel in be among people who do not know he , and doubtless take he to be something high than he really be . hearer from the Fox and Hounds also be slowly add themselves to the nucleus . Felix , accessible to the pleasure of be listen to , go on with more and more animation : " the way to get rid of folly be to get rid of vain expectation , and of thought that do not   agree with the nature of thing . the man who have have true thought about water , and what it will do when it be turn into steam and under all sort of circumstance , have make themselves a great power in the world : they be turn the wheel of engine that will help to change most thing . but no engine would have do , if there have be false notion about the way water would act . now , all the scheme about voting , and district , and annual Parliaments , and the rest , be engine , and the water or steam the force that be to work they must come out of human nature out of man 's passion , feeling , desire . whether the engine will do good work or bad depend on these feeling ; and if we have false expectation about man 's character , we be very much like the idiot who think he will carry milk in a can without a bottom . in my opinion , the notion about what mere voting will do are very much of that sort . "

 " that be very fine , " say a man in dirty fustian , with a scornful laugh . " but how be we to get the power without vote ? " 

 " I will tell you what be the great power under heaven , " say Felix , " and that be public opinion the rule belief in society about what be right and what be wrong , what be honorable and what be shameful . that be the steam that be to work the engine . how can political freedom make we well , any more than a religion we do not   believe in , if people laugh and wink when they see man abuse and defile it ? and while public opinion be what it be while man have no well belief about public duty while corruption be not feel to be a damn disgrace while man be not ashamed in Parliament and out of it to make public question which concern the welfare of million a mere screen for their own petty private end , I say , no fresh scheme of voting will much mend our condition . for , take we workingmen of all sort . suppose out of every hundred who have a vote there be thirty who have some soberness , some sense to choose with , some good feeling to make they wish the right thing for all . and suppose there be seventy out of the hundred who be , half of they , not sober , who have no sense to choose one thing in politic more than another , and who have so little good feeling in they that they waste on their own drink the money that should have help to feed and clothe their wife and child ; and another half of they who , if they do not   drink , be too ignorant or stupid to see any good for themselves well than pocket a five - shilling piece when it be offer they . where would be the political power of the thirty sober man ? the power would lie with the seventy drunken and stupid vote ; and I will tell you what sort of man would get the power what sort of man would end by return whom they please to Parliament . " 

 Felix have see every face around he , and have particularly notice a recent addition to his audience ; but now he look about he , without appear to fix his glance on any one . in spite of his cool meditation an hour ago , his pulse be get quicken by indignation , and the desire to crush what he hat be likely to vent itself in articulation . his tone become more biting . 

 " they would be man who would undertake to do the business for a candidate , and return he : man who have no real opinion , but who pilfer the word of every opinion , and turn they into a ca not which will serve their purpose at the moment ; man who look out for dirty work to make their fortune by , because dirty work want little talent and no 263   conscience ; man who know all the in and out of bribery , because there be not a cranny in their own soul where a bribe can not   enter . such man as these will be the master wherever there be a majority of voter who care more for money , for drink , more for some mean little end which be their own and nobody else 's , than for anything that have ever be call right in the world . for suppose there be a poor voter name Jack , who have seven child , and twelve or fifteen shilling a - week wage , perhaps less . Jack can not   read I do not   say whose fault that be he never have the chance to learn ; he know so little that he perhaps think God make the poor - law , and if anybody say the pattern of the work - house be lay down in the Testament , he would not   be able to contradict they . what be poor Jack likely to do when he see a smart stranger come to he , who happen to be just one of these man that I say will be the master till public opinion get too hot for they ? he be a middle - sized man , we will say ; stout , with coat upon coat of fine broadcloth , open enough to show a fine gold chain : none of your dark , scowl man , but one with an innocent pink - and - white skin and very smooth light hair a most respectable man , who call himself a good , sound , well - know english name as Green , or Baker , or Wilson , or let we say , Johnson " 

 Felix be interrupt by an explosion of laughter from a majority of the bystander . some eye have be turn on Johnson , who stand on the right hand of Felix , at the very beginning of the description , and these be gradually follow by other , till at last every hearer 's attention be fix on he , and the first burst of laughter from the two or three who know the attorney 's name , let every one sufficiently into the secret to make the amusement common . Johnson , who have keep his ground till his name be mention , now turn away , look unusually white after be unusually red , and feeling by an attorney 's instinct for his pocket - book , as if he feel it be a case for take down the name of witness . 

 all the well - dress hearer turn away too , think they have have the cream of the speech in the joke against Johnson , which , as a thing worth tell , help to recall they to the scene of dinner . 

 " who be this Johnson ? " say Christian to a young man who have be stand near he , and have be one of the first to laugh . Christian 's curiosity have naturally be awaken by what might prove a golden opportunity .

 " oh a London attorney . he act for Transome . that tremendous fellow at the corner there be some red - hot radical demagogue , and Johnson have offend he , I suppose ; else he would not   have turn in that way on a man of their own party . " 

 " I have hear there be a Johnson who be an understrapper of Jermyn 's , " say Christian . 

 " well , so this man may have be for what I know . but he be a London man now a very busy fellow on his own leg in Bedford Row . ha , ha ! it be capital , though , when these liberal get a slap in the face from the workingmen they be so very fond of . " 

 another turn along the street enable Christian to come to a resolution . have see Jermyn drive away an hour before , he be in no fear : he walk at once to the Fox and Hounds and ask to speak to Mr. Johnson . a brief interview , in which Christian ascertain that he have before he the Johnson mention by the bill - sticker , issue in the appointment of a long one at a later hour ; and before they leave Duffield they have come not exactly to a mutual understanding , but to an exchange of information mutually welcome . 

 Christian have be very cautious in the commencement , only intimate that he know something important which some chance hint have induce he to think might be interesting to Mr. Johnson , but that this entirely depend on how far he have a common interest with Mr. Jermyn . Johnson reply that he have much business in which that gentleman be not concern , but that to a certain extent they have a common interest . probably then , Christian observe , the affair of the Transome estate be part of the business in which Mr. Jermyn and Mr. Johnson might be understand to represent each other , in which case he need not detain Mr. Johnson ? at this hint Johnson could not conceal that he be become eager . he have no idea what Christian 's information be , but there be many ground on which Johnson desire to know as much as he could about the Transome affair independently of Jermyn . by little and little an understanding be arrive at . Christian tell of his interview with Tommy Trounsem , and state that if Johnson could show he whether the knowledge could have any legal value , he could bring evidence that a legitimate child of Bycliffe 's exist : he feel certain of his fact , and of his proof . Johnson explain , that in this case the death of the old bill - sticker would give the child the first valid claim to the 265   Bycliffe heirship ; that for his own part he should be glad to further a true claim , but that caution would have to be observe . how do Christian know that Jermyn , be inform on this subject ? christian , more and more convinced that Johnson would be glad to counteract Jermyn at length become explicit about Esther , but still withhold his own real name , and the nature of his relation with Bycliffe . he say he would bring the rest of his information when Mr. Johnson take the case up seriously , and place it in the hand of Bycliffe 's old lawyer of course he would do that ? Johnson reply that he would certainly do that ; but that there be legal nicety which Mr. Christian be probably not acquaint with ; that Esther 's claim have not yet accrue , and that hurry be useless . 

 the two man part , each in distrust of the other , but each well please to have learn something . Johnson be not at all sure how he should act , but think it likely that event would soon guide he . Christian be begin to meditate a way of secure his own end without depend in the least on Johnson 's procedure . it be enough for he that he be now assure of Esther 's legal claim on the Transome estate . 

 CHAPTER XXXI . 

 " in the copia of the factious language the word Tory be entertain , and be a vocal clever - sound word , readily pronounce , it keep its hold , and take possession of the foul mouth of the faction . the Loyalists begin to cheer up and to take heart of grace , and in the working of this crisis , accord to the common law of scold , they consider which way to make payment for so much of Tory as they have be treat with to clear score . immediately the train take , and run like wildfire and become general . and so the account of Tory be balance , and soon begin to run up a sharp score on the other side . " North 's Examen , p. 321 . 

 at last the great epoch of the election for North Loamshire have arrive . the road approach Treby be early traverse by a large number of vehicle , horseman , and also foot - passenger than be ever see at the annual fair . Treby be the polling - place for many voter whose face be quite strange in the town ; and if there be some stranger who do not come to poll , though they have business not unconnected with the election , they be not liable to be regard with suspicion or especial curiosity . it be understand that no division of a county have ever be more thoroughly canvass , and that there would be a hard run between Garstin and Transome . Mr. Johnson 's headquarters be at Duffield ; but it be a maxim which he repeat after the great Putty , that a capable agent make himself omnipresent ; and quite apart from the express between he and Jermyn , Mr. John Johnson 's presence in the universe 266   have potent effect on this December day at Treby Magna . 

 a slight drizzling rain which be observe by some Tories who look out of their bedroom window before six o'clock , make they hope that , after all , the day might pass off well than alarmist have expect . the rain be feel to be somehow on the side of quiet and Conservatism ; but soon the breaking of the cloud and the mild gleam of a December sun bring back previous apprehension . as there be already precedent for riot at a Reformed election , and as the Trebian district have have its confidence in the natural course of thing somewhat shake by a land proprietor with an old name offer himself as a radical candidate , the election have be look forward to by many with a vague sense that it would be an occasion something like a fighting match , when bad character would probably assemble , and there might be struggle and alarm for respectable man , which would make it expedient for they to take a little neat brandy as a precaution beforehand and a restorative afterward . the tenant on the Transome estate be comparatively fearless : poor Mr. Goffe , of Rabbit 's end , consider that " one thing be as maul as the other , " and that an election be no bad than the sheep - rot ; while Mr. Dibbs , take the more cheerful view of a prosperous man , reflect that if the Radicals be dangerous , it be safe to be on their side . it be the voter for Debarry and Garstin who consider that they alone have the right to regard themselves as target for evil - minded man ; and Mr. Crowder , if he could have get his idea countenance , would have recommend a muster of farm - servant with defensive pitchfork on the side of church and king . but the bold man be rather gratify by the prospect of be groan at , so that they might face about and groan in return . 

 Mr. Crow , the high constable of Treby , inwardly rehearse a brief address to a riotous crowd in case it should be want , have be warn by the rector that it be a primary duty on these occasion to keep a watch against provocation as well as violence . the rector , with a brother magistrate who be on the spot , have think it desirable to swear in some special constable , but the presence of loyal man not absolutely require for the polling be not look at in the light of a provocation . the Benefit Clubs from various quarter make a show , some with the orange - color ribbon and streamer of the true Tory candidate , some with the mazarine of the Whig . the orange - color 267   band play " Auld Lang Syne , " and a loud mazarine band come across they with " oh , whistle and I will come to thee , my lad " probably as the tune the most symbolical of Liberalism which their repertory would furnish . there be not a single club bear the radical blue : the Sproxton Club member wear the mazarine , and Mr. Chubb wear so much of it that he look   at a sufficient distance   like a very large gentianella . it be generally understand that " these brave fellow , " represent the fine institution of Benefit Clubs , hold aloft the motto , " let brotherly love continue , " be a civil force calculate to encourage voter of sound opinion and keep up their spirit . but a considerable number of unadorned heavy navvy , collier , and stone - pit man , who use their freedom as british subject to be present in Treby on this great occasion , look like a possible uncivil force whose politic be dubious until it be clearly see for whom they cheer and for whom they groan . 

 thus the way up to the polling - booth be variously line , and those who walk it , to whatever side they belong , have the advantage of hear from the opposite side what be the most marked defect or excess in their personal appearance ; for the Trebians of that day hold , without be aware that they have Cicero 's authority for it , that the bodily blemish of an opponent be a legitimate ground for ridicule ; but if the voter frustrate wit by be handsome , he be groan at and satirize accord to a formula , in which the adjective be Tory , Whig , or Radical , as the case might be , and the substantive a blank to be fill up after the taste of the speaker . 

 some of the more timid have choose to go through this ordeal as early as possible in the morning . one of the early be Mr. Timothy Rose , the gentleman - farmer from Leek Malton . he have leave home with some foreboding , have swathe his more vital part in layer of flannel , and put on two greatcoat as a soft kind of armor . but reflect with some trepidation that there be no resource for protect his head , he once more waver in his intention to vote ; he once more observed to Mrs. Rose that these be hard time when a man of independent property be expect to vote " willy - nilly ; " but finally coerce by the sense that he should be look ill on " in these time " if he do not stand by the gentleman round about , he set out in his gig , take with he a powerful wagoner , whom he order to keep he in sight as he go to 268   the polling - booth . it be hardly more than nine o'clock when Mr. Rose , have thus come up to the level of his time , cheer himself with a little cherry - brandy at the Marquis , drive away in a much more courageous spirit , and get down at Mr. Nolan 's , just outside the town . the retired Londoner , he consider , be a man of experience , who would estimate properly the judicious course he have take , and could make it know to other . Mr. Nolan be superintend the removal of some shrub in his garden . 

 " well , Mr. Nolan , " say Rose , twinkle a self - complacent look over the red prominence of his cheek , " have you be to give your vote yet ? " 

 " no ; all in good time . I shall go presently . " 

 " well , I would not   lose an hour , I would not . I say to myself , if I have   get to do gentleman a favor , I will do it at once . you see , I have   get no landlord , Nolan I be in that position o ' life that I can be independent . " 

 " just so , my dear sir , " say the wiry - faced Nolan , pinch his under - lip between his thumb and finger , and give one of those wonderful universal shrug , by which he seem to be recall all his garment from a tendency to disperse themselves . " come in and see Mrs. Nolan ? " 

 " no , no , thankye . Mrs. Rose expect I back . but , as I be say , I be a independent man , and I consider it be not my part to show favor to one more than another , but to make thing as even as I can . if I have be a tenant to anybody , well , in course I must have vote for my landlord that stand to sense . but I wish everybody well ; and if one be return to Parliament more than another , nobody can say it be my doing ; for when you can vote for two , you can make thing even . so I give one to Debarry and one to Transome ; and I wish Garstin no ill , but I can not   help the odd number , and he hang on to Debarry , they say . " 

 " God bless I , sir , " say Mr. Nolan , cough down a laugh , " do not   you perceive that you might as well have stay at home and not vote at all , unless you would rather send a Radical to Parliament than a sober Whig ? " 

 " well , I be sorry you should have anything to say against what I have   do , Nolan , " say Mr. Rose , rather crestfallen , though sustain by inward warmth . " I think you would agree with I , as you be a sensible man . but the most a independent man can do be to try and please all ; and if he have not   the luck here be wish I may do it another time , " add Mr. Rose , apparently confound a toast with a salutation , for he put out 269   his hand for a pass shake , and then step into his gig again . 

 at the time that Mr. Timothy Rose leave the town , the crowd in King Street and in the market - place , where the polling - booth stand , be fluctuate . voter as yet be scanty , and brave fellow who have come from any distance this morning , or who have sit up late drink the night before , require some reinforcement of their strength and spirit . every public house in Treby , not except the venerable and sombre Cross - Keys , be lively with change and numerous company . not , of course , that there be any treating : treat necessarily have stop , from moral scruple , when once " the wit be out ; " but there be drinking , which do equally well under any name . 

 Poor Tommy Trounsem , breakfast here on Falstaff 's proportion of bread , and something which , for gentility 's sake , I will call sack , be more than usually victorious over the ill of life , and feel himself one of the hero of the day . he have an immense light - blue cockade in his hat , and an amount of silver in a dirty little canvas bag which astonish himself . for some reason , at first inscrutable to he , he have be pay for his bill - stick with great liberality at Mr. Jermyn 's office , in spite of his have be the victim of a trick by which he have once lose his own bill and paste up Debarry 's ; but he soon see that this be simply a recognition of his merit as " an old family keep out of its right , " and also of his peculiar share in an occasion when the family be to get into Parliament . under these circumstance , it be due from he that he should show himself prominently where business be go forward , and give additional value by his presence to every vote for Transome . with this view he get a half - pint bottle fill with his peculiar kind of ' sack , " and hasten back to the market - place , feel good - natured and patronize toward all political party , and only so far partial as his family bind he to be . 

 but a disposition to concentrate at that extremity of King Street which issue in the market - place , be not universal among the increase crowd . some of they seem attract toward another nucleus at the other extremity of King Street , near the Seven Stars . this be Garstin 's chief house , where his committee sit , and it be also a point which must necessarily be pass by many voter enter the town on the eastern side . it seem natural that the mazarine color should be visible 270   here , and that Pack , the tall ' shepherd " of the Sproxton man , should be see move to and fro where there would be a frequent opportunity of cheer the voter for a gentleman who have the chief share in the Sproxton mine . but the side lane and entry out of King Street be numerous enough to relieve any pressure if there be need to make way . the lane have a distinguished reputation . two of they have odor of brewing ; one have a side entrance to Mr. Tiliot 's wine and spirit vault ; up another Mr. Muscat 's cheese be frequently be unload ; and even some of the entry have those cheerful suggestion of plentiful provision which be among the characteristic of Treby . 

 between ten and eleven the voter come in more rapid succession , and the whole scene become spirited . cheer , sarcasm , and oath , which seem to have a flavor of wit for many hearer , be begin to be reinforce by more practical demonstration , dubiously jocose . there be a disposition in the crowd to close and hem in the way for voter , either go or come , until they have pay some kind of toll . it be difficult to see who set the example in the transition from word to deed . some think it be due to Jacob Cuff , a Tory charity - man , who be a well - know ornament of the pothouse , and give his mind much leisure for amusing device ; but question of origination in stir period be notoriously hard to settle . it be by no mean necessary in human thing that there should be only one beginner . this , however , be certain that Mr. Chubb , who wish it to be notice that he vote for Garstin solely , be one of the first to get rather more notice than he wish , and that he have his hat knock off and crush in the interest of Debarry by Tories oppose to coalition . on the other hand , some say it be at the same time that Mr. Pink , the saddler , be stop on his way and make to declare that he be go to vote for debarry , get himself well chalk as to his coat , and push up an entry , where he remain the prisoner of terror combine with the want of any back outlet , and never give his vote that day . 

 the second Tory joke be perform with much gusto . the majority of the Transome tenant come in a body from the Ram Inn , with Mr. Banks , the bailiff , lead they . Poor Goffe be the last of they , and his worn melancholy look and forward - lean gait give the jocose Cuff the notion that the farmer be not what he call " compus . " Mr. Goffe be cut off from his companion and hem in : ask , by voice with hot breath 271   close to his ear , how many horse he have , how many cow , how many fat pig ; then jostle from one to another , who make trumpet with their hand , and deafen he by tell he to vote for Debarry . in this way the melancholy Goffe be hustle on till he be at the polling - booth , fill with confused alarm , the immediate alarm be that of have to go back in still bad fashion than he have come . arrive in this way after the other tenant have leave , he astonish all hearer who know he for a tenant of the Transomes by say " debarry , " and be jostle back trembling amid shout of laughter . 

 by stage of this kind the fun grow fast , and be in danger of get rather serious . the Tories begin to feel that their joke be return by other of a heavy sort , and that the main strength of the crowd be not on the side of sound opinion , but might come to be on the side of sound cudgel and kicking . the navvy and pitman in dishabille seem to be multiply , and to be clearly not belong to the party of Order . the shop be freely resort to for various form of playful missile and weapon ; and news come to the magistrate , watch from the large window of the Marquis , that a gentleman come in on horseback at the other end of the street to vote for Garstin have have his horse turn round and frighten into a headlong gallop out of it again . 

 Mr. Crow and his subordinate , and all the special constable , feel that it be necessary to make some energetic effort , or else every voter would be intimidate and the poll must be adjourn . the rector determine to get on horseback and go amidst the crowd with the constable ; and he send a message to Mr. Lingon , who be at the Ram , call on he to do the same . " Sporting Jack " be sure the good fellow mean no harm , but he be courageous enough to face any bodily danger , and ride out in his brown legging and colored bandana , speak persuasively . 

 it be nearly twelve o'clock when this sally be make : the constable and magistrate try the most pacific measure , and they seem to succeed . there be a rapid thinning of the crowd : the most boisterous disappear , or seem to do so by become quiet ; missile cease to fly , and a sufficient way be clear for voter along King Street . the magistrate return to their quarter , and the constable take convenient post of observation . Mr. Wace , who be one of Debarry 's committee , have suggest to the rector that it might be wise to send 272   for the military from Duffield , with order that they should station themselves at Hathercote , three mile off : there be so much property in the town that it would be well to make it secure against risk . but the rector feel that this be not the part of a moderate and wise magistrate , unless the sign of riot recur . he be a brave man , and fond of think that his own authority suffice for the maintenance of the general good in Treby . 

 CHAPTER XXXII . 

 go from I . yet I feel that I shall stand 
 Henceforward in thy shadow . nevermore 
 alone upon the threshold of my door 
 of individual life . I shall command 
 the us of my soul , nor lift my hand 
 Serenely in the sunshine as before 
 without the sense of that which I forbear 
 Thy touch upon the palm . the wide land 
 Doom take to part we , leave thy heart in mine 
 with pulse that beat double . what I do 
 and what I dream include thee , as the wine 
 must taste of its own grape . and when I sue 
 God for myself , he hear that name of thine , 
 and see within my eye the tear of two . 
  Mrs. Browning . 
 Felix Holt , seat at his work without his pupil , who have ask for a holiday with a notion that the wooden booth promise some sort of show , notice about eleven o'clock that the noise which reach he from the main street be get more and more tumultuous . he have long see bad augury for this election , but , like all people who dread the prophetic wisdom that end in desire the fulfillment of its own evil foreboding , he have check himself with remember that , though many condition be possible which might bring on violence , there be just as many which might avert it . there would , perhaps , be no other mischief than what he be already certain of . with these thought he sit down quietly to his work , mean not to vex his soul by go to look on at thing he would fain have make different if he could . but he be of a fiber that vibrate too strongly to the life around he to shut himself away in quiet , even from suffer and irremediable wrong . as the noise grow louder , and work more and more strongly on his imagination , he be oblige to lay down his delicate wheel - work . his mother come from her turnip - paring , in the kitchen , where little Job be her companion , to observe that they must be kill everybody in the High Street , and that the election , which have never be before at Treby , must have come for a judgment ; that there be mercy where you do not   look for 273   they , and that she thank God in his wisdom for make she live up a back street . 

 Felix snatch his cap and rush out . but when he get to the turning into the market - place the magistrate be already on horseback there , the constable be move about , and Felix observe that there be no strong spirit of resistance to they . he stay long enough to see the partial dispersion of the crowd and the restoration of tolerable quiet , and then go back to Mrs. Holt to tell she that there be nothing to fear now ; he be go out again , and she must not be in any anxiety at his absence . she might set by his dinner for he . 

 Felix have be think of Esther and her probable alarm at the noise that must have reach she more distinctly than they have reach he , for Malthouse Yard be remove but a little way from the main street . Mr. Lyon be away from home , have be call to preach charity sermon and attend meeting in a distant town ; and Esther , with the plaintive Lyddy for her sole companion , be not cheerfully circumstanced . Felix have not be to see she yet since her father 's departure , but to - day he give way to new reason . 

 " Miss Esther be in the garret , " Lyddy say , try to see what be go on . but before she be fetched she come run down the stair , draw by the knock at the door , which have shake the small dwelling . 

 " I be so thankful to see you , " she say , eagerly . " pray come in . " 

 when she have shut the parlor door behind they , Felix say , " I suspect that you might have be make anxious by the noise . I come to tell you that thing be quiet now . though , indeed , you can hear that they be . " 

 " I be frightened , " say Esther . " the shouting and roaring of rude man be so hideous . it be a relief to I that my father be not at home that he be out of the reach of any danger he might have fall into if he have be here . but I give you credit for be in the midst of the danger , " she add , smile , with a determination not to show much feeling . " sit down and tell I what have happen . " 

 they sit down at the extremity of the old black sofa , and Felix say 

 " to tell you the truth , I have shut myself up , and try to be as indifferent to the election as if I have be one of the fish in the Lapp , till the noise get too strong for I . but I only see the tail end of the disturbance . the poor noisy simpleton seem to give way before the magistrate and the constable . I hope nobody have be much hurt . 274   the fear be that they may turn out again by - and - by ; their give way so soon may not be altogether a good sign . there be a great number of heavy fellow in the town . if they go and drink more , the last end may be bad than the first . however " 

 Felix break off , as if this talk be futile , clasp his hand behind his head , and , lean backward , look at Esther , who be look at he . 

 " may I stay here a little while ? " he say , after a moment , which seem long . 

 " pray do , " say Esther , color . to relieve herself she take some work and bow her head over her stitch . it be in reality a little heaven to she that Felix be there , but she see beyond it see that by - and - by he would be go , and that they should be far on their way , not toward meeting , but parting . his will be impregnable . he be a rock , and she be no more to he than the white cling mist - cloud . 

 " I wish I could be sure that you see thing just as I do , " he say abruptly , after a minute 's silence . 

 " I be sure you see they much more wisely than I do ! " say Esther , almost bitterly , without look up . 

 " there be some people one must wish to judge truly . not to wish it would be mere hardness . I know you think I be a man without feel at least , without strong affection . you think I love nothing but my own resolution . " 

 " suppose I reply in the same sort of strain ? " say Esther , with a little toss of the head . 

 " how ? " 

 " why , that you think I a shallow woman , incapable of believe what be good in you , set down everything that be too high for I as a deficiency . " 

 " do not   parry what I say . answer I . " there be an expression of painful beseech in the tone with which Felix say this . Esther let she work fall on her lap and look at he , but she be unable to speak . 

 " I want you to tell I once that you know it would be easy to I to give myself up to love and be love , as other man do , when they can , than to " 

 this breaking - off in speech be something quite new in Felix . for the first time he have lose his self - possession , and turn his eye away . he be at variance with himself . he have begin what he feel he ought not to finish . 

 Esther , like a woman as she be a woman wait for love , never able to ask for it have her joy in these sign of her power ; but they 275   make her generous , not chary , as they might have do if she have have a pettier disposition . she say , with deep yet timid earnestness 

 " what you have choose to do have only convince I that your love would be the well worth have . " 

 all the fine part of Esther 's nature tremble in those word . to be right in great memorable moment be perhaps the thing we need most desire for ourselves . 

 Felix as quick as lightning turn his look upon she again , and , lean forward , take her sweet hand and hold it to his lip some moment before he let it fall again and raise his head . 

 " we shall always be the well for thinking of each other , " he say , lean his elbow on the back of the sofa , and support his head as he look at she with calm sadness . " this thing can never come to I twice over . it be my knighthood . that be always a business of great cost . " 

 he smile at she , but she sit bite her inner lip and press her hand together . she desire to be worthy of what she reverence in Felix , but the inevitable renunciation be too difficult . she see herself wander through the future weak and forsake . the charming sauciness be all go from her face , but the memory of it make this childlike dependent sorrow all the more touching . 

 " tell I what you would " Felix burst out , lean near to she ; but the next instant he start up , go to the table , take his cap in his hand and come in front of she . 

 " good - bye , " he say , very gently , not dare to put out his hand . but Esther put up hers instead of speak . he just press it and then go away . 

 she hear the door close behind he , and feel free to be miserable . she cry bitterly . if she might have marry Felix Holt , she could have be a good woman . she feel no trust that she could ever be good without he . 

 Felix reproach himself . he would have do well not to speak in that way . but the prompting to which he have chiefly listen have be the desire to prove to Esther that he set a high value on her feeling . he could not help see that he be very important to she ; and he be too simple and sincere a man to ape a sort of humility which would not have make he any the well if he have possess it . such pretence turn our life into sorry drama . and Felix wish Esther to know that her love be dear to he as the beloved dead be dear . he feel that they must 276   not marry that they would ruin each other 's life . but he have long for she to know fully that his will to be always apart from she be renunciation , not an easy preference . in this he be thoroughly generous ; and yet , now some subtle , mysterious conjuncture of impression and circumstance have make he speak , he question the wisdom of what he have do . express confession give definiteness to memory that might more easily melt away without ; and Felix feel for Esther 's pain as the strong soldier , who can march on hungering without fear that he shall faint , feel for the young brother the maiden - cheek conscript whose load be too heavy for he . 

 CHAPTER XXXIII . 

 Mischief , thou art afoot . Julius Casar . 

 Felix could not go home again immediately after quit Esther . he get out of the town , skirt it a little while , look across the December stillness of the field , and then re - enter it by the main road into the market - place , think that , after all , it would be well for he to look at the busy doing of man than to listen in solitude to the voice within he ; and he wish to know how thing be go on . 

 it be now nearly half - past one , and Felix perceive that the street be fill with more than the previous crowd . by the time he get in front of the booth , he be himself so surround by man who be be thrust hither and thither that retreat would have be impossible ; and he go where he be oblige to go , although his height and strength be above the average even in a crowd where there be so many heavy - armed workman use to the pickaxe . almost all shabby - coat Trebians must have be there , but the entry and back street of the town do not supply the mass of the crowd ; and besides the rural incomer , both of the more decent and the rougher sort , Felix , as he be push along , think he discern here and there man of that keener aspect which be only common in manufacturing town . 

 but at present there be no evidence of any distinctly mischievous design . there be only evidence that the majority of the crowd be excited with drink , and that their action could hardly be calculate on more than those of the oxen and pig congregate amidst hooting and pushing . the confused deafening shout , the incidental fighting , the knock over , pull and scuffling , seem to increase every moment . 277   such of the constable as be mix with the crowd be quite helpless ; and if an official staff be see above the head , it move about fitfully , show as little sign of a guide hand as the summit of a buoy on the wave . doubtless many hurt and bruise have be receive , but no one could know the amount of injury that be widely scatter .

 but the rector 's voice be ring and penetrate , and when he appear on the narrow balcony and read the formula , command all man to go to their home or about their lawful business , there be a strong transient effect . every one within hear listen , and for a few moment after the final word , " God save the king ! " the comparative silence continue . then the people begin to move , the buzz rise again , and grow , and grow , till it turn to shout and roar as before . the movement be that of a flood hem in ; it carry nobody away . whether the crowd would obey the order to disperse themselves within an hour , be a doubt that approach near to a negative certainty . 

 presently Mr. Crow , who call himself a tactician , take a well - intentione step , which go far to fulfill his own prophecy . he have arrive with the magistrate by a back way at the Seven Stars , and here again the Riot Act be read from a window , with much the same result as before . the rector have return by the same way to the Marquis , as the headquarters most suited for administration , but Mr. Crow remain at the other extremity of King Street , where some awe - strike presence be certainly need . see that the time be pass , and all effect from the voice of law have disappear , he show himself at an upper window , and address the crowd , tell they that the soldier have be send for , and that if they do not disperse they would have cavalry upon they instead of constable .

 mingle with the more headlong and half - drunken crowd there be some sharp - visage man who love the irrationality of riot for something else than its own sake , and who at present be not so much the rich as they desire to be , for the pain they have take in come to the Treby election , induce by certain prognostic gather at Duffield on the nomination - day that there might be the condition favorable to that confusion which be always a harvest - time . it be know to some of these sharp man that Park Street lead out toward the grand house of Treby Manor , which be as good nay , well , for their purpose than the bank . while Felix be entertain his ardent purpose , these other son of 284   Adam be entertain another ardent purpose of their peculiar sort , and the moment have come when they be to have their triumph .

 Felix wound in the Riot .
